description:
About the job

Customer Support Specialist (Hospitality & Partnerships)




Full-Time | Remote | Pacific Time




Overview

Our client, a fast-growing U.S.-based hospitality company, is looking for a Customer Support Specialist (Hospitality & Partnerships) to help deliver exceptional guest experiences while supporting inbound inquiries, partnership development, and private tour bookings across two growing travel brands.




This is not a traditional customer support role. You'll build relationships, guide clients through the booking process, manage partner accounts, and help drive business growth through consultative communication and outstanding service.




Key Responsibilities

Respond promptly to inbound inquiries from private and corporate clients.
Guide prospects through the booking process and prepare tailored proposals.
Build and maintain relationships with hotels, event planners, concierges, and referral partners.
Follow up with warm leads and convert inquiries into confirmed bookings.
Maintain accurate CRM records, pipeline updates, and client documentation.
Track sales activity and conversion metrics.
Collaborate with Marketing and Operations to ensure an outstanding customer experience.




Success in this Role

Build strong client relationships, grow strategic partnerships, and consistently convert inquiries into successful bookings.




Requirements

2+ years of experience in Customer Support, Client Relations, Hospitality, Tourism, Sales, or Account Coordination.
Excellent spoken and written English.
Experience using CRM platforms.
Strong Google Sheets and Google Docs skills.
Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced remote environment.
Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills.




What We're Looking For

Customer-first mindset.
Responsive and proactive.
Relationship-driven.
Organized and dependable.
Collaborative team player.




Nice to Have

Hospitality, tourism, events, or food & beverage experience.
Experience working with hotels, concierges, event planners, or corporate clients.
Canva experience.
Airtable, HubSpot, or Salesforce.
Familiarity with AI tools such as Claude.




Why Join

Join two fast-growing hospitality brands where you'll build meaningful relationships, create memorable experiences, and play a key role in the company's growth.

description:
About the job

Digital Marketing Content Specialist - Remote | Full-Time

About The Company

We are a private equity firm that owns and operates two regional lawn and pest control companies — one in New Orleans, Louisiana, and one in Jacksonville, Florida. Both businesses are in active growth mode with a concentrated push into digital marketing to drive customer acquisition and brand visibility across their local markets.

About The Role

This role exists because leadership has a clear marketing vision and no bandwidth to execute it. The principals are running these businesses day-to-day and can set the strategy — they need someone who can take a brief, work independently, and deliver finished product without being managed through every step.

You won't be handed a detailed playbook for every task. You'll be handed a direction and expected to move. This is an execution role, and the right person will treat it like a craft.

This person will support digital marketing efforts across both portfolio companies.

Key Responsibilities

Ad Content Production


Build Facebook and Instagram ad variations based on strategy briefs from leadership — expect to produce 5-10 creative variations per campaign
Write ad copy across multiple angles (offer-driven, testimonial, awareness, seasonal) and test messaging approaches
Resize and adapt existing creative assets for different placements and formats (feed, story, reel, carousel)
Monitor ad performance at a surface level and flag low performers or creative fatigue to leadership
Stay current on Meta ad best practices — formats, creative trends, and what's working in local service marketing


Video Editing & Production Support


Edit raw video footage into social-ready clips for Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube Shorts
Add captions, transitions, music, and branded elements to raw footage
Maintain brand consistency across all video output — tone, fonts, color treatment
Turn around edited video within 24 hours of receiving source material in most cases
Repurpose long-form video into short clips and platform-specific cuts


Graphic Design & Creative Assets


Design digital and print-ready assets: flyers, door hangers, mailers, promotional graphics, and seasonal campaign materials
Create branded templates for recurring content types to improve production speed over time
Maintain a library of approved brand assets, fonts, colors, and logo files for both companies
Produce social graphics, cover images, and profile assets as needed


Content Creation & Copywriting


Draft social media captions, post copy, and short-form content for both company accounts
Assist with blog posts, email newsletters, or promotional write-ups on an as-needed basis
Adapt tone and messaging between the two portfolio brands as needed
Use AI tools to accelerate content drafts — then refine, edit, and elevate the output to publish-ready quality


Workflow, Communication & Execution


Manage multiple concurrent projects across two companies and communicate priorities clearly
Receive direction asynchronously and execute with minimal follow-up needed
Proactively flag blockers, capacity constraints, or timeline risks — before they become problems
Maintain an organized file structure and content calendar so nothing falls through the cracks
Coordinate with the external SEO firm on an as-needed basis (website and landing pages are handled separately)


Required Qualifications


2+ years of hands-on experience in digital marketing, content creation, or a closely related role
Demonstrated ability to produce Meta/Facebook ads — copy, creative, and format adaptation
Proficiency with AI content tools (ChatGPT, Midjourney, or similar) as a production accelerator, not a replacement for judgment
Video editing competency in CapCut, DaVinci Resolve, Adobe Premiere, or equivalent
Graphic design skills sufficient for social and print assets (Canva, Adobe Express, Photoshop, or similar)
Strong written English — this person communicates directly with company principals and produces outward-facing content
Portfolio or work samples required — candidates without demonstrable creative output will not advance


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting multiple brands or clients simultaneously (agency background is a strong signal)
Familiarity with home services, trades, lawn care, pest control, or local service business marketing
Working knowledge of Meta Ads Manager — campaign structure, audience basics, creative specs
Experience managing or contributing to a content calendar across multiple accounts
Comfort working U.S. business hours or maintaining overlap with U.S. Eastern/Central time zones

title: PPC Specialist (Lead Generation, Home Services)
description:
About the job

About company

We are a fast-growing performance agency focused on lead generation for home-services brands. We value ethics, clarity, and measurable outcomes. The team invests in learning and avoids siloed hand-offs so people can own results end to end.




The role

Run PPC for a portfolio of home-services clients and be accountable for outcomes from onboarding through scale. The job blends in-platform execution with tagging, analytics, and client communication. You will work alongside teammates in data and client services rather than sitting in a narrow channel lane.




What you’ll do

• Plan, build, launch, and optimize Google Ads and Microsoft Ads for lead generation

• Join client calls, translate data into decisions, set expectations, and build trust

• Own measurement quality with Google Tag Manager, including data layer checks and debugging

• Use GA4, CRM-based attribution views, Looker Studio, and CallRail to assess performance and recommend changes

• Maintain hygiene, budgets, and a steady test plan; use scripts where helpful

• Collaborate with the existing account team during onboarding and share context with peers




What you’ll bring

• 3-4 or more years in PPC for lead generation, with solid home-services experience

• Strong English for a client-facing role; accents are welcome

• Hands-on strength in Google Ads or Microsoft Ads plus GTM, GA4, Sheets, Looker Studio, and basic CallRail usage

• Comfort reading closed-loop data and making decisions from CRM-sourced revenue

• Clear thinking, attention to detail, and the ability to move between strategy and execution

• Ability to align most working hours to the Central time U.S. day; typical weeks average 40 to 50 hours




Nice to have

• Experience with Google Ads scripts or simple automation

• Zapier or similar workflow tools

• Familiarity with Google Ads API concepts and partnering with data engineers

• Landing page tools such as WordPress, Unbounce, or Leadpages




Interview process

• Initial interview

• Paid audit task

• Follow-up interview to review your work

• Decision




Start date

As soon as possible, with respect for notice periods.




Schedule: Align most of your day to the U.S. Central work window; plan to overlap broadly between 7:00 a.m. and 7:00 p.m. CT

title: Especialista en Marketing de Influencers
skills: Own TikTok Shop Seller Center day to day. Live in the dashboards, know your numbers cold, and make sure performance is healthy across GMV, conversion, and orders.

Make the affiliate program profitable. Track and grow GMV, revenue per creator, sample-to-sales ROI, and commission costs. Protect margin. Every dollar of product and commission has to earn its return.
Build and manage a pipeline of hundreds to thousands of active TikTok Shop affiliates, and keep them actually posting and selling, not just signing up.
Recruit high-converting creators and live sellers who know how to talk about product and drive sales, not just collect free samples.
Go live and sell yourself. You will host TikTok Lives, get on camera, and sell the glasses directly. Then you bring affiliates on, show them how it is done, and coach them to do the same.
Coordinate TikTok Live schedules with strong sellers, secure Spark Ads permissions, and optimize listings and content angles for maximum GMV.
Watch performance weekly. Scale top converters aggressively, and cut low performers fast.
Keep the data honest. No padded numbers, no inflated reporting. Decisions are only as good as the data behind them.
Source Instagram influencers who can actually sell, creators who understand product and know how to drive their audience to buy, not just post a pretty photo.
Run gifting-only and affiliate-only collaborations, and move creators from one gift to repeat posting to consistent affiliate sales.

Rebuild our existing relationships. We have lists of past and dormant influencers. A big part of this role is reigniting those relationships and turning old contacts back into active, converting partners.
Build genuine, consistent relationships so TopFoxx stays top of mind, through thoughtful, regular touchpoints across Instagram and DMs.
Recruit and activate creators on LTK and other affiliate networks (Amazon, affiliate links) who are still actively driving commission-based sales.
Run weekly onboarding calls. You will be on calls regularly, onboarding new influencers, walking them through the program, and getting them excited and set up to post.
Get on calls and close collabs. Be confident and persuasive, build rapport quickly, and convince creators to say yes to working with us, often with gifting only.
Understand creator psychology and motivate influencers to post consistently through relationship, community value, and a clear win for them.
Build the strategy. Bring a clear point of view on where influencer and affiliate marketing is going and how TopFoxx wins, then put it into action.
Plan and execute campaigns tied to product launches, holidays, seasonal moments, and major sales events (BFCM, summer, new drops).
Manage content calendars, gifting coordination, sample sends, and follow-ups so nothing slips.
Build weekly performance reports that connect activity to revenue, and use them to decide what to scale and what to kill.
Create simple SOPs, trackers, and playbooks as you go, so the program is repeatable and scalable as the team grows.
Collaborate with the creative, marketing, and product teams to keep messaging aligned, and step in on urgent launch-related projects when needed.
Stay ahead of trends, new creators, and new affiliate opportunities before competitors do.

Help with wider marketing as needed. With a team this small, marketing is just three people and everyone pitches in. From time to time things will come up across other marketing areas and departments that need a hand. We need someone proactive and solution-oriented who brings energy, jumps in, and helps build this with us rather than sitting idle.
Proactive: You see the gap and fill it. You bring the plan, you do not wait to be told.
A real executor: You move fast, ship daily, and adapt as things change hour to hour.
Analytical and profit-obsessed: You can read a Seller Center dashboard, understand ROI and margin, and turn data into decisions.
Persuasive and outgoing: You love talking to creators, you are comfortable on camera and on calls, and you can sell the partnership and the product.
Relationship-driven: You are a natural connector who builds loyalty and keeps partners coming back.
Organized under pressure: You can juggle hundreds of moving pieces without dropping the ball.
2+ years in influencer or affiliate marketing, preferably in fashion, beauty, lifestyle, or e-commerce / DTC.
Expert, hands-on knowledge of TikTok Shop Seller Center mechanics and affiliate programs.
Proven track record of generating real sales through gifting-only or zero-budget strategies.
Comfort with LTK, Amazon, and other affiliate platforms.
Comfortable on camera and hosting TikTok Lives to sell product directly.
Perfect written and spoken English, and a strong understanding of U.S. culture and digital trends. This is genuinely important.
KPI-driven, highly organized, and able to build simple systems and SOPs as you scale.
Salary of $1,500 to $2,000 per month based on experience, plus performance bonuses tied to affiliate revenue and profitability.
A fully remote role on a fast-moving global team.
Real ownership of the influencer and affiliate function, with a path into leadership as it scales.
PTO including vacation, holidays, sick, personal, and mental health days.
Free TopFoxx products and partner brand perks.
description:
About the job

Affiliate & Influencer Marketing Specialist

TopFoxx | Remote | Full-Time

 

Location: Remote (must work U.S. East Coast hours).

Hours: Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM EST. Hours are flexible. We care about performance, not clocks.

Salary: $1,500 to $2,000 per month based on experience, plus performance bonuses tied to affiliate revenue and profitability.

Language: Perfect written and spoken English is required (this is non-negotiable), along with a deep understanding of U.S. culture, slang, and social media behavior.




About TopFoxx

TopFoxx is a fast-growing, women-owned fashion startup reinventing the sunglasses industry with solution-oriented designs and bold branding. Since 2016, we have grown from a bootstrapped e-commerce brand into a global business serving hundreds of thousands of customers, backed by an engaged community of influencers and creators.

We are not here to follow trends. We create them. Our sunglasses are tangle-free, floatable, foldable, shatter-resistant, and sustainably made, and we are just getting started.

We are a small, lean, founder-led team of fewer than ten people, with only about three of us on marketing. That means there is nowhere to hide and no one to pass the work to. Everyone wears multiple hats, owns real outcomes, and what you build genuinely moves the company. We do not do slow. We move fast, we pivot often, and we only hire A-players who treat the business like their own.




The Role

You will be the main one. This is the person who owns influencer and affiliate marketing end to end at TopFoxx. You are not waiting for a manual or a list of tasks. You are expected to bring the strategy, bring the ideas, and then execute them yourself, fast.

This space changes daily. New creators, new trends, new platform rules. You have to move quickly, test constantly, double down on what converts, and cut what does not. We are not looking for someone who only posts and reports. We are looking for a real executor who is proactive, creative, and obsessed with one thing above all: profitable, converting partnerships.

This is a big opportunity, and we want to be honest with you: it is also real work. We already have a large base of affiliates and creators, but your job is to grow it, activate it, and make it far more profitable. This is not a coast-along role. You will be building, pushing, and problem-solving every single day. For the right person, that is exactly the appeal.

This is a high-ownership role with a direct path into leading the influencer marketing function as it scales.




Why This Is Worth It

Here is the honest pitch. This is not a maintenance job and it is not busywork. Influencer and affiliate marketing is one of the biggest growth levers we have, and we believe this channel can become a multi-million dollar engine for TopFoxx. We are looking for the person who can build that.

We reward outcomes, not hours. If you can show us you can grow this and drive real, profitable revenue, you grow with it. That means a clear path into leadership and compensation that reflects the results you create. The ceiling here is high for someone who actually delivers, and you will be able to point to a business you helped build, not just tasks you completed.

If you want to be a small piece of a big machine, this is not it. If you want real ownership, real upside, and to look back in a year knowing you built something that matters, keep reading.




1. TikTok Shop & Affiliate Program (Your Primary Focus)

This is the heart of the role and where most of your time and energy goes. The big one is affiliate. Your job is to make sure the affiliates are converting, the program is profitable, and the numbers in TikTok Shop Seller Center are strong and trending up.

Own TikTok Shop Seller Center day to day. Live in the dashboards, know your numbers cold, and make sure performance is healthy across GMV, conversion, and orders.
Make the affiliate program profitable. Track and grow GMV, revenue per creator, sample-to-sales ROI, and commission costs. Protect margin. Every dollar of product and commission has to earn its return.
Build and manage a pipeline of hundreds to thousands of active TikTok Shop affiliates, and keep them actually posting and selling, not just signing up.
Recruit high-converting creators and live sellers who know how to talk about product and drive sales, not just collect free samples.
Go live and sell yourself. You will host TikTok Lives, get on camera, and sell the glasses directly. Then you bring affiliates on, show them how it is done, and coach them to do the same.
Coordinate TikTok Live schedules with strong sellers, secure Spark Ads permissions, and optimize listings and content angles for maximum GMV.
Watch performance weekly. Scale top converters aggressively, and cut low performers fast.
Keep the data honest. No padded numbers, no inflated reporting. Decisions are only as good as the data behind them.




2. Instagram Influencers

Instagram is the second pillar. Here we work on gifting and affiliate only, so your skill is finding the right people and getting them to convert without a paid budget.

Source Instagram influencers who can actually sell, creators who understand product and know how to drive their audience to buy, not just post a pretty photo.
Run gifting-only and affiliate-only collaborations, and move creators from one gift to repeat posting to consistent affiliate sales.
Rebuild our existing relationships. We have lists of past and dormant influencers. A big part of this role is reigniting those relationships and turning old contacts back into active, converting partners.
Build genuine, consistent relationships so TopFoxx stays top of mind, through thoughtful, regular touchpoints across Instagram and DMs.




3. LTK, Affiliate Platforms & Onboarding Calls

The third pillar is affiliate-driven platforms like LTK, and just as important, getting on calls with creators. This role is not behind a screen all day. You have to be outgoing and genuinely good at talking to people.

Recruit and activate creators on LTK and other affiliate networks (Amazon, affiliate links) who are still actively driving commission-based sales.
Run weekly onboarding calls. You will be on calls regularly, onboarding new influencers, walking them through the program, and getting them excited and set up to post.
Get on calls and close collabs. Be confident and persuasive, build rapport quickly, and convince creators to say yes to working with us, often with gifting only.
Understand creator psychology and motivate influencers to post consistently through relationship, community value, and a clear win for them.




4. Strategy, Reporting & Full Influencer Marketing Scope

Because you own this function, you also handle everything a strong e-commerce influencer marketing lead would handle. Study what bigger e-commerce brands do, then adapt the best of it to a lean, fast-moving team.

Build the strategy. Bring a clear point of view on where influencer and affiliate marketing is going and how TopFoxx wins, then put it into action.
Plan and execute campaigns tied to product launches, holidays, seasonal moments, and major sales events (BFCM, summer, new drops).
Manage content calendars, gifting coordination, sample sends, and follow-ups so nothing slips.
Build weekly performance reports that connect activity to revenue, and use them to decide what to scale and what to kill.
Create simple SOPs, trackers, and playbooks as you go, so the program is repeatable and scalable as the team grows.
Collaborate with the creative, marketing, and product teams to keep messaging aligned, and step in on urgent launch-related projects when needed.
Stay ahead of trends, new creators, and new affiliate opportunities before competitors do.
Help with wider marketing as needed. With a team this small, marketing is just three people and everyone pitches in. From time to time things will come up across other marketing areas and departments that need a hand. We need someone proactive and solution-oriented who brings energy, jumps in, and helps build this with us rather than sitting idle.




Who You Are

Proactive: You see the gap and fill it. You bring the plan, you do not wait to be told.
A real executor: You move fast, ship daily, and adapt as things change hour to hour.
Analytical and profit-obsessed: You can read a Seller Center dashboard, understand ROI and margin, and turn data into decisions.
Persuasive and outgoing: You love talking to creators, you are comfortable on camera and on calls, and you can sell the partnership and the product.
Relationship-driven: You are a natural connector who builds loyalty and keeps partners coming back.
Organized under pressure: You can juggle hundreds of moving pieces without dropping the ball.




Required Experience & Skills

2+ years in influencer or affiliate marketing, preferably in fashion, beauty, lifestyle, or e-commerce / DTC.
Expert, hands-on knowledge of TikTok Shop Seller Center mechanics and affiliate programs.
Proven track record of generating real sales through gifting-only or zero-budget strategies.
Comfort with LTK, Amazon, and other affiliate platforms.
Comfortable on camera and hosting TikTok Lives to sell product directly.
Perfect written and spoken English, and a strong understanding of U.S. culture and digital trends. This is genuinely important.
KPI-driven, highly organized, and able to build simple systems and SOPs as you scale.




What You Get

Salary of $1,500 to $2,000 per month based on experience, plus performance bonuses tied to affiliate revenue and profitability.
A fully remote role on a fast-moving global team.
Real ownership of the influencer and affiliate function, with a path into leadership as it scales.
PTO including vacation, holidays, sick, personal, and mental health days.
Free TopFoxx products and partner brand perks.

description:
About the job

This position is 100% remote for Mexico, Columbia, Venezuela and Guatemala, Argentina, Honduras, DR, Brazil applicants. Not a US based post. The hours are 10am-7pm MST and includes one Saturday a month from 8-5 MST.

About Us

The Savings Group (TSG), the parent company of RateGenius, AUTOPAY and Tresl, is the most diversified consumer marketplace for automotive finance, refinance, and protection plans. Through a network of more than 180 lenders across all 50 states, TSG provides consumers more choices for their origination and refinanced auto loans, while also delivering loan volume growth to its lender network and partners through a proprietary, web-based platform.

Why Work at The Savings Group?

Working at TSG is more than your standard J-O-B. That's because we work with some of the most amazing goal-oriented and driven people in fintech and still embody the spirit of a startup! Not only have we worked together to perfect the way we serve our customers, but we've built a pretty awesome culture in the process. Our people come from all kinds of backgrounds to create something exciting and new: A BETTER WAY TO DO CAR LOANS. We offer steady growth, a strong and supportive leadership team, and in-depth training and tools to ensure your success.

Summary

The Customer Success Representative Level I ensures that customers receive wonderful customer service in all aspects when taking care of the customers. This includes any and all customers that are calling in to inquire regarding potential vehicle purchases, refinances or questions regarding open applications and so forth. Customer Success maintains all overflow through TSG phone systems and is the start of the TSG journey for all our customers throughout all of its brands. Customer Success Representatives Level I are required to answer all phone calls received, respond to customer inquiries, complete customer applications either over the phone or via text/email, and send emails to the correct corresponding departments as needed to resolve customer complaints. This includes internal transfers as well as maintaining Legal and Compliance concerns when completing applications as well. 

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES include achieving positive results in the following key areas:


Understand and demonstrate principles of The Savings Group’s Mission, Vision, and Values. 
Document all account, contact, and activity information into various systems. 
Answer calls from internal and external sources, while providing a world-class customer service experience. 
Complete applications by utilizing various systems to obtain the necessary information and consent
Manage inbound and outbound communication by email, phone and/ or text to customers to complete applications
Ensure to send communications to the correct departments when the customer calls in for something that goes beyond the scope of what you are able to assist with (i.e, titles issues, escalations, payoff issues, loan specialist request etc..)
Consistently respond to auto finance inquiries in a timely, efficient, and professional manner. 
Meet all metrics and standards set forth by management
Additional duties as required. 


QUALIFICATIONS

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities


Education: High School diploma is required. One (1) year of customer service-related experience in a call center preferred
Experience: Experience in inside phone sales, telemarketing, quality assurance or customer service experience with auto finance companies or, car dealerships
Language/Communication/Interpersonal: Strong listening, verbal, and written communication skills. Ability to read and comprehend simple instructions, short correspondence, and memos. Ability to write simple correspondence. Ability to effectively present information to customers, clients, and all levels of management
Reasoning Ability: Ability to apply common sense understanding to carry out detailed but uninvolved written or oral instructions. Ability to deal with problems involving concrete variables in standardized situations
Analytical: Detail-oriented with ability to organize, prioritize and work independently. Strong critical thinking skills
Technical: Ability to toggle between applications on a dual monitor setup. Proficiency with Microsoft Office, with intermediate Excel ability, required. Keyboarding/computer skills, heavy computer and phone usage
Other: High level of personal motivation, professionalism and integrity required. Ability to exercise discretion and judgment. Must be dependable


Computer IT/Requirements/compensation:

Must have a desktop or laptop PC capable of installing and running the Windows App. Device must support hardwired (Ethernet) connection for stable network access.


Reliable internet connection with minimum speeds of:
Download: 50 Mbps or higher. Upload: 10 Mbps or higher
Must provide their own headset, mouse, and keyboard
IT can only provide very limited support for personal devices; it is the responsibility of the new hire to run updates and maintain their personal device
$1500 US Dollars per month paid on the last day of each month. 


Job description statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this job title. They are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and skills required.

The Savings Group (including all its subsidiaries: AUTOPAY, RateGenius Loan Services, Inc., and Innovative Funding Services dba Tresl) is an equal opportunity employer. With regard to hiring and promotions, qualified persons will not be denied employment opportunity based on race, color, national origin, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, age 40 and over, disability, military status, or genetic information. Any questions or concerns about our EEO policy should be directed to Human Resources

description:
About the job

Do work that matters. 

At AlertMedia, everything we do supports our mission: To save lives and minimize loss by identifying active threats globally and facilitating the timely communications when an emergency threatens personal safety and business continuity. 

Our core values drive us in our important mission of keeping people safe & informed: 


We’re humans not robots 
Customers always come first 
We work better together 
Simplicity is our strength 
Our reputation is priceless 
Hard work pays off 


The Customer Success Specialist will take ownership of managing customer relationships, which we value above all else. This role will focus on emerging customers and partner closely with our Customer Support Team to foster success. In this key role, working closely with Customer Success, Customer Support, Marketing and Sales teams, they are responsible for driving engagement to support customer health, retention, and growth. The right candidate will be determined to achieve effective customer enablement, as well as the highest possible satisfaction rates across the accounts they manage. They will make sure customer needs are always met and problems are solved quickly. 

As one of the fastest growing software companies in the nation, we’re focused on finding the best talent and building the best team to continue accelerating our rapid growth to keep up with our demand! 

Who you are: 

You'd be excited to buy into our "customer love" mentality and are motivated by going above and beyond for the customers you serve. You are a team player, an individual contributor and have a roll-up-your-sleeves mentality when it comes to your customers and there is no task too big or too small to best support them. You enjoy building relationships, community engagement, and driving adoption of all features of the AlertMedia platform. 

What you get to do every day: 


Support our customers in the SMB segment as a product expert to drive adoption and enable them to accomplish their goals 
Manage customer touch points with automated processes and innovative approaches 
Host customer webinars and facilitate customer collaboration 
Align with the Customer Success organization to support initiatives to grow and expand existing accounts through adoption, cross-sell, and upsell opportunities 
Evaluate health and assess risk for your book of business 
Partner with our Customer Support team to manage escalations requiring more strategic oversight 
Respond to all customer inquiries in a timely manner, within 1 business day 
Effectively track all customer communication and data in Gainsight and Salesforce 
Acquire industry knowledge related to general trends, use cases, and emerging technologies 
Ensure positive and productive customer experience to maintain software renewals 
Contribute to AlertMedia’s culture, values, and vision for the future 
This is an individual contributor role and not a manager role 


What you bring to the role: 


2+ years’ experience in customer success management or account management 
Experience in a Saas or Tech environment strongly preferred 
Salesforce or Hubspot experience preferred 
Strong organization and time management skills with ability to prioritize 
Comfort level managing a large volume of accounts 
Outstanding verbal and written communication skills 
Willingness to test and implement innovative ideas 
Experience with customer implementation and software adoption preferred 
Strong listening skills with  the ability to mirror and work with a variety of customer profiles 
Ability to work with all members of our team (customer support, business development, software development, and product management) 
Must be independent, self-motivated, success-driven, and adaptable 
The ability and desire to work in a fast-paced, challenging environment 
A vision for efficiency 
The desire to learn and be coached 
Bilingual English/Spanish a must 
This is an in office, hybrid position (2 days a week) in our downtown Mexico City (Reforma) offices 


Location: This is a hybrid position based in Mexico City.

Sponsorship: AlertMedia does not sponsor

Why you’ll love working at AlertMedia:


Competitive Base Salary
Annual Company Bonus
100% Employer-Paid Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
Parental Leave Program 
Christmas Bonus (Aguinaldo)
Generous Vacation and Holiday Policy
Monthly Work-from-Home Stipend
All Necessary Equipment Provided: Laptop, monitor and accessories
Recognition & Rewards: We celebrate wins big and small
Language Immersion Program with employees spread out across the globe (English, Spanish, etc...depending on your interest)
Continuous Learning & Growth: From mentorship and tech talks to personalized 1:1 career development sessions, we’re deeply committed to investing in your professional journey
Award Winning Culture: We’re passionate about the meaningful work we do—and even more grateful for the incredible people we get to do it with!


About AlertMedia:

AlertMedia helps organizations protect their people and businesses through all phases of an emergency. Our award-winning threat intelligence, emergency communication, and travel risk management solutions help companies of all sizes identify, respond to, and recover from critical events faster and more confidently. AlertMedia supports essential communication for thousands of leading businesses—including JetBlue, Coca-Cola Bottling, and Walmart—in more than 150 countries.

We are an equal opportunity employer focused on creating a collaborative and exciting place for all to work. Ensuring a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace for all people is key to our success and core to our values. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

This is an amazing opportunity to be part of our wave of momentum and take our company, and your career, to the next growth stage. We'd love to get to know you better and share how we serve our amazing customers. For more information, please visit www.alertmedia.com.

Come join us in our mission to save lives and minimize loss through effective communication.

By applying for the role, you agree that Alert Media will use your Personal Information in connection with the recruitment process and in accordance with our Privacy Policy – https://www.alertmedia.com/legal/privacy-policy/

description:
About the job

Overview

CAREERS TO SMILE ABOUT

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

Responsibilities

The Opportunity

This position contributes to the success of the Pepsi Beverages Company by providing outstanding customer service to customers and employees in the California, Mountain and Midwest Market Units. The agent will answer inbound calls and ensure issues are resolved through the screening and tracking of each request and dispatching to the appropriate Pepsi service team; while also performing follow up on each call to ensure complete resolution.

Your Impact

As Commercial Assoc Analyst your functions would consist of …


Answer and provide outstanding customer service of 50+ calls a day (Based on avg call volume of 500 calls per day)
Process inbound calls to determine appropriate area, (i.e., Delivery, Merchandising, MEM, etc.)
Partner with other departments to achieve root cause resolution of customer issues and provide feedback through reporting.
Achieve individual and team objectives to include: Average Call Handle Time, Calls per Hour, Cases Closed, Quality Service Level, Achieve < 2% abandonment rate goal for the Pepsi Support Center
Maintain complete and accurate documentation within the Customer Service Center Call Log
Maintain an On Target or better performance rating
Contribute toward team goals and objectives
Contribute toward improving National Customer Survey Score
Communicate with field partners to ensure up-to-date staffing/location contact changes


Qualifications

Who Are We Looking For?


English language proficiency
Strong PC data entry skills. Typing skills at a minimum of 50+ WPM
Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite - Word, Excel, Outlook and Access. Knowledge of Database Maintenance/creation is preferred
Previous relevant work experience in a customer service-phone based environment
Highly developed listening, analytical, and problem-solving skills
Able to work within a team environment


If this is an opportunity that interests you, we encourage you to apply even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements.

What can you expect from us: 


Opportunities to learn and develop every day through a wide range of programs. 
Internal digital platforms that promote self-learning. 
Development programs according to Leadership skills. 
Specialized training according to the role. 
Learning experiences with internal and external providers. 
We love to celebrate success, which is why we have recognition programs for seniority, behavior, leadership, moments of life, among others. 
Financial wellness programs that will help you reach your goals in all stages of life. 
A flexibility program that will allow you to balance your personal and work life, adapting your working day to your lifestyle.
And because your family is also important to us, they can also enjoy benefits such as our Wellness Line, thousands of Agreements and Discounts, Scholarship programs for your children, Aid Plans for different moments of life, among others. 


We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We respect and value diversity as a work force and innovation for the organization.

title: Analista Comercial de Cuentas - Marketplace
skills: Vibran energía emprendedora: se mueven por la curiosidad, nunca se rinden y se enfocan en superar sus propios límites.
Dan el máximo porque les gusta trabajar con compromiso y dedicación.
Viven los cambios como oportunidades y aprenden de sus errores.
La excelencia y la ejecución son claves en su forma de hacer las cosas.
Promueven el buen clima, aportan alegría y diversión.
Saben cómo construir con otras personas y disfrutan trabajando en equipo.

Definir la visión y estrategia de negocio para las marcas del segmento de Supermercado. Manejar el P&L y desarrollar planes para lograr los objetivos de rentabilidad y crecimiento esperados así como ser responsable del desempeño y planeación de inventarios, asegurando una correcta disponibilidad y rotación de inventarios.

Establecer sinergias y relaciones estrechas con las marcas que permitan un crecimiento sostenible y sustentable. Es el encargado de liderar las negociaciones para asegurar los objetivos de rentabilidad y crecimiento.

Establecer planes y asegurar una selección óptima, competitiva y completa que nos permita asegurar una óptima experiencia de compra para el cliente, contando con una alta calidad en publicaciones (imágenes y contenido) y selección.

Generación de análisis y documentos que permitan un correcto seguimiento a KPIs de manera oportuna y eficiente y colaborar con equipos internos para generar herramientas y procesos automáticos que permitan flujos de trabajo más eficientes.
Planeación de ciclos de vida de producto que permitan una correcta ejecución de cambios de lineal dentro de las marcas a cargo.
Ser profesional de las carreras de Cs Economicas o Ingenieria.
Contar con experiencia de 2 años en roles de Compras, Ventas, Mkt o similares, llevando la gestión de cuentas, desarrollando negociaciones y manejando el P&L.
Es deseable contar con manejo de bases de datos como SQL, Looker, Data Studio o Power BI.
Ser parte de una compañía con espíritu emprendedor en la que nos encanta pensar en grande y a largo plazo.
Ser protagonista de tu desarrollo en un ambiente de oportunidades, aprendizaje, crecimiento, expansión y proyectos desafiantes.
Compartir y aprender en equipo junto a grandes profesionales y especialistas.
Un excelente clima de trabajo, con todo lo necesario para que vivas una gran experiencia. :)
description:
About the job

En Mercado Libre estamos democratizando el comercio, el dinero y los pagos en América Latina.




Nuestro Marketplace es el lugar donde las personas que compran y las que venden encuentran la más amplia oferta de productos y servicios disponible al precio más competitivo del mercado. Constantemente buscamos mejorar nuestros servicios comerciales, de producto y marketing garantizando una experiencia única para quienes ingresan a nuestro sitio. ¿Te gustaría revolucionar el e-commerce?




Tenemos un desafío para quienes:

Vibran energía emprendedora: se mueven por la curiosidad, nunca se rinden y se enfocan en superar sus propios límites.
Dan el máximo porque les gusta trabajar con compromiso y dedicación.
Viven los cambios como oportunidades y aprenden de sus errores.
La excelencia y la ejecución son claves en su forma de hacer las cosas.
Promueven el buen clima, aportan alegría y diversión.
Saben cómo construir con otras personas y disfrutan trabajando en equipo.




Imaginate emprendiendo proyectos desafiantes, dinámicos e innovadores y siendo responsable de:

Definir la visión y estrategia de negocio para las marcas del segmento de Supermercado. Manejar el P&L y desarrollar planes para lograr los objetivos de rentabilidad y crecimiento esperados así como ser responsable del desempeño y planeación de inventarios, asegurando una correcta disponibilidad y rotación de inventarios.
Establecer sinergias y relaciones estrechas con las marcas que permitan un crecimiento sostenible y sustentable. Es el encargado de liderar las negociaciones para asegurar los objetivos de rentabilidad y crecimiento.
Establecer planes y asegurar una selección óptima, competitiva y completa que nos permita asegurar una óptima experiencia de compra para el cliente, contando con una alta calidad en publicaciones (imágenes y contenido) y selección.
Generación de análisis y documentos que permitan un correcto seguimiento a KPIs de manera oportuna y eficiente y colaborar con equipos internos para generar herramientas y procesos automáticos que permitan flujos de trabajo más eficientes.
Planeación de ciclos de vida de producto que permitan una correcta ejecución de cambios de lineal dentro de las marcas a cargo.




Requisitos:

Ser profesional de las carreras de Cs Economicas o Ingenieria.
Contar con experiencia de 2 años en roles de Compras, Ventas, Mkt o similares, llevando la gestión de cuentas, desarrollando negociaciones y manejando el P&L.
Es deseable contar con manejo de bases de datos como SQL, Looker, Data Studio o Power BI.




Te proponemos:

Ser parte de una compañía con espíritu emprendedor en la que nos encanta pensar en grande y a largo plazo.
Ser protagonista de tu desarrollo en un ambiente de oportunidades, aprendizaje, crecimiento, expansión y proyectos desafiantes.
Compartir y aprender en equipo junto a grandes profesionales y especialistas.
Un excelente clima de trabajo, con todo lo necesario para que vivas una gran experiencia. :)

title: Customer Success Specialist
skills: Conduct daily outreach to Faculty (via calls, emails, and 1:1 demos) to set up JoVE integrations in courses and increase product usage.
Increase JoVE adoption by delivering product awareness sessions across existing customer universities and research institutes.
Grow engagement and usage by delivering presentations at (1) exhibits and conferences, (2) university seminars and workshops, and (3) onsite customer visits.
Strengthen subscription renewal outcomes by ensuring smooth implementation support, and consistent usage of JoVE resources.
Influence product decisions by communicating customer feedback to sales, product, and marketing teams.
A master's degree or PhD in Life Science disciplines.
Experience in science communications, academic outreach or other customer-facing roles is preferred.
Fluent communication skills English.
Knowledge of education, market trends, and challenges in assigned content areas i.e. Biology, Chemistry, Medicine, Physics, Engineering, or Psychology.
Strong interpersonal skills with a passion for remote relationship-building.
Experience in networking using cold calling is preferred.
You can expect compensation packages competitively placed within the local market.
You will make a direct impact in accelerating science research and in improving student learning in science education.
Opportunity to work with global teams and in an environment that promotes innovation and collaboration.
Our strong promotion from within culture draws a clear path to advance your career with us.
description:
About the job

JoVE is the world- leading producer and provider of video solutions with the mission to improve scientific research and education. Millions of scientists, educators and students use JoVE for their research, teaching and learning. Our institutional clients comprise over 1,000 universities, colleges, and biopharma companies, including such leaders as Harvard, MIT, Yale and Stanford. As a rapidly growing company, with offices in the USA, UK, Australia, and India servicing clients in over 60 countries, we are seeking talented and ambitious individuals to join our company.


The Role


As a Customer Success Specialist, you will help professors at leading universities in the US integrate JoVE videos into their courses to improve teaching outcomes and student success. You'll play a key role in ensuring users get the most value from their JoVE subscription.


Please share your resume in English.


Responsibilities:


Conduct daily outreach to Faculty (via calls, emails, and 1:1 demos) to set up JoVE integrations in courses and increase product usage.
Increase JoVE adoption by delivering product awareness sessions across existing customer universities and research institutes.
Grow engagement and usage by delivering presentations at (1) exhibits and conferences, (2) university seminars and workshops, and (3) onsite customer visits.
Strengthen subscription renewal outcomes by ensuring smooth implementation support, and consistent usage of JoVE resources.
Influence product decisions by communicating customer feedback to sales, product, and marketing teams.


Requirements


A master's degree or PhD in Life Science disciplines.
Experience in science communications, academic outreach or other customer-facing roles is preferred.
Fluent communication skills English.
Knowledge of education, market trends, and challenges in assigned content areas i.e. Biology, Chemistry, Medicine, Physics, Engineering, or Psychology.
Strong interpersonal skills with a passion for remote relationship-building.
Experience in networking using cold calling is preferred.


Benefits


You can expect compensation packages competitively placed within the local market.
You will make a direct impact in accelerating science research and in improving student learning in science education.
Opportunity to work with global teams and in an environment that promotes innovation and collaboration.
Our strong promotion from within culture draws a clear path to advance your career with us.

title: Especialista de Mercadotecnia
description:
About the job

¿Por qué trabajar en Fresenius Kabi?




En Fresenius Kabi México, tenemos una misión clara: salvar, cuidar y mejorar la vida de las personas. Cada acción que realizamos tiene un impacto real en la salud y el bienestar de millones, y trabajamos con pasión porque sabemos que lo que hacemos importa. Ofrecemos más que un empleo: brindamos carreras con propósito. Impulsamos el desarrollo de nuestros colaboradores en un entorno seguro, inclusivo y desafiante, con oportunidades de aprendizaje, bienestar integral, liderazgo cercano y reconocimiento.




Creemos en el poder del cuidado, no solo hacia nuestros pacientes, sino también entre nosotros. Fomentamos relaciones basadas en el respeto, la colaboración y la confianza, promoviendo el equilibrio entre la vida laboral y personal. Aquí, cada colaborador es clave para cumplir nuestra misión. En Fresenius Kabi, todo lo que hacemos transforma vidas. Por eso, lo damos todo. Siempre.




Te invitamos a formar parte de nuestro equipo de trabajo como:




Especialista de Mercadotecnia




Escolaridad: Licenciatura en nutrición/mercadotecnia /administración




Ubicación: Ciudad de México




Principales Funciones:




Elaboración del plan táctico de mercadotecnia de la línea de productos (anestesia/nutrición enteral, monitoreo o infusión)




Capacitación y Actualización técnica sobre productos, bases científicas, estrategia de ventas al equipo de ventas.




Capacitación continúa a la fuerza de ventas de acuerdo a estrategias de venta, lanzamiento de nuevos productos y actualización de la literatura médica.




Desarrollo y actualización de material promocional utilizado por la fuerza de ventas para capacitación interna y capacitación a interlocutores del área de la salud.




Requisitos:




Mínimo 2 años de experiencia en el industria farmacéutica en áreas de ventas y/o marketing con productos de uso en Hospitales

Análisis de información

Inglés conversacional




Competencias:




· Análisis y planificación estratégica.

· Comunicación

· Trabajo en equipo




Ofrecemos:




Prestaciones superiores a las de la ley (seguro de gastos médicos mayores, seguro de vida, seguro dental, fondo de ahorro, vales de despensa, etc.)

skills: Administrar la implementación de campañas en adserver, asegurando la correcta configuración, ejecución y cumplimiento de estándares operativos.

Realizar QA de creativos e implementaciones, monitorear el desempeño de campañas, identificar discrepancias entre plataformas y proponer optimizaciones.

Licenciatura concluida Marketing, comunicación o Ing de datos.

Manejo avanzado de adserver (CM, Sizmek, Weborama similares).

Conocimientos básicos de html, javascript, html5 (deseable)
Conocimientos básicos de plataformas de gestión de dashboards. (Data studio)
Inglés intermedio-avanzado

title: Order Fulfillment Specialist – Customer Success
skills: Receive, review, and validate customer purchase orders to ensure accuracy of pricing, contractual terms, delivery requirements, and compliance with internal policies.
Request and coordinate product allocation, confirm material availability, and enter customer orders accurately into internal systems while monitoring order status through fulfillment.

Proactively track and resolve order delays or discrepancies, collaborating cross-functionally with Sales, Supply Chain, Planning, Finance and Customer Success teams to align on delivery timelines and customer expectations.
Generate and distribute sales order confirmations to customers, ensuring clear communication of order details and fulfillment milestones.
Document, verify, and maintain records of any applicable third-party broker commissions associated with customer orders, ensuring accuracy and audit readiness.
Plan, select, and execute all outbound customer shipments, balancing, cost efficiency, service reliability, and customer-specific delivery requirements.
Monitor shipment progress from dispatch through final delivery, providing timely, proactive updates to customers and internal stakeholders.
Coordinate closely with freight brokers, carriers, and warehouse partners to schedule shipments, resolve transit issues, mitigate delays, develop contingency plans to minimize customer impact.
Identify recurring logistics challenges and contribute to continuous improvement efforts aimed at enhancing delivery performance, visibility, and overall customer satisfaction.
Build and maintain strong customer relationships through proactive communication, responsiveness, and a solutions-oriented approach that reinforces thrust and long-term partnership.
Act as an extension of the Sales team by supporting customer requests, identifying upsell or follow-on order opportunities, and ensuring seamless handoffs between Sales and Operations.

Communicate extensively with customers, carriers, brokers, and internal teams via high-volume email and time-sensitive phone calls, responding promptly to inquiries, issues, and escalation needs related to active orders and shipments.

Prepare, retrieve, and distribute customer-requested documentation such as invoices, packing lists, certificates, and shipping records, ensuring accuracy and compliance with customer and regulatory requirements.
Carry out other duties to support departmental and organizational objectives.
description:
About the job

Principal Function: The Order Fulfillment Specialist - Customer Success plays a critical role in efficiently receiving, reviewing, and processing all inbound customer purchase orders. This position ensures seamless coordination between customers, sales and internal teams, facilitates accurate order entry and timely fulfillment, and contributes to maintaining organized, compliant, and accurate internal processes.




Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Duties include, but are not limited to:




1. Customer Order Processing

Receive, review, and validate customer purchase orders to ensure accuracy of pricing, contractual terms, delivery requirements, and compliance with internal policies.
Request and coordinate product allocation, confirm material availability, and enter customer orders accurately into internal systems while monitoring order status through fulfillment.
Proactively track and resolve order delays or discrepancies, collaborating cross-functionally with Sales, Supply Chain, Planning, Finance and Customer Success teams to align on delivery timelines and customer expectations.
Generate and distribute sales order confirmations to customers, ensuring clear communication of order details and fulfillment milestones.
Document, verify, and maintain records of any applicable third-party broker commissions associated with customer orders, ensuring accuracy and audit readiness.

2. Customer Outbound Logistics

Plan, select, and execute all outbound customer shipments, balancing, cost efficiency, service reliability, and customer-specific delivery requirements.
Monitor shipment progress from dispatch through final delivery, providing timely, proactive updates to customers and internal stakeholders.
Coordinate closely with freight brokers, carriers, and warehouse partners to schedule shipments, resolve transit issues, mitigate delays, develop contingency plans to minimize customer impact.
Identify recurring logistics challenges and contribute to continuous improvement efforts aimed at enhancing delivery performance, visibility, and overall customer satisfaction.

3. Customer Support

Build and maintain strong customer relationships through proactive communication, responsiveness, and a solutions-oriented approach that reinforces thrust and long-term partnership.
Act as an extension of the Sales team by supporting customer requests, identifying upsell or follow-on order opportunities, and ensuring seamless handoffs between Sales and Operations.
Communicate extensively with customers, carriers, brokers, and internal teams via high-volume email and time-sensitive phone calls, responding promptly to inquiries, issues, and escalation needs related to active orders and shipments.
Prepare, retrieve, and distribute customer-requested documentation such as invoices, packing lists, certificates, and shipping records, ensuring accuracy and compliance with customer and regulatory requirements.

4. Other

Carry out other duties to support departmental and organizational objectives.







Supervisory Responsibilities: This job has no supervisory responsibilities.

Qualifications: To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty according to the requirements of the Company. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skills, and/or abilities required. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential duties and responsibilities.




Company-Related Competencies: To perform their jobs successfully, all employees working in the St. Louis Group should demonstrate the following competencies:

· Ethics – Treats people with respect; Keeps commitments; Inspires the trust of others; Works ethically and with integrity; Upholds Company values.

· Professionalism – Approaches others in a tactful manner; Reacts well under pressure; Treats others with respect and consideration regardless of their status or position; Accepts responsibility for personal actions; Follows through on commitments.

· Problem Solving – Identifies and resolves problems in a timely manner; Gathers and analyzes information skillfully; Develops alternative solutions; Works well in group problem solving situations; Uses reason even when dealing with emotional topics.

· Written and Oral Communication – Speaks clearly and persuasively in positive or negative situations; Listens and obtains clarification; Responds well to questions; Demonstrates group presentation skills; Participates in meetings; Writes clearly and informatively; Edits work for spelling and grammar; Varies writing style to meet needs; Presents numerical data effectively; Reads with the ability to interpret written information.

· Teamwork – Balances team and individual responsibilities; Exhibits objectivity and openness to others’ views; Gives and welcomes feedback; Contributes to building a positive team spirit; Puts success of team above own interests; Able to build morale and group commitments to goals and objectives; Supports everyone’s efforts to succeed.

· Adaptability – Adapts to changes in the work environment; Manages competing demands; Changes approach or method to best fit the situation; Able to deal with frequent change, delays or unexpected events.




Job-Related Competencies – To perform this job successfully, employees in this job should demonstrate the following competencies:

· Quality and Quantity – Demonstrates accuracy and thoroughness; Looks for ways to improve and promote quality; Applies feedback to improve performance; Monitors own work to ensure quality; Meets productivity standards; Completes work in timely manner; Strives to increase productivity; Works quickly but efficiently.

· Customer Service – Manages difficult or emotional customer situations; Responds promptly to customer needs; Solicits customer feedback to improve service; Responds to requests for service and assistance; Meets commitments.

· Dependability – Follows instructions; Responds to management direction; Takes responsibility for own actions; Keeps commitments; Commits to long hours of work when necessary to reach goals; Completes tasks on time or notifies appropriate person with an alternate plan.

· Organizational Support – Follows policies and procedures; Completes administrative tasks correctly and on time; Supports organization’s goals and values; Benefits organization through outside activities; Supports affirmative action and respects diversity.

· Attendance/Punctuality – Is consistently at work and is on time; Ensures work responsibilities are covered when absent; Arrives at meetings and appointments on time.




Education and/or Experience: Bachelor's Degree and two-years related experience and/or training in an corporate office setting; or equivalent combination of education and experience.




Other Skills and Abilities: The following skills and abilities are either required or desired.

· Computer software skills including: Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Word, Excel), Adobe, and Slack messaging software are required. The ability to quickly adapt and embrace new software is required.

· The ability to develop positive working relationships and interact effectively with vendors, brokers, customers, and colleagues is required.

· The ability to effectively listen and communicate orally and in writing is required.

· The ability to manage and prioritize multiple projects simultaneously is required.

· The ability to prepare reports and forms is required.

· The ability to follow all established protocols, guidelines, and departmental policies is required.

· The ability to work alone with minimum supervision and with others in a team environment is required.

· The ability to work rapidly for long periods, under time pressure, and on several tasks at the same time is required.

· The ability to remain calm, perform duties, and make sound and rational decisions, including exercising independent judgment within established protocols and guidelines is required.

· The ability to be sensitive to professional ethics, gender, cultural diversities and disabilities is required.




· A working knowledge of standard office practices and the ability to apply such knowledge to a variety of interrelated processes, tasks, and operations is required.

· The ability to operate a computer, printer, telephone/switchboard, calculator, postage meter, copy machine, scanner, fax machine, shredder, and other office equipment is required.

· The ability to speak, read and write in English is required. Spanish is desired.

· The ability to occasionally travel out-of-town on Company-related business and/or to attend conferences and trainings, is desired.




Work Environment: The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential duties and responsibilities. While performing the duties of this job, the employee may be exposed to the risk of electrical shock when working with the St. Louis Group office equipment. The noise level in the work environment is usually quiet to moderate. While the offices of the St. Louis Group are usually open from 9:00a.m. to 5:00 p.m., Monday through Friday, this position may require work beyond these hours and on the weekends

title: Sales Development Representative
skills: 1+ year of outbound sales or SDR experience in a high-volume environment (75+ touches per day)
Proven ability to execute 1,500+ monthly activities across calls, email, and LinkedIn
Strong prospect qualification skills (ICP fit, decision-maker access, clear pain points)
Hands-on experience with CRM tools, preferably HubSpot
Proficiency using LinkedIn Sales Navigator and sales engagement tools
Excellent written and verbal English communication skills
Ability to work U.S. hours in a remote, async environment
High resilience with consistent performance despite rejection
Experience selling into financial services or wealth management firms
Familiarity with RIAs, broker-dealers, or multi-family offices
Experience using tools like Ample Market or similar prospecting platforms
Track record of 50%+ SAL conversion from held meetings
Exposure to outbound sequence optimization and messaging testing
Execute 1,500+ outbound activities per month across cold and recycled leads using calls, email, and LinkedIn
Identify and prioritize net-new prospects aligned with the ideal customer profile
Qualify prospects before booking by validating firm type, AUM, decision-maker access, and pain points
Book 15+ qualified meetings per month and ensure 80%+ show rate through confirmations and follow-ups
Re-engage dormant pipeline leads with tailored outreach sequences to revive opportunities
Log all prospecting activity in HubSpot in real time with accurate notes and pipeline updates
Prepare detailed handoff notes for Account Executives to ensure productive meetings
Monitor SAL conversion rates and refine qualification approach to maintain 50%+ conversion
Share frontline feedback on messaging, objections, and sequence performance to improve playbooks
Collaborate with the outbound manager on reporting, call reviews, and continuous process optimization
description:
About the job

Our client builds risk management and compliance technology for wealth management firms. They serve a broad SME market of ~15,000 independent firms and drive growth through disciplined, data-driven outbound. Their model combines high-volume execution with rigorous qualification to generate qualified pipeline for Account Executives.




Need to have qualifications:

1+ year of outbound sales or SDR experience in a high-volume environment (75+ touches per day)
Proven ability to execute 1,500+ monthly activities across calls, email, and LinkedIn
Strong prospect qualification skills (ICP fit, decision-maker access, clear pain points)
Hands-on experience with CRM tools, preferably HubSpot
Proficiency using LinkedIn Sales Navigator and sales engagement tools
Excellent written and verbal English communication skills
Ability to work U.S. hours in a remote, async environment
High resilience with consistent performance despite rejection




Nice to have:

Experience selling into financial services or wealth management firms
Familiarity with RIAs, broker-dealers, or multi-family offices
Experience using tools like Ample Market or similar prospecting platforms
Track record of 50%+ SAL conversion from held meetings
Exposure to outbound sequence optimization and messaging testing




Responsibilities:

Execute 1,500+ outbound activities per month across cold and recycled leads using calls, email, and LinkedIn
Identify and prioritize net-new prospects aligned with the ideal customer profile
Qualify prospects before booking by validating firm type, AUM, decision-maker access, and pain points
Book 15+ qualified meetings per month and ensure 80%+ show rate through confirmations and follow-ups
Re-engage dormant pipeline leads with tailored outreach sequences to revive opportunities
Log all prospecting activity in HubSpot in real time with accurate notes and pipeline updates
Prepare detailed handoff notes for Account Executives to ensure productive meetings
Monitor SAL conversion rates and refine qualification approach to maintain 50%+ conversion
Share frontline feedback on messaging, objections, and sequence performance to improve playbooks
Collaborate with the outbound manager on reporting, call reviews, and continuous process optimization

description:
About the job

About the job

About AdLib At AdLib, we believe the next evolution of digital advertising is here. Marketers today face an increasingly complex landscape, where reaching the right audiences efficiently requires cutting-edge technology and a fresh approach. That's why we're building the modern ad platform for programmatic advertising - one that drives measurable results without the complexity of legacy solutions.

Our team is made up of seasoned industry experts who have helped shape the digital advertising ecosystem, now bringing that expertise to redefine how brands grow across channels.




We're a remote-first company with a culture of innovation, ownership, and impact.




Why Join AdLib?

Be part of a fast-growing startup redefining digital advertising.
Work on impactful challenges in a $300B industry ripe for innovation.
Join a high-caliber team with deep experience in adtech, analytics, and performance marketing.
Make an immediate impact with ownership and room to grow.




About the Role: Account Analyst (REMOTE - EST or CST) 




AdLib is looking for a detail-oriented Account Analyst to support the day-to-day operations behind our brand and agency partnerships. In this role, you'll keep campaigns running smoothly, handle the operational tasks that keep accounts healthy, and work alongside our Account Managers to deliver a great client experience.




You'll be the person who makes sure the details are right, from campaign setup to performance reporting. This role is a great fit for someone early in their adtech career who wants hands-on experience with media buying, analytics, and the mechanics of programmatic advertising.




What You'll Be Doing

Support clients with setup, trafficking, and QA of campaigns across multiple DSPs.
Pull, organize, and format performance reports for clients and internal teams.
Monitor active campaigns for pacing, delivery, and performance issues, and flag anything that needs attention.
Help troubleshoot day-to-day campaign questions and escalate when needed.
Maintain accurate account records, documentation, and trackers.
Assist Account Managers with client requests, meeting prep, and follow-ups.
Learn the AdLib platform inside and out so you can answer common client questions and support best practices.
Keep an eye on the small stuff that keeps accounts accurate and clients happy.




What You Bring

1-2 years of experience in account management, ad operations, or a similar role at a DSP




Familiarity with the digital advertising ecosystem and a desire to learn more.
Strong attention to detail and solid organizational skills.
Comfort working in spreadsheets and reporting tools.
Clear communication skills and a team-first attitude.
Eagerness to learn in a fast-moving environment.




What We Offer

A foot in the door at the forefront of modern adtech innovation
Competitive salary & performance-based bonuses
Unlimited PTO
Flexible, remote-first culture
Coworking space allowance
Gym allowance
Mobile Phone Plan allowance




Join us at AdLib and help brands unlock their next level of growth.

description:
About the job

EL EQUIPO

El equipo de Universe forma parte del ecosistema de Ticketmaster y está enfocado en ofrecer soluciones innovadoras de venta de boletos para eventos de admisión general, complementadas con estrategias de marketing digital que impulsan el alcance, engagement y conversión de nuestros clientes.




DESCRIPCIÓN DEL CARGO

Buscamos un Ejecutivo/a de Servicios Digitales para Universe, nuestra plataforma enfocada en eventos de admisión general, que combine la venta de boletos con soluciones estratégicas de marketing digital. Esta posición será responsable de asesorar a clientes (promotores, venues y organizadores) para maximizar el desempeño de sus eventos a través de herramientas digitales y acompañamiento consultivo.




Tendrás la oportunidad de trabajar en un puesto que combina tecnología y marketing digital impactando directamente en la venta de boletos y en la experiencia de nuestros clientes.







LO QUE HARÁS

Asesorar a clientes sobre el uso las soluciones de marketing digital de para maximizar la venta de boletos.
Ejecutar reuniones consultivas con organizadores e inmuebles para entender sus necesidades y proponer estrategias digitales.
Coordinar la implementación de campañas digitales (orgánicas y paid media) para eventos en la plataforma.
Gestionar la recepción de contenidos asegurando el cumplimiento de lineamientos técnicos y creativos.
Dar seguimiento a la ejecución de campañas para asegurar entregables en tiempo y forma.
Monitorear métricas clave de desempeño para optimizar resultados comerciales.
Elaborar y presentar reportes de resultados a clientes utilizando herramientas analíticas.
Coordinar la publicación de contenido en canales digitales como blog y LinkedIn B2B para posicionamiento de marca.
Documentar acuerdos, minutas y planes de acción para garantizar el seguimiento con clientes y equipos internos.
Colaborar con equipos internos (Paid Media, Comercial, Revenue, Legal, Operaciones) para la correcta ejecución de campañas.
Proponer mejoras continuas en estrategias digitales basadas en análisis de datos y tendencias de mercado.
Fortalecer relaciones con clientes para asegurar retención y crecimiento.




LO QUE NECESITAS SABER (o HABILIDADES TÉCNICAS)

Licenciatura en Marketing, Publicidad, Comunicación o afín.
Experiencia mínima de 2 años en marketing digital o gestión de campañas.
Conocimiento en marketing B2B y manejo de cuentas o clientes.
Experiencia en interpretación de métricas digitales y analítica.
Manejo de herramientas como Office 365; deseable Google Analytics y Looker Studio.
Experiencia en coordinación de campañas paid media y orgánicas.
Inglés intermedio-avanzado.
Habilidad para gestionar múltiples proyectos simultáneamente.
Conocimiento en plataformas digitales, e-commerce




TÚ (BEHAVIOURAL SKILLS)

Construyes relaciones de confianza a largo plazo con clientes y stakeholders.
Comunicas ideas de forma clara, empática y efectiva en distintos niveles de la organización.
Te enfocas en cumplir y superar objetivos comerciales.
Comunicas ideas de forma clara, estructurada y persuasiva.
Tomas decisiones basadas en datos y análisis.
Te adaptas a entornos dinámicos y de rápido crecimiento.
Actúas con ética y responsabilidad en todas tus interacciones.




VIDA EN TICKETMASTER

Nos enorgullece formar parte de Live Nation Entertainment, la empresa de entretenimiento en vivo más grande del mundo. Nuestra visión en Ticketmaster es conectar a las personas alrededor del mundo con los eventos en vivo que aman.

Como el mercado de boletos más grande del mundo y el proveedor global líder de herramientas y servicios para la industria del entretenimiento en vivo, estamos en una posición única para cumplir con esa visión.

Lo hacemos todo con una pasión intensa por el entretenimiento en vivo y una cultura inspiradora y diversa, impulsada por líderes accesibles, gerentes atentos y equipos entusiastas. Si te apasiona el entretenimiento en vivo como a nosotros, y deseas trabajar en una empresa dedicada a ayudar a millones de fanáticos a vivir esa experiencia, queremos saber de ti.

Nuestra labor se guía por nuestros valores:

Fiabilidad: Entendemos que los fanáticos y clientes dependen de nosotros para hacer posible sus experiencias en eventos en vivo, y nosotros dependemos los unos de los otros para lograrlo.
Trabajo en equipo: Creemos que el logro individual es insignificante en comparación con el éxito que se puede lograr en equipo.
Integridad: Estamos comprometidos con los más altos estándares morales y éticos en nombre de los innumerables socios y partes interesadas que representamos.
Sentido de pertenencia: Estamos comprometidos a construir una cultura en la que todas las personas puedan ser su yo auténtico, tengan voz igualitaria y oportunidades para prosperar.







OPORTUNIDADES IGUALES

Estamos apasionados y comprometidos con nuestra gente, y vamos más allá de la retórica sobre diversidad e inclusión. Trabajarás en un entorno inclusivo y se te alentará a ser tú mismo en el trabajo. Haremos todo lo posible para ayudarte a equilibrar exitosamente tu vida laboral y personal. Como negocio en crecimiento, fomentaremos que desarrolles tanto tus aspiraciones profesionales como personales, disfrutes de nuevas experiencias y aprendas de las personas talentosas con las que trabajarás. Nos importa el talento, y alentamos las postulaciones de personas sin importar su género, raza, orientación sexual, religión, edad, estado de discapacidad o responsabilidades familiares.




PRÁCTICAS DE CONTRATACIÓN

La descripción del puesto anterior ha sido diseñada para indicar la naturaleza general y el nivel del trabajo realizado por los empleados dentro de esta clasificación. No está destinada a contener ni a interpretarse como un inventario exhaustivo de todas las funciones, responsabilidades y cualificaciones requeridas para los empleados asignados a este puesto.

Las políticas de contratación de Ticketmaster están diseñadas para incorporar a las personas más calificadas disponibles de manera oportuna y eficiente. Ticketmaster puede utilizar todos los medios disponibles, incluyendo la promoción interna, referencias de empleados, publicidad externa, agencias de empleo, reclutamiento por internet, ferias de empleo, reclutamiento universitario y head hunters. Ticketmaster puede utilizar herramientas de inteligencia artificial (IA) para apoyar la preselección y evaluación de candidaturas. Todas las decisiones de contratación se toman con revisión humana.

description:
