About The Role

As a Product Analyst, you will be supporting the growth, engagement, and commercial success of SportyBet in Mexico by delivering high-quality analysis, market intelligence, and actionable insights. This role is responsible for deep-diving into data, conducting competitor research and benchmarking, and translating findings into clear recommendations for the Product Manager and wider product team.

You will combine strong analytical skills with deep local market understanding to inform product decisions in a fast-moving, regulated environment.

title: Pasantía en Comunicación y Redes Sociales
description:
About the job

🎓 ¿Buscás tu primera experiencia profesional internacional en comunicación?




Si dijiste que sí, esta es tu oportunidad con Huellas




En Huellas, nos encontramos en búsqueda de estudiantes de Comunicación, Relaciones Públicas, Marketing, Publicidad o carreras afines para el puesto de analista de Comunicaciones. 

 

Las principales tareas a desarrollar como pasante de comunicación son:




✅Generación de contenidos para redes sociales. 

✅Redacción de notas de prensa, publicaciones institucionales, comunicaciones internas, entre otros. 

✅Mantener contacto con influencers, periodistas y medios de comunicación. 

✅Desarrollo de estrategias comunicacionales, para distintos aspectos de la vida organizacional.

title: Strategy & Operations Project Intern (GBS-Monetization Strategy and Operations-LATAM) - 2026 Start (BS/MS)
skills: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to perform market and competitive research to inform strategic planning.
Assist in preparing executive reports and presentations based on your analyses and research.
Analyze revenue trends and product performance to support monetization strategy and decision-making.
Contribute to ongoing initiatives across Sales, Product Strategy, and Operations.
Leverage data tools (Excel/basic SQL) to extract insights that drive business strategy.
Develop clear and compelling charts and visualizations to communicate insights effectively to stakeholders.
Build and maintain dashboards, analytical tools, and automated reporting pipelines.
Conduct rigorous data hygiene practices: clean datasets, identify discrepancies, and propose solutions to ensure data integrity.
Currently pursuing an undergraduate degree, ideally in the final year (last 12 months) of study
We welcome all fields of study, especially Business, Engineering, Economics, or related management and analytical disciplines with a strong strategic focus.
Demonstrated ability to conduct structured analysis and break down complex, ambiguous problems into clear, manageable components.
Strong written communication skills, with a proven ability to articulate complex ideas clearly and concisely to stakeholders at all levels.
Familiarity with data analysis and basic modeling in Excel or Google Sheets.
Proactive, "get-it-done" attitude, combined with high intellectual curiosity and a passion for solving business problems.
Strong interest in the technology industry, digital media, and the competitive landscape of social platforms.
Full professional proficiency in English (mandatory) and Spanish.
Prior internship experience in strategy, management consulting, business operations, or finance.
Advanced proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets (e.g., pivot tables, complex formulas, data modeling).
Basic understanding of or familiarity with SQL and/or data visualization tools (like Tableau, Looker) is a plus, but not required.
Demonstrated leadership experience through extracurricular activities, volunteer work, or other projects.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic, and often ambiguous environment.
description:
About the job

Responsibilities

Team Introduction

The Monetization Strategy & Operations team at TikTok LATAM plays a crucial role in driving strategic insights, optimizing performance, and enabling data-driven decision-making to fuel the region’s rapid growth. We are a high-performing, cross-functional team at the intersection of sales, product, and operations. Our work spans everything from revenue strategy and business analysis to data visualization and competitive benchmarking.

We are seeking an exceptional Strategy & Operations Intern who is passionate about using data to uncover insights, support operational excellence, and shape strategic direction. This internship offers a unique opportunity to work on high-impact projects and gain exposure to both local and global business operations at TikTok.

We are looking for talented individuals to join us for an internship in 2026. Internships at TikTok aim to offer students industry exposure and hands-on experience. Turn your ambitions into reality as your inspiration brings infinite opportunities at TikTok.

As a project intern, you will have the opportunity to engage in impactful short-term projects that provide you with a glimpse of professional real-world experience. You will gain practical skills through on-the-job learning in a fast-paced work environment and develop a deeper understanding of your career interests.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis . We encourage you to apply early.

Responsibilities


 Collaborate with cross-functional teams to perform market and competitive research to inform strategic planning.
 Assist in preparing executive reports and presentations based on your analyses and research.
 Analyze revenue trends and product performance to support monetization strategy and decision-making.
 Contribute to ongoing initiatives across Sales, Product Strategy, and Operations.
 Leverage data tools (Excel/basic SQL) to extract insights that drive business strategy.
 Develop clear and compelling charts and visualizations to communicate insights effectively to stakeholders.
 Build and maintain dashboards, analytical tools, and automated reporting pipelines.
 Conduct rigorous data hygiene practices: clean datasets, identify discrepancies, and propose solutions to ensure data integrity.


Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:


 Currently pursuing an undergraduate degree, ideally in the final year (last 12 months) of study
 We welcome all fields of study, especially Business, Engineering, Economics, or related management and analytical disciplines with a strong strategic focus.
 Demonstrated ability to conduct structured analysis and break down complex, ambiguous problems into clear, manageable components.
 Strong written communication skills, with a proven ability to articulate complex ideas clearly and concisely to stakeholders at all levels.
 Familiarity with data analysis and basic modeling in Excel or Google Sheets.
 Proactive, "get-it-done" attitude, combined with high intellectual curiosity and a passion for solving business problems.
 Strong interest in the technology industry, digital media, and the competitive landscape of social platforms.
 Full professional proficiency in English (mandatory) and Spanish.


Preferred Qualifications:


 Prior internship experience in strategy, management consulting, business operations, or finance.
 Advanced proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets (e.g., pivot tables, complex formulas, data modeling).
 Basic understanding of or familiarity with SQL and/or data visualization tools (like Tableau, Looker) is a plus, but not required.
 Demonstrated leadership experience through extracurricular activities, volunteer work, or other projects.
 Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic, and often ambiguous environment.


Job Information

About TikTok

TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.

Why Join Us

Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.

We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion

TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

TikTok Accommodation

TikTok is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in our recruitment processes for candidates with disabilities, pregnancy, sincerely held religious beliefs or other reasons protected by applicable laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at https://tinyurl.com/RA-request

description:
About the job

Project Overview:




Are you a professional linguist or do you hold an academic degree in translation? 

We are hiring professional linguists (or people who have an academic degree in translation) to join a fascinating project focused on auditing and evaluating the quality of conversations generated by two different AI models. As a linguist on this project, you will assess chatbot conversations based on a set of core dimensions, thus helping to improve the quality of AI-driven communication.




Job Type: Freelance

Location: Remote

Work Schedule: Part-time - 10+ hours per week. Flexible - work whenever you want.

Start Date: Immediately

Rate: 23 USD




What We Offer:

Flexible Schedule: You have the freedom to complete your daily hours at any time that suits you, day or night
Competitive Rates: We offer competitive compensation for your expertise
Future Opportunities: Successful linguists may be invited to join our vetted pool for similar exciting projects in the future
Cutting-Edge Project: Be part of a project that's at the forefront of AI development and conversational technology




Responsibilities:

Audit and evaluate chatbot conversations based on three core dimensions: Naturalness/Engagement, Utility, and Audio Quality
Follow project-specific guidelines to ensure consistent and accurate evaluations
Use a proprietary client platform to complete your tasks
Preferably, meet a daily workload of approximately 4 hours per business day




Requirements:

A degree in Translation/Linguistics or a related field OR experience as a professional linguist
Native-level fluency in Spanish (Latam)
Available to start on or around July 1st
Full availability for the entire 2-month project duration
Ability to commit to a workload of approximately 4 hours per business day - preferably.
Must be a freelancer (we are not engaging with companies for this project)
Must be willing to take a project-specific qualification test




Recruitment Process:

Application Review: We will review your application to ensure you meet the basic requirements
Language Knowledge test: You’ll complete a short assessment in English, and also in your target language
Onboarding: Once you pass the test, you'll go through a streamlined onboarding process to get you started
Qualification Test: You will be asked to complete a short, automated test based on the project guidelines to ensure you understand the task




Vendor Type

When applying, please select the option that best describes your working status.

Are you a Freelancer or Private Individual?

A Freelancer is someone who has a business license and may have a VAT number or EIN for tax purposes. A VAT (Value Added Tax) number is a unique identifier assigned to registered businesses in many countries; an EIN (Employer Identification Number) is a similar identifier used in the US. A Private Individual does not have a business license. If you aren’t sure, you are probably a private individual.




RWS embraces DEI and promotes equal opportunity, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind. RWS is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. All employment decisions at RWS are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, religion, nationality, ethnicity, sex, age, disability, or sexual orientation. RWS will not tolerate discrimination based on any of these characteristics.

We are a high-production U.S. real estate operation seeking an experienced Transaction Coordinator to manage listings and contracts from pre-listing through closing. This is not a basic VA role.

You will manage multiple active files, communicate directly with clients, agents, lenders, and title companies, and ensure transactions move efficiently and compliantly to closing.

Full-time (40 hours per week)

Long-term international contractor role

Has 2+ years of U.S. real estate transaction experience

Has managed MLS listings directly

description:
About the job

Persona is where the world’s best companies and the world’s best talent meet.




We place exceptional people from 98 countries into full-time, long-term remote roles with the most ambitious companies on earth. Fortune 500 enterprises, fast-growing startups backed by Sequoia, Founders Fund, and Andreessen Horowitz, and high-performing companies of every size in between. Project management, operations, finance, design, marketing, customer success, and more. One person, one dedicated client, built to last.




The bar is the highest in the industry. More than 200,000 people apply every month, and fewer than 1 in 2,000 are accepted. If you make it, you join a network of people who have cleared a standard most companies couldn’t recruit for on their own, and you get the kind of role that’s almost impossible to find on your own: industry-leading pay set against your experience and skill, real career growth, and a stable seat at a serious company, working from home, anywhere in the world.




Persona was founded in 2018 by two Stanford graduates on a simple idea: talent is everywhere, but the systems that connect talent to opportunity are broken. We’re rebuilding them, and we’ve grown into one of the fastest-growing companies in the global talent industry doing it.




We hire people who care about the craft of their work, take ownership without being asked, and want to build a career rather than just hold a job. If that’s you, we’d like to meet you.




Role Summary

We are looking for a Customer Support and IT Helpdesk Specialist to support internal employees (not external customers). This role focuses on resolving employee technical and procedural issues, supporting internal systems and software, and delivering high-quality IT helpdesk service in a remote-first environment. Strong familiarity with Windows, macOS, common SaaS platforms, and ticketing systems is required.




Key Responsibilities

Respond to employee queries (email and chat) in a timely, accurate, and professional way
Identify employee needs; help them use specific features and resolve troubleshooting issues
Analyze and report product malfunctions; reproduce issues and escalate to engineering
Update internal databases and knowledge bases with technical issues, fixes, and discussions
Share feature requests and effective workarounds with internal team members
Inform employees about new features and functionality changes; deliver release training
Follow up with employees to ensure technical issues are fully resolved
Gather employee feedback and share with operations and IT leadership
Assist in training junior Customer Support Representatives and IT helpdesk team members




Required Qualifications

Proven experience as a Customer Support Specialist, IT Helpdesk Specialist, or similar role
Proficiency with both Windows and macOS operating systems
Familiarity with general IT administration (user provisioning, account access, MFA)
Experience using help desk and ticketing software (Jira Service Management, Zendesk, Freshservice, ServiceNow)
Strong communication and patience when handling tough cases
Comfort with remote work tools and shadowing techniques
Fully fluent in English
Ability to commit long-term and full-time




Preferred Qualifications

Familiarity with Microsoft 365 / Office 365 and Google Workspace administration
Familiarity with NetSuite, ERP systems, project management or accounting software
Experience with user acceptance testing (UAT)
CompTIA A+ or similar entry-level IT certification




Tools & Technologies

Jira Service Management, Zendesk, Freshservice, ServiceNow, Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Slack, Okta, MFA tools; NetSuite or ERP exposure (a plus)




Core Competencies

Patient, empathetic communicator with non-technical employees
Methodical troubleshooter

Curious about new tools and willing to learn




What We Offer

Best salaries in the industry
Work from anywhere permanently

title: DiDi Shop User Growth Operations Analyst
skills: Building deep data insights and understanding of our potential and existing users and retailers in the local culture, in order to build your own growth-oriented methodologies and initiatives, and also to constantly improve user experience.

Using data to understand user and merchant behavior and experience, and working closely with commercial and merchant partners to identify new opportunities for merchant growth, while always being aware of ROI.
Previous operational/analytical experience in strategy operations, data analytics, consulting, investment banking, or the tech industry is a plus.
Fluent in English.
Data-driven with strong analytical thinking skills.
Excellent strategic thinking and an evolving mindset.
Self-motivated, self-directed, and eager to take ownership of tasks to ensure solutions.
Experience in online Grocery (or Food) delivery businesses, especially in user, merchant, or category growth roles, is a nice to have.
description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

The User Growth Operations Analyst role offers a unique opportunity to drive growth in one of the fastest-growing companies, specifically in an exciting & booming new business line. This data-driven position requires a blend of analytical thinking and interpersonal skills to effectively manage the user base and influence behavior.

In this role, you'll be...

What will you be doing?


Building deep data insights and understanding of our potential and existing users and retailers in the local culture, in order to build your own growth-oriented methodologies and initiatives, and also to constantly improve user experience.
Using data to understand user and merchant behavior and experience, and working closely with commercial and merchant partners to identify new opportunities for merchant growth, while always being aware of ROI.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


 Previous operational/analytical experience in strategy operations, data analytics, consulting, investment banking, or the tech industry is a plus.
 Fluent in English.
 Data-driven with strong analytical thinking skills.
 Excellent strategic thinking and an evolving mindset.
 Self-motivated, self-directed, and eager to take ownership of tasks to ensure solutions.
 Experience in online Grocery (or Food) delivery businesses, especially in user, merchant, or category growth roles, is a nice to have.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

title: CX Intelligence Analyst
skills: Deeply understand the information needs of the CX team and other key internal clients, proactively identifying gaps and opportunities to drive the business forward.
Monitor customer-centric KPIs through robust reporting and analytics, promptly alerting the organization to critical developments and strategic opportunities.
Generate actionable, data-driven recommendations that directly improve customer experience and support business objectives.
Act as Point of Contact (PoC) for the CX IBG Business Insights team in Mexico, representing the area and ensuring alignment with local and global stakeholders.
Design, evolve, and maintain automated reports, dashboards, and alert systems to enable self-service and foster a data-driven culture across the organization.
Continuously refine analytical solutions to meet emerging business needs and ensure scalability.
Collaborate with Business Process teams and other stakeholders to develop comprehensive user guides and documentation, guaranteeing the proper use of analytical tools.
Lead training sessions and workshops to empower users to leverage data in solving complex business cases, reinforcing data-driven decision making at all levels.
Proven experience in roles delivering data-driven insights to organizations, such as Business Intelligence, Business Analytics, Finance, Strategic Planning or Business Consulting.
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Finance, Economics or related fields.
Strong statistical foundation, with the ability to design hypothesis tests and apply appropriate algorithms to solve complex business problems.
Solid Python or R for data analysis and modeling, along with SQL skills and advanced use of Google Sheets (or Excel).
Demonstrated ability to synthesize large, complex datasets into clear insights and actionable recommendations.
Highly results-oriented, with a mindset geared toward continuous improvement and delivering business impact.
Passion for data and uncovering stories behind the numbers.
Strong business acumen to contextualize analyses within strategic objectives.
Communication and stakeholder management skills, capable of engaging both technical and non-technical audiences.
Fluent in English (required); Spanish is a plus.

About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. About The Team/role

We embed ourselves in key projects and ensure that the team’s decisions are grounded in robust information, enabling smarter, faster, and more customer-centric outcomes.

In this role, you will drive analytical initiatives, shape metrics frameworks, and help answer critical business questions with clarity and depth. It’s an opportunity to influence CX strategies across multiple markets through rigorous analysis and a passion for elevating customer experiences.

In this role, you'll be...


 Deeply understand the information needs of the CX team and other key internal clients, proactively identifying gaps and opportunities to drive the business forward.
 Monitor customer-centric KPIs through robust reporting and analytics, promptly alerting the organization to critical developments and strategic opportunities.
 Generate actionable, data-driven recommendations that directly improve customer experience and support business objectives.
 Act as Point of Contact (PoC) for the CX IBG Business Insights team in Mexico, representing the area and ensuring alignment with local and global stakeholders.
 Design, evolve, and maintain automated reports, dashboards, and alert systems to enable self-service and foster a data-driven culture across the organization.
 Continuously refine analytical solutions to meet emerging business needs and ensure scalability.
 Collaborate with Business Process teams and other stakeholders to develop comprehensive user guides and documentation, guaranteeing the proper use of analytical tools.
 Lead training sessions and workshops to empower users to leverage data in solving complex business cases, reinforcing data-driven decision making at all levels.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


 Proven experience in roles delivering data-driven insights to organizations, such as Business Intelligence, Business Analytics, Finance, Strategic Planning or Business Consulting.
 Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Finance, Economics or related fields.
 Strong statistical foundation, with the ability to design hypothesis tests and apply appropriate algorithms to solve complex business problems.
 Solid Python or R for data analysis and modeling, along with SQL skills and advanced use of Google Sheets (or Excel).
 Demonstrated ability to synthesize large, complex datasets into clear insights and actionable recommendations.
 Highly results-oriented, with a mindset geared toward continuous improvement and delivering business impact.
 Passion for data and uncovering stories behind the numbers.
 Strong business acumen to contextualize analyses within strategic objectives.
 Communication and stakeholder management skills, capable of engaging both technical and non-technical audiences.
 Fluent in English (required); Spanish is a plus.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. title: Product Manager
skills: Competitive salary
Professional development opportunities
Collaborative and supportive work environment
100% remote work
Run continuous discovery with advisors, RIAs, and internal teams to validate problems before building
Own a single intake for feature and enhancement requests from CS, Sales, SE, and Product Marketing
Capture and prioritize feature gaps that block deals, using Sales as a discovery channel
Define the "why" and success metric for each initiative and pressure-test against the ICP
Maintain a living quarterly roadmap tied to company goals (e.g., pilot-to-recurring conversion)
Make explicit prioritization calls, balancing new features, customer commitments, and technical health
Keep a single, visible source of truth for what's next and why
Re-sequence quickly and transparently when priorities shift
Write clear PRDs with acceptance criteria and edge cases
Run internal requirements meetings with product owners, designers, and engineers
Keep at least two sprints of groomed, ready-to-build work ahead of the team at all times
Participate in sprint planning to align goals with engineering
Track delivery against commitments and surface risks early
Create release notes and coordinate newsletters with the Product Marketing team
Demo new features internally and with customers to close the product-feedback loop
Measure adoption and outcomes post-release
Give Sales, CS, and customers timely answers on product questions and timelines
Equip Product Marketing, Sales, and CS with substance for each release
Publish regular roadmap and release communications
Manage expectations honestly when scope or dates change
Proven experience as a Product Manager, ideally in a B2B SaaS environment
Strong ability to write clear specs, PRDs, and acceptance criteria engineers can build from without back-and-forth
Experience running customer discovery and translating insights into prioritized roadmap decisions
Proficiency with product and project management tools (e.g., Jira, Linear, Productboard, or similar)
Exceptional organizational skills and strong attention to detail
Strong communication skills — able to translate between technical detail and business/customer language
Experience in fintech, wealth management, or financial advisory software
Familiarity with the RIA/advisor ecosystem (compliance, risk, operations workflows)
Experience working with Agile/Scrum teams
Proven ability to drive adoption metrics and close the loop post-release
description:
About the job

Floowi connects top LATAM professionals with leading U.S. companies through remote talent solutions. Our mission is to make LATAM the world's premier hub for growth and excellence.




🌟 About our Client:

A fintech company building software for financial advisors and RIAs (Registered Investment Advisors). The organization provides a suite of compliance, risk, and operations tools — helping advisory firms scale through structured workflows, data-driven decision-making, and integrated digital operations.




✍ Short Description:

We're seeking a Product Manager to own the full path from customer problem to shipped product. Reporting to the Head of Product & Technology, you will turn company strategy into a prioritized roadmap, keep engineering continuously fed with well-specified work, and serve as the single source of truth on product status for Sales, CS, customers, and leadership — all while driving our client's growth into recurring scale.




💰 Compensation: Competitive salary in USD

🗣 Language Requirements: Native-level English fluency required

📌 Location: 100% Remote

😀 Start Date: ASAP




🤔 What We Offer:

Competitive salary
Professional development opportunities
Collaborative and supportive work environment
100% remote work




💼 Responsibilities:

Discovery & Customer Insight

Run continuous discovery with advisors, RIAs, and internal teams to validate problems before building
Own a single intake for feature and enhancement requests from CS, Sales, SE, and Product Marketing
Capture and prioritize feature gaps that block deals, using Sales as a discovery channel
Define the "why" and success metric for each initiative and pressure-test against the ICP

Backlog & Roadmap Management

Maintain a living quarterly roadmap tied to company goals (e.g., pilot-to-recurring conversion)
Make explicit prioritization calls, balancing new features, customer commitments, and technical health
Keep a single, visible source of truth for what's next and why
Re-sequence quickly and transparently when priorities shift

Delivery Readiness

Write clear PRDs with acceptance criteria and edge cases
Run internal requirements meetings with product owners, designers, and engineers
Keep at least two sprints of groomed, ready-to-build work ahead of the team at all times
Participate in sprint planning to align goals with engineering

Release Management

Track delivery against commitments and surface risks early
Create release notes and coordinate newsletters with the Product Marketing team
Demo new features internally and with customers to close the product-feedback loop
Measure adoption and outcomes post-release

Stakeholder Communication

Give Sales, CS, and customers timely answers on product questions and timelines
Equip Product Marketing, Sales, and CS with substance for each release
Publish regular roadmap and release communications
Manage expectations honestly when scope or dates change




✅ Qualifications:

Proven experience as a Product Manager, ideally in a B2B SaaS environment
Strong ability to write clear specs, PRDs, and acceptance criteria engineers can build from without back-and-forth
Experience running customer discovery and translating insights into prioritized roadmap decisions
Proficiency with product and project management tools (e.g., Jira, Linear, Productboard, or similar)
Exceptional organizational skills and strong attention to detail
Strong communication skills — able to translate between technical detail and business/customer language




🎯 Preferred Qualifications:

Experience in fintech, wealth management, or financial advisory software
Familiarity with the RIA/advisor ecosystem (compliance, risk, operations workflows)
Experience working with Agile/Scrum teams
Proven ability to drive adoption metrics and close the loop post-release




🚀 Ready to own the product roadmap and make a real impact at a growing fintech company? Apply now and grow with us.

description:
About the job

Hay personas que viajan para descansar. Y hay personas que viajan para sentir la energía de una ciudad, descubrir su cultura, recorrer sus calles y vivir experiencias que solo ocurren en ciertos lugares del mundo.




Nueva York, Tokio, Londres, Dubái, París, Singapur, Seúl y muchas otras ciudades atraen cada año a millones de viajeros que buscan algo más que un destino: buscan una experiencia.




Si te gustan los viajes urbanos, la cultura internacional y conectar personas con experiencias memorables, puedes desarrollar una actividad enfocada en la creación y gestión de experiencias en Big Cities alrededor del mundo, con acceso a herramientas profesionales, proveedores internacionales y acompañamiento constante.




No necesitas experiencia previa en turismo. Lo más importante es el interés por aprender, la organización y el gusto por trabajar con personas.


Tareas



Diseñar experiencias según el perfil de cada cliente


Cotizar y gestionar reservas con herramientas profesionales


Acompañar a tus clientes antes, durante y después del viaje


Mantenerte actualizado/a en tendencias y novedades del sector



Requisitos



Mayor de edad


Manejo básico de herramientas digitales


Interés por los viajes y el servicio al cliente


Organización, compromiso y actitud proactiva


Inglés intermedio


Aportación mensual



Beneficios



Acceso a proveedores especializados en destinos internacionales


Formación continua sobre ciudades, temporadas y tendencias de viaje


Entrenamientos y certificaciones para fortalecer tu perfil profesional


Comunidad y mentoría constante


Posibilidad de desarrollar experiencia en diferentes tipos de viajeros y destinos


Acceso a credenciales profesionales del sector turístico





IMPORTANTE: La dedicación es flexible y se adapta a tus objetivos personales, con estructura y acompañamiento continuo.




Si quieres conocer más sobre cómo funciona esta actividad, te puedo agendar a la próxima sesión vía Zoom.

title: Spanish Language Expert - Remote
skills: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Spanish, Linguistics, Translation, Communications, Journalism, English, Education, Quality Assurance, or a relevant domain/related field.
Native or near-native Spanish proficiency with strong reading and writing skills.
Strong grasp of the English language to follow project guidelines, communicate with teams, and provide clear feedback in English.
3+ years of professional experience in Spanish writing, editing, translation, localization, content QA, AI training, education, annotation, or related language-review workflows.
Strong understanding of Spanish grammar, spelling conventions, punctuation, accents, tone, register, formal/informal usage, and cultural context.

Comfortable handling Latin American Spanish and/or European Spanish, including vocabulary, voseo/tuteo/ustedeo usage, localization expectations, and audience adaptation. Candidates should specify which variant(s) they can support.

Ability to evaluate Spanish content against detailed rubrics and identify issues such as mistranslation, literal phrasing, unnatural tone, incorrect register, missing accents, hallucinated claims, ambiguity, or inconsistent terminology.
Experience leading or supporting remote teams of trainers, annotators, reviewers, editors, or QAs is strongly preferred.
Comfortable working in fast-moving remote environments using tools such as Discord, Google Sheets, Google Docs, trackers, dashboards, and project management systems.
Highly detail-oriented and organized, with the ability to maintain style guides, FAQs, trackers, onboarding materials, honeypots, and other quality documentation.
Experience with AI training, data annotation, large language models, prompt/response evaluation, or rubric-based LLM QA is a strong plus.
Quality monitoring: Spot-check Spanish items, identify quality issues, provide ongoing feedback through DMs, and escalate recurring or critical issues.
Trainer and QA communication: Update trainers and QAs on Discord about new item guidelines, project changes, workflow updates, and quality expectations.

Question handling: Respond to trainer/QA questions clearly and promptly, especially around Spanish wording, grammar, tone, register, translation fidelity, cultural context, terminology, and Latin American vs European Spanish usage differences.
Trainer/QA activation management: DM contributors who are inactive or not working, encourage activation, track follow-ups, and flag availability issues when needed.
Documentation: Create and maintain Spanish project documentation, including style guides, trackers, FAQs, quality notes, examples, honeypots, and onboarding materials.

Onboarding and training: Schedule and run onboarding/training calls with trainers and QAs to explain project expectations, workflows, rubrics, quality standards, and Spanish-specific style requirements.
Quality alignment: Ensure all trainers and QAs apply Spanish language guidelines consistently and understand updates as projects evolve.
Process improvement: Identify recurring quality gaps, propose workflow improvements, and help build scalable QA processes for Spanish-language projects.

title: Sales Executive (B2B SaaS | Healthcare | AI)
description:
About the job

We are looking for a highly driven Sales Executive to join a fast-growing AI technology company transforming healthcare across Latin America.




This role is ideal for someone who thrives in outbound sales, enjoys building relationships from scratch, and wants to own the entire sales cycle while helping clinics and hospitals adopt cutting-edge AI solutions.




Responsibilities

Own the full sales cycle from prospecting to closing.

Generate new business primarily through outbound prospecting.

Identify and engage decision-makers within clinics, hospitals, and healthcare organizations.

Conduct discovery meetings to understand customer challenges and business needs.

Deliver product demonstrations and communicate business value to prospective clients.

Build, manage, and maintain a healthy sales pipeline using CRM tools.

Negotiate commercial terms and close new business opportunities.

Collaborate closely with Product, Customer Success, and Operations to ensure smooth customer handoffs.

Consistently achieve and exceed individual sales targets.

Provide market insights and customer feedback to help improve the product and sales strategy.




Requirements

3+ years of experience in B2B outbound sales.

Proven experience managing the complete sales process from prospecting through closing.

Experience selling SaaS, software, technology, AI, HealthTech, FinTech, or other complex B2B solutions.

Strong outbound prospecting skills and experience identifying key decision-makers.

Experience managing opportunities through a CRM.

Excellent negotiation and relationship-building skills.

Comfortable working in a fast-paced startup environment with high ownership and autonomy.

Fluent English and Spanish.




Nice to Have

Experience selling to healthcare organizations, clinics, or hospitals.

Experience selling enterprise or mid-market solutions.

Familiarity with AI, automation, or workflow technologies.




Compensation

On-Target Earnings (OTE): USD $6,000/month

Compensation structure: 60% base salary / 40% variable

Uncapped commission plan.

Opportunity to join one of the fastest-growing AI companies in healthcare.

High-impact role with significant ownership and career growth opportunities.




If you are passionate about building relationships, closing complex B2B deals, and helping shape the future of healthcare through AI, we'd love to hear from you.

description:

En The Cluster agency buscamos una persona comprometida con su trabajo y con ganas de crecer profesionalmente en un ambiente agradable en la Roma norte, CDMX (con horario flexible) y que aplique su conocimiento para coordinar las actividades y proyectos del día a día reportando los resultados de cada iniciativa para nutrir y fortalecer la estrategia en cada cuenta de la agencia.

• Resolución de problemas 

• Planificación y organización

• Uso de herramientas para análisis de tráfico y conversión

• Capacidad para trabajar en múltiples actividades al mismo tiempo.

• Alto nivel de habilidades de comunicación interpersonal, escrita y verbal.

• Redacción de informes de resultados orgánicos y mejoras en las cuentas. 




Valoraremos positivamente:

OFRECEMOS:

TU DÍA a DÍA:

Si crees que cumples con los requerimientos del puesto para superar expectativas y te interesa ser parte de un equipo multidisciplinario; si te gustan los ambientes sanos de trabajo, tienes una actitud positiva, sabes tomar iniciativa y darle seguimiento a tus proyectos; si además eres puntual y tienes empatía con clientes y equipos de trabajo, nos encantaría que trabajes con nosotros ☺️

description:
About the job

About the Role

We’re a retention-first marketing agency supporting fast-growing e-commerce brands. We pride ourselves on tight operations, fast turnaround times, and clear communication. Our team operates with structure and discipline while staying flexible and collaborative. This role blends coordination, communication, and hands-on execution across modern lifecycle platforms.




Job Summary

We’re hiring a full-time Campaign Coordinator who can manage campaign timelines, communicate clearly with strategists and project managers, and also build a QA email/SMS campaign directly. You’ll keep projects moving, ensure all deliverables stay on track, and handle the end-to-end setup of assets inside modern email and SMS programs.

If you’re organized, proactive, detail-focused, and able to move fast without sacrificing accuracy, this role is built for you.




Core Tasks

Coordination

● Manage campaign timelines and ensure deliverables move smoothly from creative to implementation

● Update Asana tasks throughout the build process

● Communicate proactively with strategists, PMs, and creatives

● Ensure all assets, briefs, and calendars stay organized and up to date

● Join client calls to provide status updates or take notes

● Flag roadblocks early and surface issues before they become delays

Implementation

● Build email and SMS campaigns inside modern lifecycle platforms

● Set up segmentation, tagging, UTMs, and filtering logic

● Schedule campaigns and flows according to the marketing calendar

● Troubleshoot basic to intermediate technical issues (events, profile inconsistencies, dynamic content)

● Conduct full QA across emails and SMS (links, rendering, device checks, dynamic content, formatting, scheduling)

● Follow structured SOPs with precision and consistency




Must Have

● 1 to 3 years of experience coordinating digital marketing campaigns or working inside modern email/SMS platforms

● Strong English communication skills (spoken and written)

● Extremely organized, dependable, and proactive in communication

● Fast-moving executor who maintains accuracy under speed

● Comfortable with process-heavy, detail-driven work

● Confident in managing timelines and juggling multiple campaigns at once

● Able to handle basic-to-intermediate troubleshooting

● Comfortable using Asana and Slack in a structured operational environment

● Eager to grow in both coordination and lifecycle execution

● Must be available during US hours, specifically 9am–5pm in CT, MT, or PT




Nice to Haves

● Client-facing relationship management experience

● Eager to grow in both coordination and lifecycle execution

● 1–2+ years working in an eCommerce-related role within an agency setting

description:
About the job

Role: AI Data Collector (Remote)

Location: Work from anywhere

Job Type: Part-Time / Flexible

Daily Commitment: Flexible

No language barriers — this project records tasks only; no audio or speech required.

No experience required.




About the Role

Rilith AI is partnering with contributors — students, working professionals (office/field), and homemakers — to work on a research project in which participants record short egocentric (first-person POV) videos of everyday household tasks and daily activities performed at their work locations. You will record your hands carrying out real-life activities — at home or at work — with no extra time required beyond your normal routine. Everybody can participate.




Pay Rate

USD 10 per hour — United States
USD 6.5 per hour — Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Colombia, Argentina




Each participant can record up to 100 hours per environment (e.g. home, warehouse).




Eligibility Requirements

You must:

Reside in the United States, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Colombia, or Argentina.
Own a compatible device:
Apple iPhone — iPhone 11 through 17 (all variants: Mini / Plus / Pro / Pro Max) (Not accepted: iPhone 16e, iPhone 17e)
Google Pixel — Pixel 6 through 10 (standard & Pro)
Samsung Galaxy — Galaxy S21 through S26 (standard / + / Ultra)




What You Will Do

Record first-person (egocentric) videos while performing real-life activities, including:

Household tasks (cooking, cleaning, laundry, and more)
Warehouse operations
Logistics activities
Retail and commercial operations

Check the app for the full list of available tasks.




Bonus & Payment Terms

Pay rate: USD 10/hr (US) / USD 6/hr (LATAM)
Payment method: PayPal
Flexible payment options - hourly, daily, weekly - as per the participants




Why Join?

Contribute to the development of next-generation AI and robotics systems
Get paid for every hour of video recorded in accordance with project guidelines
Start earning from day one
Record during your normal daily routine — no extra time needed
24/7 expert support available
Students and working professionals may request a certificate of contribution upon completion

Your data will remain secure. No personal information will be shared or disclosed without your consent.




About Rilith:

Rilith is empowering intelligent machines at scale. With teams spanning the USA, LATAM, EU, and Asia, we bring a truly global perspective to every project we undertake. Our on-the-ground experience extends beyond software and data — our team has hands-on experience working directly alongside robotic hardware, giving us a deeper understanding of real-world deployment needs. At Rilith, we take data responsibility seriously; all footage we collect is PII-free, with no face capture, ensuring every deliverable is fully privacy-safe. We don't just collect data — we collect it the right way.




We are highly agile and adaptive to challenging data projects.




Specializations:

Egocentric Head-Mounted Video – First-person POV captures using head-mounted rigs across residential and commercial settings. We handle participant recruitment, field deployments, and high-quality footage delivery.

Egocentric Human Hand Video – Hand-object interaction recordings in manufacturing, household, and daily-use settings using a multi-view rig (head, left-wrist, and right-wrist cameras).

Robotics Manipulation – Video capture of robotic gripper (2–3 finger) and dexterous hand (4–5 finger) tasks using a multi-view rig (head/overhead, left/right gripper, and front/top views).




Join our global network of Rilith Warriors:

Rilith has expertise in creating data specifically for Physical AI, which it does in collaboration with a globally spread freelancer network — we call them Rilith Warriors. What differentiates us from others is that we respect, care for, and understand the challenges faced by freelancers.

We ensure:

Timely payment
Continuous projects

title: AI Operations Analyst
skills: Frontline support: Be the first point of contact for client teams. Take in requests, respond, escalate to the founder when needed.
Diagnose the problem: Most requests won't come with clear specs. Figure out what the client actually needs and what the response should look like.
Update the tooling: Once you know the right response, go into Notion, Linear, Slack, Google Workspace, Claude, Fireflies, etc. and make it real. The work is configuration and curation.
Turn messy material into useful artifacts: Transcripts, email threads, scattered documents — read them, find the structure, produce a summary, decision log, brief, or procedure.
Steward the knowledge base: Keep each client's institutional memory layer organized, current, and trustworthy. Catch staleness, gaps, and drift before they compound.
Monitor quality: Review AI-assisted outputs on a regular cadence. Fix wrong-voiced or thin output and feed corrections back into the templates.
Document the system: Write the procedures and reference materials that let a client's team operate on their own.
Onboard new clients: Stand up tooling, ingest context, build initial structures, get routines running. Each onboarding makes the next one faster.
You're wired for correctness: You also can't comfortably walk away from something that's half-finished or quietly broken. You notice when an output is subtly wrong, thin, or off-voice.
You learn by doing: You don't wait for perfect documentation; you take a stab, and when you get stuck you come back with a precise account of what you don't understand.
You teach yourself the frontier: You already poke at new tools, features, and techniques on your own time to see how far you can push them because learning is fun for you.

You want to own outcomes: You're the person who can see the fix and is frustrated by not having the authority to deliver. You want the accountability — not the title, the responsibility — and you understand that owning something means defining what success looks like and being measured against it.

You can understand what people actually need: Requests arrive messy and underspecified. You're good at translating "the client said X" into "here's what actually needs to happen," and triaging across tools and teams to make it real.
You do not need a finance background or a degree: You do need to be smart, curious, easy to work with, and genuinely interested in the problem.
Evals and traces — how we can measure whether an AI system is actually doing its job, and how we follow what it did, step by step.
GraphRAG / Hybrid RAG — how systems retrieve the right memory and context.
Auto-research loops — e.g. Andrej Karpathy's framing, or "Codex goals" and "Claude dreaming" as different takes on the same core idea.
You don't need to understand how it works. The bar is simpler and harder: can you grasp the value, and picture how you'd operate and wield these in practice?
description:
About the job

About Our Client:

Our Client builds institutional memory for investment firms and family offices. The judgment, decisions, and conversations that make a firm what it is mostly live in people's heads — and disappear when those people move on. We change that, working on top of the tools each firm already uses to turn how they operate into something the team can use, allocators can see, and the next generation can inherit.




The Role

This is a frontline operations role for an AI-native consulting practice. The founder owns each client engagement and sets the architecture. You keep the systems running and the clients well-served.

As AI lets very small teams deliver work that used to require very large ones, someone has to make sure the prompts, knowledge bases, capture routines, and recurring deliverables behind that actually work as intended.




What you'll do:

Frontline support: Be the first point of contact for client teams. Take in requests, respond, escalate to the founder when needed.
Diagnose the problem: Most requests won't come with clear specs. Figure out what the client actually needs and what the response should look like.
Update the tooling: Once you know the right response, go into Notion, Linear, Slack, Google Workspace, Claude, Fireflies, etc. and make it real. The work is configuration and curation.
Turn messy material into useful artifacts: Transcripts, email threads, scattered documents — read them, find the structure, produce a summary, decision log, brief, or procedure.
Steward the knowledge base: Keep each client's institutional memory layer organized, current, and trustworthy. Catch staleness, gaps, and drift before they compound.
Monitor quality: Review AI-assisted outputs on a regular cadence. Fix wrong-voiced or thin output and feed corrections back into the templates.
Document the system: Write the procedures and reference materials that let a client's team operate on their own.
Onboard new clients: Stand up tooling, ingest context, build initial structures, get routines running. Each onboarding makes the next one faster.




Who you are: 

AI operations didn't exist as a job a couple of years ago so we care more about how you're wired than what's on your résumé.

You're wired for correctness: You also can't comfortably walk away from something that's half-finished or quietly broken. You notice when an output is subtly wrong, thin, or off-voice.
You learn by doing: You don't wait for perfect documentation; you take a stab, and when you get stuck you come back with a precise account of what you don't understand.
You teach yourself the frontier: You already poke at new tools, features, and techniques on your own time to see how far you can push them because learning is fun for you.
You want to own outcomes: You're the person who can see the fix and is frustrated by not having the authority to deliver. You want the accountability — not the title, the responsibility — and you understand that owning something means defining what success looks like and being measured against it.
You can understand what people actually need: Requests arrive messy and underspecified. You're good at translating "the client said X" into "here's what actually needs to happen," and triaging across tools and teams to make it real.
You do not need a finance background or a degree: You do need to be smart, curious, easy to work with, and genuinely interested in the problem.




A Litmus Test (Read Before you apply):

This role is about operating and wielding advanced AI systems — not building them. You will not be asked to code or to derive any math. But you do need enough conceptual fluency to know what these systems are doing and when they're going wrong.

Before you apply, spend an afternoon — call it four hours — getting the gist of three things (YouTube is fine):




Evals and traces — how we can measure whether an AI system is actually doing its job, and how we follow what it did, step by step.
GraphRAG / Hybrid RAG — how systems retrieve the right memory and context.
Auto-research loops — e.g. Andrej Karpathy's framing, or "Codex goals" and "Claude dreaming" as different takes on the same core idea.
You don't need to understand how it works. The bar is simpler and harder: can you grasp the value, and picture how you'd operate and wield these in practice?




If, after an afternoon, you can honestly say, "I don’t know the inner workings, but I understand its purpose and how to use it," you’ll do great here. If it still feels unclear, a genuine effort, this might not be the right fit and that’s completely okay.

description:
About the job

JOB MISSION

Are you ready to make a meaningful impact? We’re looking for passionate individuals eager to help grow our Hair Care business while upholding our core values of beauty, ethics, and sustainability.

As our Marketing & Commercial Strategy Manager, you’ll be executing and leading the marketing of the Davines brand in Mexico. You will handle requests and opportunities to market and build the brand image, and will be in charge of developing trade promotion plans and materials in partnership with the sales team, implementing and monitoring a comprehensive commercial strategy that attracts new customers and boosts awareness to increase sales in the most innovative and profitable way.

Your efforts will directly contribute to market growth while upholding the core values that define Davines.

What You Will Do


 Work in conjunction with the headquarters Commercial Strategy & Planning department to ensure strategies and tactics are delivered effectively.
 Define and coordinate the product launch strategy, the Marketing Plan and the initiatives from the initial launch strategy and its respective forecast to the price strategy, the promotional execution in salons, in order to achieve brand visibility, high level of sales and a substantial ranking in salons within the market.
 Analyze sales, customer and stock reports, identifying objectives according to sales and market analysis, sharing substantial and relevant information to the sales department and Director.
 Actively support brand positioning through a deep understanding of target consumers, industry trends, and competitive brands.
 Get the most out of our marketing channels to impact the customer journey and influence purchasing decisions (consumer insights, consumer touchpoints, consumer journey).
 Explore market trends and competitor strategies to gain competitive advantage.
 Manage and organize events in close relationship with the educational team.
 Visit clients to understand their needs and business order, creating custom programs, materials, and tools, and supporting customers as needed with sales managers' approval.
 Carry out refill of Merchandising and products in conjunction with the Management.
 Canvas organization for Direct channel and Distributors, as well as local events, launches and annual meeting of Distributors.
 Creation of Direct Sales Promotions and Distributors (entry into X3 + POspace)
 Maintenance of commercial tools (launches, products, merchandising, digital and printed material)
 Loyalty Program creation and monitoring
 Marketing/Launches/Campaigns/Presentations Translations
 Annual price increase list + entry into the system and price entry of new products in X3
 Quarterly Distributors Report Analysis + Pospace entry



Who You Are


 Have completed Higher Education Studies in marketing, sales or related.
 English language proficiency is essential.
 Have proven experience and strong working knowledge in event management, marketing or trade in the professional beauty industry.
 Have a business vision and understanding of trade marketing in the Mexican market.
 Be up to date and strategically apply the latest industry trends.
 Being able to manage a variety of simultaneous activities in a dynamic environment, working with deadlines and in multi-tasking / project management.
 Have strong interpersonal communication skills to build strong relationships with clients, trainers, colleagues and the Davines community in general.
 Domain of the Office package, mainly Excel and Power Point.
 Excellent proactive interpersonal, written and verbal communication skills.



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING WITH US

At Davines Group, we may not be perfect, but we are dedicated to creating a supportive and inspiring environment where we grow together. We believe in the power of beauty—both in the products we create and in the way we treat the planet and people. Sustainability is at the heart of everything we do, and when you join our team, you’ll become part of a company that makes decisions with the future in mind. We value individuals who are passionate about purposeful innovation and are eager to contribute to a positive impact on the world.

OUR COMMITMENT TO EQUITY, DIVERSITY, INCLUSION, BELONGING, WELLBEING AND CARE

At Davines Group, we believe that beauty begins with people. We are committed to fostering an environment rooted in equity, diversity, inclusion, belonging, wellbeing, and care, where everyone can be fully themselves and feel seen and valued.

This commitment is embedded in how we hire, collaborate, and make decisions, as we continuously evolve our practices to ensure fair access to opportunities, reduce bias, and create conditions where everyone can thrive. This commitment reflects our ambition to do our best, with the awareness that this path is a never-ending journey of continuous learning.

Learn more about our full JEDI (Justice, Equity, Diversity, Inclusion) Commitment here https://davinesgroup.com/en/our-impact/le-nostre-policy

EQUAL EMPLOYER OPPORTUNITY

At Davines Group, we celebrate diversity and inclusiveness as core values that are integral to our Ethical Chart. We are committed to creating a workplace where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered to be their authentic selves. Our hiring practices are free from discrimination, and we do not make employment decisions based on race, religion, gender (including pregnancy and gender identity), national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, age, parental status, or any other factor unrelated to merit. We believe that a diverse team strengthens our ability to innovate and thrive.

description:
About the job

Job Description:

🚀 ¡Únete a Nuestro Equipo como Ejecutivo/a de Ventas Internacional Junior!

¿Te apasionan los negocios globales y la industria aeroespacial? ¿Te consideras una persona con gran capacidad de análisis y ganas de ir más allá? Buscamos un talento proactivo y con visión internacional para ser una pieza clave en nuestro crecimiento en la venta de helicópteros y servicios de soporte técnico.

¿Cuál será tu Misión Principal? Serás el motor de seguimiento y reflexión en el proceso de ventas internacionales para la división de Airbus Helicopters Mexico. Tu enfoque estará en garantizar una comunicación fluida y en la gestión integral de las oportunidades comerciales (tanto de aeronaves como de servicios de mantenimiento y soporte), aportando tu capacidad analítica para garantizar cierres exitosos junto al equipo directivo.

🎯 Tus Desafíos y Responsabilidades


Aceleración de Oportunidades: Dar seguimiento oportuno a la venta de helicópteros y paquetes de servicios de soporte, documentando con precisión cada avance.
Análisis Estratégico: Colaborar en el análisis técnico para evaluar la viabilidad de las ofertas comerciales y proponer soluciones a la medida del cliente.
Investigación de Mercados: Identificar y explorar nuevas oportunidades en mercados internacionales dentro del sector aeronáutico.
Desarrollo de Negocios: Crear y mantener relaciones sólidas con clientes nuevos y existentes, promoviendo nuestra excelencia en servicios postventa.
Gestión Comercial: Organizar visitas y representar a la empresa en entornos de alta exigencia.
Soporte de Cierre: Apoyar directamente al Jefe de Ventas en la documentación técnica, presentaciones y negociación final.


📝 Requisitos:


Experiencia: Máximo 1 año en ventas (servicios, industriales o cualquier sector). Valoramos más tus habilidades blandas y tu potencial.
Idiomas Indispensables: * Español: Nivel Avanzado/Nativo (Excelente redacción y comunicación oral).
Inglés: Nivel Conversacional (B2) para interacción internacional.
Herramientas Digitales: * Conocimiento deseable de Salesforce o manejo de algún CRM.

 ¿Este es el Desafío que Estás Buscando?

Si cumples con el perfil, te motiva ir más allá de lo esperado y quieres impactar nuestro crecimiento global, ¡queremos conocerte!

En AIRBUS promovemos la igualdad laboral y la no discriminación. Todas nuestras vacantes están abiertas a personas sin distinción de origen étnico o nacional, género, edad, discapacidad, condición social, condiciones de salud, religión, opiniones, preferencias sexuales, estado civil o cualquier otra condición protegida por la ley. We are looking for proactive talent with an international vision to become a key player in our growth within helicopter sales and technical support services.

If you fit this profile, are motivated to go beyond expectations, and want to impact our global growth, we want to meet you!

description:
About the job

Community Manager Role at Remotasks

Job Description:

We are seeking an experienced Community Manager to oversee and engage our online community in the tech operations sector. The ideal candidate will have a passion for technology, a knack for communication, and an eye for design. This role involves managing our online presence, engaging with community members, and collaborating with our tech team to ensure our community is vibrant, informative, and supportive.

Key Responsibilities:


Develop and implement community engagement strategies to foster a positive and active community atmosphere.
Monitor, moderate, and respond to discussions within the community, ensuring a respectful and constructive tone.
Collaborate with the tech operations team to provide community feedback and insights.
Design and create engaging multimedia content (images, videos, infographics) for social media platforms and community forums.
Organize and manage online events, webinars, and Q&A sessions to engage and educate the community.
Analyze community engagement metrics and provide reports on community health and growth.
Stay updated with the latest trends in community management and tech operations.

Strong proficiency in design software such as Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, and other relevant tools.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Experience in managing social media platforms and online forums.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and manage multiple projects simultaneously.

Base pay is determined by multiple factors, including the area of expertise, years of experience, and skillset needed for the project. Pay rates vary during the project lifecycle. For example, rates for onboarding are lower.

description:
About the job

Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

As Renewals Specialist you will manage a focused portfolio of customer accounts to ensure the renewal of support contracts. You will create high renewal rates by engaging customers and channel partners, communicating contract value, and removing friction from the renewal process.

As Renewals Specialist you will work through remote engagement (telephone, email, Gainsight, and CRM workflows) and partners with Customer Success, Sales, Channel, and Operations and Support teams. You will use Salesforce data and processes to manage renewals, forecast risk, and support we are recurring revenue and retention goals for North America–based customers

Your Responsibilities


You will manage a defined portfolio of accounts with a primary focus on renewing support contracts on time and in full.
You will engage customers and channel partners ahead of contract expiration to create seamless renewal outcomes. Sending notifications of renewal expiration to customers.
You will communicate contract coverage, renewal options, timelines, and the value of continued support services.
You will ensure renewals, working toward renewal rate, churn, and forecasting Indicators set by leadership.
You will use Salesforce to manage renewal opportunities, track progress, maintain accurate contract data, receive and send customer quotes, and support forecasting activities.
You will partner with Partner Success Managers to align renewal strategies with channel partners.
You will collaborate with Sales, Channel teams, Contracts, and Operations on pricing, terms, documentation, and renewal execution.
You will provide first-line support for renewal-related inquiries, including contract scope, pricing questions, and renewal logistics.
You will identify renewal risk early and escalate to ensure leadership visibility and proactive mitigation.
You will follow established Salesforce and renewal processes to ensure data quality, compliance, and operational efficiency.
You will support our revenue retention and growth strategy by reducing renewal friction and improving the customer experience.


The Essentials - You Will Have


Bachelor's degree.


The Preferred - You Might Also Have


Minimum 1–2 years of experience in Renewals, Customer Success, Inside Sales, Sales Operations, or Contract Management (preferred).
Experience working with CRM tools such as Salesforce for opportunity management, forecasting, and reporting.
Experience supporting North America–based customers.


What We Offer

Our benefits package includes …


Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm
Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees.
Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.
Employee Assistance Program
Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program
On-demand digital course library for professional development... and other local benefits!


Rockwell Automation’s hybrid policy aligns that employees are expected to work at a Rockwell location at least Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays unless they have a business obligation out of the office.

3+ years working in a procurement department (preferred)
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a focus on customer service

description:
About the job

We are a leading global software company dedicated to the world of computer aided design, 3D modeling and simulation— helping innovative global manufacturers design better products, faster! With the resources of a large company, and the energy of a software start-up, we have fun together while creating a world class software portfolio. Our culture encourages creativity, welcomes fresh thinking, and focuses on growth, so our people, our business, and our customers can achieve their full potential.

This role reports directly to the Director of Strategic Sourcing and will work in a team environment that provides support and guidance to ensure that Strategic Procurement is effectively serving its internal stakeholders.

Position Overview

The Procurement Buyer will be directly accountable for overall management of contracts for the assigned procurement category. This is an opportunity for a highly motivated person with professional experience in procurement capable of both hands-on individual contribution and thought leadership. This person will work independently and cross functionally with the various teams to provide support and accountability throughout the P2P lifecycle.

You Will:


Manage assigned categories for all procurement functions including category strategic/tactical tasks
Perform RFP/negotiations, contract negotiations and supplier management
Complete annual cost savings initiatives and projects for the assigned Categories
Proactively monitors vendor performance and strategic agreements in order to achieve cost savings targets
Collaborate with Finance, Accounting, Accounts Payable, Legal, Sourcing, Security & Risk Teams to ensure compliance with policies and procedures.
Be an Ambassador for the Procurement Team


Qualifications


3+ years working in a procurement department (preferred)
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a focus on customer service
Proficiency in MS Office Suite (i.e. Outlook, Excel, Word), Adobe
BS/BA degree, or equivalent combination of education and related work experience
Degree in Supply Chain Management or Business (is a plus)


Why Us?

At Siemens Software, flexibility is how we work—hybrid by default, built on trust and autonomy. Together, 30,000 people across more than 200 countries build technology that shapes the real world. You'll grow through real projects, strong technical peers, and global mobility, backed by the scale and benefits of an industrial software leader. We're committed to equality and inclusion, and we hire based on merit, skills, and impact. Bring your curiosity and creativity and help us shape tomorrow!

Compensation & Benefits

The salary range for this position is 498,600 pesos to 1,096,900 pesos and this role is eligible to earn incentive compensation. The actual compensation offered is based on the successful candidate's job-related skills, experience, and relevant education/training. Siemens offers health and wellness benefits to employees; you can access the benefits available in your country via the link: https://jobs.sw.siemens.com/benefits/

Our Commitment to Equity and Inclusion in our Diverse Global Workforce

We value your unique identity and perspective. We are fully committed to providing equitable opportunities and building a workplace that reflects the diversity of society, while ensuring that we attract the best talent based on qualifications, skills, and experiences. We welcome you to bring your authentic self and transform the everyday with us.

Siemens Software. Transform the Everyday
