title: Sales Development Representative
description:
About the job

Remote Automotive Sales Development Representative




Please watch this 2-minute video first:

https://www.loom.com/share/699b59fd8ebc427583977b751453994b




Auto City | California-Based Automotive Dealership

https://www.goautocity.com/about-us

Compensation: $8 USD per hour, based on a full-time schedule of 40 hours per week. This equals approximately $1,385 USD per month, with the opportunity to earn up to an additional $500 USD per month in performance-based commissions.




Contract Type: Independent contractor (you will be responsible for your own taxes and benefits).




Equipment: You need to provide your own computer equipment and internet.

Auto City is a used-car dealership based in El Cajon, California, focused on making the car-buying process simple, transparent, and no-pressure. The dealership emphasizes up-front pricing, vehicle-history transparency, quality inventory, helpful financing options, and a relaxed customer experience where shoppers can move at their own pace.

About the Role

We are looking for a remote Remote Automotive Sales Development Representative to support a San Diego-based automotive dealership. This person will make outbound calls, follow up with leads, schedule appointments, and help convert interested customers into dealership visits.

Working Hours

9:00 AM–5:00 PM U.S. Pacific Time.

CDMX / Costa Rica: 10:00 AM–6:00 PM | Colombia: 11:00 AM–7:00 PM | Argentina / Brazil: 1:00 PM–9:00 PM

Key Responsibilities

• Make outbound calls to internet leads, past customers, and follow-up prospects.

• Respond to customers by phone, text, and email.

• Schedule and confirm appointments for test drives or sales consultations.

• Update CRM notes and track follow-up activity accurately.

• Transfer qualified leads to the appropriate salesperson or manager.

• Provide a professional and friendly customer experience.

Requirements

• Advanced English and strong phone communication skills.

• Experience in outbound calling, appointment setting, customer service, sales support, or call center work.

• Comfortable making a high volume of calls.

• Organized, reliable, and detail-oriented.

• Reliable internet connection and quiet remote workspace.

Nice to Have

• Automotive dealership or BDC experience.

• Experience with dealership CRMs such as VinSolutions, DealerSocket, Elead, or similar.

Application Process

Interview with Latin Remote Workers

Short Practical take-home exercise

Final interview with the hiring manager

title: Lifecycle Marketing Specialist (LATAM, Remote)
skills: Campaign Operations: Build, QA, and deploy 3-4 weekly email campaigns in Iterable, focusing on audience segmentation, triggered sends, and automated journeys.
Calendar & Project Management: Manage the email content calendar using Jira to ensure all campaigns, briefs, and stakeholder approvals remain on schedule.
Creative Collaboration: Write creative briefs and partner with designers and copywriters to ensure all assets align with email marketing best practices.
Technical Execution: Build and edit templates using Stripo and use basic HTML to troubleshoot formatting issues within Iterable.
Performance Optimization: Conduct A/B testing and pull performance data to report on open rates, CTR, and conversion metrics, providing actionable recommendations for strategy.
Database Health: Maintain strict list hygiene, segmentation accuracy, and deliverability standards while ensuring full privacy compliance.
Process Growth: Assist in building new automated workflows and triggered campaigns from the ground up as the lifecycle program scales.
Experience: 3+ years of hands-on experience in lifecycle, CRM, or email marketing, preferably within a high-growth startup environment.
Platform Expertise: Strong experience with Iterable (preferred) or Braze. Proficiency in HubSpot or Klaviyo will be considered if you have deep automation and journey-building experience.

Data Literacy: Ability to interpret email metrics and translate data into performance-improving strategies.
Communication: Excellent communication skills regarding timelines, blockers, and creative feedback.
Logistics: Ability to work US business hours and maintain high QA standards in a fast-paced environment.
You have direct experience in healthcare, wellness, or membership-based brands.
You are an expert-level user of Stripo and Iterable.
You have familiarity with Power BI, SQL, or advanced reporting workflows.
You have experience providing creative feedback based on engagement data.

Fully Remote: 100% Remote work environment, never fight traffic again

$25 monthly perfect attendance bonus
Performance bonus
Anniversary bonus ($100 at 1 year; $200 at 2 years)
Earn a $300 referral bonus for each candidate you refer who is successfully hired, and $500 when you help us identify and close a new opportunity with your client

Step 2 Recruiter Interview: If we believe we have a great client match, we’ll reach out to schedule a human-to-human chat to discuss your career goals, experience, and what makes you tick.
Step 3 Online Assessment: This is your chance to shine! You’ll complete a role-specific test, a personality assessment, and your official GA Profile Form so we can represent you.

Step 4 Match Interview: You’ll meet with one of our Senior Leaders for a deep-dive validation to ensure we’re matching you with the right opportunity. This is your moment to bring your A-game and really shine - it’s the final step for many of our roles!
Step 5 15-min Prep Call: We’ve got your back. Before the final round, your Recruiter will hop on a quick call to coach you, share insider tips, and ensure you’re 100% ready.

Step 6 Client Interview: You’ll meet the client to discuss how you’ll help them scale and succeed. Bring your metrics and be on your A-game - this is your last chance to make a great first impression!
description:
About the job

Who We Are

GrowthAssistant connects top marketing and operations talent with fast-growing companies that depend on great execution to scale. Founded in 2021 and named by Forbes as one of the best startups to work at, we're a permanently remote team that runs on integrity, proactive candor, and a bias toward action — and we prioritize the human experience in everything we do. We hire for skill and for heart, and we invest in our people for the long haul.




The Opportunity

We are looking for a detail-oriented Lifecycle Marketing Specialist to join a high-growth brand and take ownership of end-to-end email campaign operations. In this role, you won't just be executing tasks; you’ll be a key driver of the lifecycle process—managing the content calendar, drafting creative briefs, and collaborating with brand and design teams to bring campaigns to life.




Operating primarily within Iterable, you will build, QA, and deploy sophisticated automated journeys and weekly campaigns designed to drive lead conversion, member engagement, and reactivation. If you are a proactive marketer who balances technical execution with strong project management and a data-driven mindset, we want to meet you.




What Your Day-to-Day Will Look Like

Campaign Operations: Build, QA, and deploy 3-4 weekly email campaigns in Iterable, focusing on audience segmentation, triggered sends, and automated journeys.
Calendar & Project Management: Manage the email content calendar using Jira to ensure all campaigns, briefs, and stakeholder approvals remain on schedule.
Creative Collaboration: Write creative briefs and partner with designers and copywriters to ensure all assets align with email marketing best practices.
Technical Execution: Build and edit templates using Stripo and use basic HTML to troubleshoot formatting issues within Iterable.
Performance Optimization: Conduct A/B testing and pull performance data to report on open rates, CTR, and conversion metrics, providing actionable recommendations for strategy.
Database Health: Maintain strict list hygiene, segmentation accuracy, and deliverability standards while ensuring full privacy compliance.
Process Growth: Assist in building new automated workflows and triggered campaigns from the ground up as the lifecycle program scales.




What You Bring

Experience: 3+ years of hands-on experience in lifecycle, CRM, or email marketing, preferably within a high-growth startup environment.
Platform Expertise: Strong experience with Iterable (preferred) or Braze. Proficiency in HubSpot or Klaviyo will be considered if you have deep automation and journey-building experience.
Strategic Mindset: A deep understanding of segmentation logic, customer lifecycle stages, and triggered campaign operations.
Content Coordination: Proven ability to manage creative briefs and coordinate timelines across internal brand and creative teams.
Technical Skills: Experience with email builders (Stripo, Taxi, or Stensul) and basic HTML for template editing.
Data Literacy: Ability to interpret email metrics and translate data into performance-improving strategies.
Communication: Excellent communication skills regarding timelines, blockers, and creative feedback.
Logistics: Ability to work US business hours and maintain high QA standards in a fast-paced environment.




Bonus Points if:

You have direct experience in healthcare, wellness, or membership-based brands.
You are an expert-level user of Stripo and Iterable.
You have familiarity with Power BI, SQL, or advanced reporting workflows.
You have experience providing creative feedback based on engagement data.
You have exposure to SMS or omnichannel strategy (a future need for this role).




What We Offer

At GrowthAssistant, you aren't just joining a company; you're joining a movement to redefine how the world’s best brands scale. We believe that the right talent, placed in the right role, changes everything - and that starts with how we take care of you.




We support your life inside and outside of work with a comprehensive benefits package:

Fully Remote: 100% Remote work environment, never fight traffic again
Health Coverage: HMO or Healthcare Stipend (depending on location) after 90 days of service
Time to Recharge: 7 Paid Holidays and up to 6 PTO days per year
Family First: Parental leave after 1 year of service




We offer additional incentives for go-getters and proven performers!

$25 monthly perfect attendance bonus
Performance bonus
Anniversary bonus ($100 at 1 year; $200 at 2 years)
Earn a $300 referral bonus for each candidate you refer who is successfully hired, and $500 when you help us identify and close a new opportunity with your client




Your Application Roadmap

You’ve applied, now let’s get to work. Here’s the play-by-play:

Step 1 Resume Screening: Our hiring team reviews your experience to see how your unique skills align with our high-growth roles.
Step 2 Recruiter Interview: If we believe we have a great client match, we’ll reach out to schedule a human-to-human chat to discuss your career goals, experience, and what makes you tick.
Step 3 Online Assessment: This is your chance to shine! You’ll complete a role-specific test, a personality assessment, and your official GA Profile Form so we can represent you.
Step 4 Match Interview: You’ll meet with one of our Senior Leaders for a deep-dive validation to ensure we’re matching you with the right opportunity. This is your moment to bring your A-game and really shine - it’s the final step for many of our roles!




Depending on the client, there are sometimes 2 additional steps:

Step 5 15-min Prep Call: We’ve got your back. Before the final round, your Recruiter will hop on a quick call to coach you, share insider tips, and ensure you’re 100% ready.
Step 6 Client Interview: You’ll meet the client to discuss how you’ll help them scale and succeed. Bring your metrics and be on your A-game - this is your last chance to make a great first impression!




Join our 100% remote global team today, and grow with GrowthAssistant!




GrowthAssistant is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All employment decisions are based on qualifications, merit, and business need.

title: Intern, Marketing, Mexico, 2026
skills: Apoyar el desarrollo y la ejecución de campañas de marketing alineadas con la estrategia de la marca.
Colaborar en la preparación de presentaciones, informes y materiales para reuniones internas y revisiones con la dirección.

Apoyar las actividades digitales y de redes sociales mediante la recopilación y análisis de métricas, el monitoreo del desempeño y la propuesta de mejoras.
Brindar apoyo en la logística y planificación de eventos, reuniones y actividades educativas.
Proporcionar soporte administrativo general al equipo de marca y garantizar el cumplimiento de los procesos internos de la empresa.
Ser estudiante activo de Mercadotecnia, Administración de Empresas, Comunicación o una carrera afín.
Contar con al menos un año restante para graduarse al inicio de las prácticas profesionales.
Dominio del español y nivel avanzado de inglés, tanto oral como escrito.
Manejo de Microsoft Office, especialmente PowerPoint y Word, así como conocimientos prácticos de Excel.
Disponibilidad para trabajar de 4 a 6 horas diarias, de lunes a viernes, y compromiso para realizar prácticas profesionales durante 12 meses.
Estar inscrito en una universidad en México durante toda la duración del programa de prácticas profesionales.
Experiencia previa en prácticas profesionales o trabajo en áreas de marketing, atención médica o ciencias de la vida.
Familiaridad con herramientas de analítica digital, como Google Analytics o plataformas de métricas para redes sociales.
Experiencia en el uso de herramientas de colaboración, como Teams, Zoom o Webex.
Conocimientos avanzados de Excel, incluyendo el uso de tablas de datos básicas y la creación de gráficos.
Facilidad para colaborar con agencias creativas y proveedores externos.
Interés en el marketing del sector salud y motivación por generar un impacto positivo.
Remuneración económica
Vales de comida
Equipo abierto y amigable
Aprendizaje constante
description:
About the job

Ayúdanos a adelantarnos a las enfermedades junto con nuestra Área de Marketing.

Formación requerida: Actualmente cursando carreras de Marketing, Administración de Empresas, Comunicación o afines.

Mínimo 1 año para terminar.

Otros requisitos: Debe estar matriculado/a en una universidad de México durante la pasantía.

Requisitos de idioma: Inglés avanzado

Fecha de inicio prevista: Agosto 2026

Periodo de prácticas: 1 año

Modelo híbrido: 2 días onsite

Fecha límite de solicitud: Recomendamos postular lo antes posible. Cerraremos esta vacante cuando tengamos suficientes solicitudes, así que aplica cuanto antes para que tu candidatura pueda ser considerada.

¿Buscas adquirir una valiosa experiencia laboral a nivel mundial y ayudar a impactar positivamente en la salud de miles de millones de personas? ¡Solicita hoy una práctica en GSK!

Una práctica en GSK te ofrece la oportunidad de impulsar tu carrera: ¡asumirás un puesto real con un impacto genuino! Realizarás un trabajo exigente y significativo en proyectos o asignaciones reales. También aprenderás con nosotros, desarrollarás tus habilidades y adquirirás una experiencia valiosa para dondequiera que te lleve el futuro.

¿Qué harás? 


Apoyar el desarrollo y la ejecución de campañas de marketing alineadas con la estrategia de la marca.
Colaborar en la preparación de presentaciones, informes y materiales para reuniones internas y revisiones con la dirección.
Coordinar los cronogramas de los proyectos, dar seguimiento con agencias y equipos internos, y supervisar el cumplimiento de los entregables.
Apoyar las actividades digitales y de redes sociales mediante la recopilación y análisis de métricas, el monitoreo del desempeño y la propuesta de mejoras.
Brindar apoyo en la logística y planificación de eventos, reuniones y actividades educativas.
Proporcionar soporte administrativo general al equipo de marca y garantizar el cumplimiento de los procesos internos de la empresa.


¿Qué estamos buscando?


Ser estudiante activo de Mercadotecnia, Administración de Empresas, Comunicación o una carrera afín.
Contar con al menos un año restante para graduarse al inicio de las prácticas profesionales.
Dominio del español y nivel avanzado de inglés, tanto oral como escrito.
Manejo de Microsoft Office, especialmente PowerPoint y Word, así como conocimientos prácticos de Excel.
Disponibilidad para trabajar de 4 a 6 horas diarias, de lunes a viernes, y compromiso para realizar prácticas profesionales durante 12 meses.
Estar inscrito en una universidad en México durante toda la duración del programa de prácticas profesionales.


Deseables

Si cuentas con las siguientes características, será un valor agregado:


Experiencia previa en prácticas profesionales o trabajo en áreas de marketing, atención médica o ciencias de la vida.
Familiaridad con herramientas de analítica digital, como Google Analytics o plataformas de métricas para redes sociales.
Experiencia en el uso de herramientas de colaboración, como Teams, Zoom o Webex.
Conocimientos avanzados de Excel, incluyendo el uso de tablas de datos básicas y la creación de gráficos.
Facilidad para colaborar con agencias creativas y proveedores externos.
Interés en el marketing del sector salud y motivación por generar un impacto positivo.


¿Qué aprendrás?

Te incorporarás al equipo de Marketing en México como practicante, brindando apoyo en actividades de marketing relacionadas con vacunas. Colaborarás en la ejecución de campañas, el desarrollo de contenidos y la coordinación de proyectos. Trabajarás de cerca con gerentes de producto, agencias y equipos multidisciplinarios.

¿Qué te ofrecemos? 


Remuneración económica
Vales de comida
Equipo abierto y amigable
Aprendizaje constante


¡Hagámoslo realidad!

Encontrarás consejos, sugerencias y orientación sobre nuestro proceso de selección en nuestro sitio web.

Obtén más información sobre el proceso de solicitud en gsk.to/ECprocess (opcional).

Puedes aprender más sobre GSK y nuestras carreras aquí: https://www.gsk.com/en-gb/careers/

¡Postúlate ahora!

¿Necesitas ayuda con tu solicitud?

Por favor escríbenos a AM.EarlyTalentRecruitment@gsk.com y dinos cómo podemos ayudarte.

#EarlyCareers

Why GSK?

Uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together.

GSK is a global biopharma company with a purpose to unite science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease together. We aim to positively impact the health of 2.5 billion people by the end of the decade, as a successful, growing company where people can thrive. We get ahead of disease by preventing and treating it with innovation in specialty medicines and vaccines. We focus on four therapeutic areas: respiratory, immunology and inflammation; oncology; HIV; and infectious diseases – to impact health at scale.

People and patients around the world count on the medicines and vaccines we make, so we’re committed to creating an environment where our people can thrive and focus on what matters most. Our culture of being ambitious for patients, accountable for impact and doing the right thing is the foundation for how, together, we deliver for patients, shareholders and our people.

If you require any type of accommodation and/or support during the selection process to better showcase your abilities (for example, access to ramps, accessible emergency exits, visual aids, etc.), please let us know when we contact you. We are here to support you.

Your application is voluntary; therefore, you understand and consent that GSK, located at Real Mayorazgo 130, Torre M, Piso 20, Col. Xoco, Benito Juárez, Mexico City, will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy in accordance with the candidate privacy notice, which can be consulted at Candidate Privacy Notice. Among the information you provide for this purpose, you may choose to give us personal data that will help ensure an inclusive selection process. Rest assured that your information will be treated with the utmost confidentiality, in compliance with applicable regulations, and will not be available to hiring managers or anyone outside the recruitment team. Help us maintain, together, an inclusive and welcoming environment. By submitting information, I confirm that I agree that it will be processed in accordance with the stated privacy notice.

Important notice to Employment businesses/ Agencies

GSK does not accept referrals from employment businesses and/or employment agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site. All employment businesses/agencies are required to contact GSK's commercial and general procurement/human resources department to obtain prior written authorization before referring any candidates to GSK. The obtaining of prior written authorization is a condition precedent to any agreement (verbal or written) between the employment business/ agency and GSK. In the absence of such written authorization being obtained any actions undertaken by the employment business/agency shall be deemed to have been performed without the consent or contractual agreement of GSK. GSK shall therefore not be liable for any fees arising from such actions or any fees arising from any referrals by employment businesses/agencies in respect of the vacancies posted on this site.

Find more about life at GSK at www.gsk.com

description:
About the job

About Docplanner

At Docplanner Group, we’re on a mission to help people live longer, healthier lives. As the world’s largest healthcare platform, each month, we connect 24 million patients with 280k doctors across 13 countries (through brands like ZnanyLekarz, Doctoralia, MioDottore, DoktorTakvimi, and Jameda).

Our marketplaces, SaaS and AI tools simplify daily tasks and help doctors, clinics and hospitals work more efficiently, so they can focus on what really matters: caring for their patients.

Role overview

The Commercial Insights Analyst supports the Commercial Product Operations Manager by

generating structured insights that help Sales and Customer Success improve acquisition,

retention, and expansion outcomes at Doctoralia México.

This role focuses on transforming commercial data into actionable recommendations. The

analyst works across Sales, Customer Success, Marketing, and Operations teams to analyze

performance trends, identify improvement opportunities, and support data-informed decision making.

The role is expected to progressively move from ad-hoc reporting toward deeper analytical work that explains performance drivers and proposes measurable improvement initiatives.

This is not a Business Intelligence reporting-only role.

Key Responsibilities


 Strategic Analysis & Insights
Analyze commercial performance across the customer lifecycle, focusing on: Definition of ICP, Retention and churn drivers, Customer engagement and usage patterns, Expansion and upsell opportunities, Pricing and packaging performance
Develop structured analyses that move beyond descriptive reporting into explanation of


performance drivers. Translate analytical findings into clear recommendations for Sales and Customer Success leadership.


 Commercial Performance Monitoring
Develop and maintain analytical frameworks that monitor key commercial metrics such as: Sales productivity indicators, Customer acquisition efficiency, Customer retention and churn, Expansion revenue.
Ensure metrics are consistently defined and comparable across time periods. Provide clear narrative explanations of performance trends. Monitor initiative performance and track measurable business impact
 Business Decision Support
Partner with the Commercial Product Operations Manager and commercial leadership to support strategic projects with structured analysis, quantify potential impact of proposed initiatives, validate hypotheses using commercial data, build business cases for operational improvements


🎯Qualifications


Experience: 1–3 years of experience in analytics, strategy, consulting, or commercial insights roles analyzing business performance in Sales, Customer Success, or revenue-generating environments
ADN Tech: You are highly comfortable working in Startup, Consulting, or Commercial environments.
Tech Stack: Advanced in Excel/Sheets, Power BI, Tableau y Salesforce or equivalent commercial platforms
Soft Skills: Experience translating data analysis into business recommendations, analytical, 
Language: Fluent in English


Value Proposition (Compensation & Benefits)

We offer a high-performance environment with a competitive benefits package:

🏠 Hybrid Work Model

The perfect balance between the energy of the office and the comfort of working from home.

🌴 Real Time Off

22 vacation days starting from year one + your birthday off to celebrate your special day.

🏥 Health First

Comprehensive Major Medical Insurance for your peace of mind.

💰 Financial Benefits & Perks

Savings Fund, Grocery Vouchers, And Additional Monthly Benefits.

💪 Wellbeing Matters

Access to TotalPass & iFeel memberships to support your physical and emotional wellbeing.

🌍 Global Culture

Professional growth opportunities within an international company operating in more than 13 countries.

Are you ready to transform the healthcare industry in Mexico? We’d love to meet you!

description:
About the job

Job Title: SEO & WordPress Specialist
Work Hours: Full-time (40 hours per week)
Schedule/Timezone: Monday to Friday / EST Timezone
Location: 100% Remote, (Latin America Only)




Overview 




Stop chasing algorithms and start driving measurable growth. We are looking for a data-driven SEO expert to take the wheel of a diverse client portfolio within a high-performance digital marketing agency.




Our agency is a results-first digital marketing powerhouse specializing in SEO, web development, and PPC. We pride ourselves on delivering tangible, high-impact results for a large portfolio of international clients. We are built on a culture of continuous improvement, technical excellence, and proactive problem-solving.




As our newest team member, you will step into a fast-paced environment where your expertise in platform-driven optimization and WordPress management will directly influence the success of dozens of businesses.




Key Responsibilities




Overseeing and enhancing SEO performance across a large and diverse range of client accounts
Utilizing Search Atlas to perform weekly SEO audits, monitor rankings, and identify growth opportunities
Updating and maintaining WordPress sites to ensure peak usability and SEO health
Implementing on-page SEO strategies, including metadata, keyword optimization, internal linking, and technical fixes
Analyzing performance metrics and transform complex SEO data into clear, actionable strategy steps
Managing site health, indexing, schema, and emerging technical SEO opportunities
Working closely with internal teams to ensure best practices are applied consistently across all projects




Must-Have Requirements




Proven track record in SEO, preferably within a fast-paced agency setting
Strong hands-on experience with Search Atlas (or comparable advanced SEO platforms) and WordPress
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple client accounts and prioritize tasks effectively
Deep understanding of on-page SEO, technical SEO, keyword tracking, and reporting
Ability to interpret data and turn it into execution
A strong willingness to learn, grow, and take ownership of results




Nice-to-Have Requirements




Tool Proficiency: Familiarity with Ahrefs, SEMrush, Google Search Console, and Google Analytics
Global Context: Experience working directly with U.S.-based clients
Advanced Tech: Knowledge of schema, site speed optimization, and specific technical SEO fixes
Complementary Skills: Basic understanding of PPC or web development




Why Join Us 




This is more than just a back-end role; it is an opportunity to join a team that values transparency, measurable success, and professional evolution. You’ll be working with a large-scale portfolio that keeps your day-to-day work exciting and varied. We offer a remote-first culture that respects your time zone and provides the tools you need to succeed. If you are a detail-oriented specialist who thrives on seeing your optimizations move the needle, we want to talk to you.

description:
About the job

Job Title: Customer Success Operations Coordinator
Schedule: Part-Time (20 hours/week initially), with the potential to expand based on performance and business needs
Location: Remote (Latin America Preferred)




Overview

Enjoy building strong client relationships while keeping operations running smoothly behind the scenes?




Join a growing healthcare cybersecurity company dedicated to helping healthcare organizations strengthen their security posture, protect sensitive data, and navigate today's evolving cybersecurity landscape. As the company continues to expand, they are seeking a Customer Success Operations Coordinator to become the operational backbone of their client success function.




In this role, you'll serve as the primary communication bridge between clients and internal technical teams, ensuring seamless coordination, exceptional client experiences, and long-term relationship success. This opportunity is ideal for a highly organized, detail-oriented professional who enjoys managing multiple stakeholders, communicating with confidence, and keeping projects moving forward. While deep cybersecurity expertise isn't required, an understanding of IT terminology and the ability to communicate technical concepts in client-friendly language will set you up for success.




Key Responsibilities

Organize, schedule, and coordinate Quarterly Business Review (QBR) calls with clients
Ensure meeting participants are prepared and supporting materials are delivered in advance
Serve as the primary point of contact for existing clients, responding to inquiries and coordinating internal follow-up
Facilitate communication between clients and internal technical teams to ensure timely issue resolution
Coordinate crisis communications during security incidents through phone, email, and text
Engage with clients during quarterly reviews to understand their needs and identify opportunities for service expansion and retention
Create, update, and repurpose presentations, pitch decks, and supporting materials for client meetings
Maintain accurate records of client interactions, meeting notes, and follow-up activities
Coordinate communication across internal stakeholders using Microsoft Teams, email, phone, and messaging platforms
Ensure all client communications are professional, timely, and aligned with company standards
Work with the CFO to prepare and send proposals for existing and prospective clients
Maintain accurate client activities, contacts, and records within HubSpot

Must-Have Requirements

3+ years of experience in customer success, operations coordination, account management, executive support, or a similar client-facing role
Exceptional organizational skills with strong attention to detail
Excellent written and verbal English communication skills
Strong client relationship management and communication abilities
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)
Experience with or the ability to quickly learn HubSpot CRM, DotLoop, or similar electronic signature solutions
Experience creating professional presentations, reports, and business documents
Ability to manage multiple priorities while coordinating across teams and stakeholders
Comfortable communicating with clients via phone, email, text, and virtual meetings
Ability to work independently while maintaining accuracy and professionalism
Reliable internet connection and professional remote work environment

Nice-to-Have Requirements

Familiarity with IT terminology and technology-related environments
Experience using HubSpot CRM
Experience using DotLoop or similar electronic signature platforms
Experience working within the healthcare or cybersecurity industries




Why Join Us

This is an opportunity to become a key member of a growing healthcare cybersecurity company where your work will directly contribute to delivering an outstanding client experience. You'll serve as the central connection between clients and internal teams, helping ensure every interaction is organized, responsive, and professional while supporting meaningful, long-term client relationships.




As the company continues to grow, you'll have the opportunity to expand your responsibilities, collaborate closely with leadership, and make a visible impact on customer success operations. If you enjoy building relationships, coordinating moving pieces, and creating exceptional client experiences in a fast-paced environment, this role offers the chance to grow your career while contributing to a mission-driven organization dedicated to protecting healthcare organizations and the communities they serve.

description:
About the job

Customer Relationship Management Specialist




Location: Remote – Latin America

Schedule: Part-Time (20+ hours/week) with strong full-time growth potential




Overview

Are you passionate about building systems, optimizing operations, and making a meaningful impact? This is an opportunity to join a mission-driven nonprofit organization at a pivotal stage of growth.




As the CRM & Operations Manager, you'll lead the transformation of the organization's donor CRM while serving as a strategic operational partner to executive leadership. You'll help build scalable processes, improve data integrity, coordinate key initiatives, and support the launch of a major community-focused agricultural project.




Key Responsibilities

Audit, clean, and optimize donor data across CRM platforms and spreadsheets.
Build automated workflows, segmentation, and reporting to improve donor engagement.
Create dashboards and analytics to track donor retention and campaign performance.
Manage executive calendars, scheduling, and administrative operations.
Coordinate communication across executives, board members, community partners, and project stakeholders.
Support operational planning, project tracking, and AI-powered data collection initiatives.
Prepare donor communications, newsletters, reports, and fundraising materials.
Maintain documentation, meeting notes, and operational workflows as priorities evolve.




Success in this Role

You'll create reliable systems, improve operational efficiency, and become a trusted partner to leadership during a period of rapid organizational growth.




Requirements

3+ years of experience in CRM, operations, project coordination, or similar roles.
Hands-on experience with CRM platforms such as HubSpot, Salesforce, or equivalent.
Advanced Excel and/or Google Sheets skills.
Experience building reports, managing data, and improving workflows.
Fluent English and Spanish, both written and spoken.
Comfortable using AI tools and quickly learning new software.
Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail.
Experience working independently in a remote environment.




What We're Looking For

Highly organized and detail-oriented.
Proactive and self-directed.
Collaborative and transparent in communication.
Adaptable in fast-paced environments.
Accountable with a strong sense of ownership.
Passionate about mission-driven work and community impact.




Nice to Have

Experience working in nonprofits, charities, or mission-driven organizations.
Background in donor relations, fundraising operations, or grant reporting.
Experience supporting executive leadership or Chief of Staff functions.
Familiarity with shared leadership or flat organizational structures.
Experience coordinating multiple stakeholders and cross-functional projects.




Why Join

Help build the operational foundation of a high-impact organization while taking ownership of systems, collaborating directly with leadership, and growing into an increasingly strategic role as the organization scales.

title: Creative Resource Executive
skills: Assist in the development and implementation of workforce planning strategies aligned with both short-term and long-term business goals.
Help the Resource Management team to identify, acquire, assess, and hire freelance candidates to fill open positions within a company.

Helping new freelance employees integrate into the organization.
Process documentation for creator and contractor set-up as well as initiating invoices and payments.
Conduct regular assessments of staffing needs based on current and projected workloads, industry trends, and company growth.
Utilize data analytics and forecasting tools to support staffing predictions and improve resource allocation.
Collaborate with department managers, People team, Integrated Producers and other team members to understand staffing requirements and ensure alignment across teams.
Help facilitate communication across departments to ensure a unified approach to workforce planning.
Evaluate team capacities and suggest areas for improvement to ensure optimal resource utilization.
Work with hiring managers to address skill gaps and explore upskilling opportunities.
Adjusting strategies as needed to meet changing business requirements.
Track and report workforce metrics to leadership, highlighting areas for improvement and talent risk management.
Stay informed on industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in workforce planning.
Continuously assess and improve workforce planning processes to adapt to evolving needs.
Support the global Resource Team in building a well-resourced, effective, and productive global team.
1+ years of experience in workforce planning, capacity planning, or a related role within a digital marketing, advertising, or technology business.
Advanced English proficiency.
Experience using Wrike or similar software for resource and workforce management.
Proficiency in data analysis and forecasting tools.
Strong ability to collaborate across teams and departments to optimize resources.

Able to take direction and follow guidance given from other team members and the leadership team.
description:
About the job

At Jellyfish we believe that our individual differences are our greatest assets. We are passionate about empowering every employee to reach their full potential and contribute their unique perspectives to our collaborative environment. We are an equal opportunity employer and firmly believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce drives innovation and leads to better solutions. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other personal characteristics that make them who they are.

Jellyfish is a global digital marketing agency; a unique fusion of tech enthusiasts, creative minds, and media and data experts all united to empower our clients along their digital journey. Our commitment to embracing diverse perspectives fuels our innovation and strategies that challenge the status quo, reinvent media activation, and craft influential stories for our global clients and their customers. Join us in shaping a future where business growth and personal fulfilment go hand in hand.

Job Description

Reporting into the Senior Resource Director, as a Resource Executive, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the effective utilization of our Creative team to meet business objectives. You will assist in developing workforce planning strategies, forecasting staffing needs, and optimizing resource allocation to improve operational efficiency. Collaborating closely with our capabilities, you will align workforce strategies with company goals, contribute to a positive work environment, and support the overall success of the organization.

Your Primary Responsibilities Include


Assist in the development and implementation of workforce planning strategies aligned with both short-term and long-term business goals.
Help the Resource Management team to identify, acquire, assess, and hire freelance candidates to fill open positions within a company.
Process documentation for freelance set-up to ensure a steam-lined freelance experience.
Helping new freelance employees integrate into the organization.
Process documentation for creator and contractor set-up as well as initiating invoices and payments.
Conduct regular assessments of staffing needs based on current and projected workloads, industry trends, and company growth.
Utilize data analytics and forecasting tools to support staffing predictions and improve resource allocation.
Collaborate with department managers, People team, Integrated Producers and other team members to understand staffing requirements and ensure alignment across teams.
Help facilitate communication across departments to ensure a unified approach to workforce planning.
Evaluate team capacities and suggest areas for improvement to ensure optimal resource utilization.
Work with hiring managers to address skill gaps and explore upskilling opportunities.
Adjusting strategies as needed to meet changing business requirements.
Track and report workforce metrics to leadership, highlighting areas for improvement and talent risk management.
Stay informed on industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in workforce planning.
Continuously assess and improve workforce planning processes to adapt to evolving needs.
Support the global Resource Team in building a well-resourced, effective, and productive global team.


Qualifications


1+ years of experience in workforce planning, capacity planning, or a related role within a digital marketing, advertising, or technology business.
Advanced English proficiency.
Experience using Wrike or similar software for resource and workforce management.
Proficiency in data analysis and forecasting tools.
Strong ability to collaborate across teams and departments to optimize resources.
Familiarity with digital marketing processes and workflows, particularly within creative teams.
Basic understanding of project management principles and practices.
Able to take direction and follow guidance given from other team members and the leadership team.


Note: We emphasise skills, expertise and behavioural attributes over years of experience and traditional degrees. If you want to join our collaborative team, we invite you to apply today with your resume in English.

Additional Information

Join Jellyfish and experience a workplace where we prioritise your growth, celebrate your contributions, and empower you to tailor your work environment to suit your needs.

💰 Savings fund: You'll save 5% of your monthly income and Jellyfish will contribute the same amount you save to your savings fund.

💫 Custom Work Environment: Work remotely for up to 60% of your days and shape your day between 8am. and 6:30pm with flexible working hours.

🏥 Health Insurance: We provide a private health insurance policy that includes visual and dental plan coverage.

Unfortunately, there has been an increase in fake recruiters impersonating Jellyfish and unlawfully using our brand name. If you are unsure if an email with a job offer you have received is genuinely from Jellyfish, or if you suspect any fraudulent activity, please report it to talentacquisition@jellyfish.com.

title: Marketing Manager - Reuters Events
skills: Develop and execute integrated marketing campaigns for Reuters Events conferences, digital content, and related products.
Drive delegate acquisition, audience growth, and lead generation through targeted go-to-market strategies.

Build and manage channel, media, and marketing partnerships to support event promotion and customer acquisition.
Plan and deliver multi-channel campaigns across email, web, content, paid media, partnerships, and social channels.
Monitor, analyse, and optimize campaign performance using tools such as Google Analytics and Salesforce.
Track key marketing KPIs and provide regular reporting and insight to stakeholders.
Collaborate closely with internal teams including sales, content, and event operations to ensure campaign alignment and commercial success.
Support database growth, audience segmentation, and targeted outreach initiatives.
Ensure strong brand positioning and consistent messaging across all marketing activity.
Proven ability to develop and execute B2B marketing campaigns and strategies, ideally within events, media, or professional services.
Strong background in campaign planning, demand generation, and audience development.
Experience delivering integrated marketing campaigns across multiple channels.
Ability to use data and reporting to evaluate campaign performance and drive optimization.
Familiarity with platforms such as Google Analytics, Salesforce and email marketing systems.
Excellent project management and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Commercially aware, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.
Experience marketing to senior executive or C-level audiences.
Experience working with external partners, media partners, or agencies.
Understanding of lead generation and customer acquisition strategies in a B2B environment.

Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.

Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.

Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.

Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.

Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.

Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.

Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.
description:
About the job

Reuters Events is seeking a commercially minded and data-driven Marketing Manager to lead the planning and execution of marketing campaigns across a portfolio of global conferences and content products. Reporting to the Senior Marketing Lead, this role will be responsible for driving audience growth, lead generation, delegate acquisition, and engagement among senior executive communities.

The Marketing Manager will work cross-functionally with product, sales, partnerships, and operations teams to deliver high-impact, multi-channel campaigns that support business growth and strengthen Reuters Events' market presence.

About The Role

As a Marketing Manager within Reuters Events, you will:


Develop and execute integrated marketing campaigns for Reuters Events conferences, digital content, and related products.
Drive delegate acquisition, audience growth, and lead generation through targeted go-to-market strategies.
Identify and implement innovative ways to reach senior executive audiences across key industry sectors.
Build and manage channel, media, and marketing partnerships to support event promotion and customer acquisition.
Plan and deliver multi-channel campaigns across email, web, content, paid media, partnerships, and social channels.
Monitor, analyse, and optimize campaign performance using tools such as Google Analytics and Salesforce.
Track key marketing KPIs and provide regular reporting and insight to stakeholders.
Collaborate closely with internal teams including sales, content, and event operations to ensure campaign alignment and commercial success.
Support database growth, audience segmentation, and targeted outreach initiatives.
Ensure strong brand positioning and consistent messaging across all marketing activity.


About You

To be a Marketing Manager within Reuters Events, you will likely have:


Proven ability to develop and execute B2B marketing campaigns and strategies, ideally within events, media, or professional services.
Strong background in campaign planning, demand generation, and audience development.
Experience delivering integrated marketing campaigns across multiple channels.
Ability to use data and reporting to evaluate campaign performance and drive optimization.
Familiarity with platforms such as Google Analytics, Salesforce and email marketing systems.
Excellent project management and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Commercially aware, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.


Preferred Experience


Experience marketing to senior executive or C-level audiences.
Experience working with external partners, media partners, or agencies.
Understanding of lead generation and customer acquisition strategies in a B2B environment.


What’s in it For You?


Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.
Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.
Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.
Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.


About Us

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.

We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.

As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More information on requesting an accommodation here.

Learn more on how to protect yourself from fraudulent job postings here.

More information about Thomson Reuters can be found on thomsonreuters.com

title: Creative Strategist
skills: A clear, repeatable creative testing system running across your client portfolio with consistent velocity to new concepts, angles, and iterations
Creative insights that translate into measurable performance improvements including higher click-through rates, conversion rates, lower customer acquisition costs, and scalable winning campaigns
Strong client trust in your strategic point of view, demonstrated through proactive communication, data-backed recommendations, and leadership in creative direction
Elevated internal creative standards through contributions to team training, standard operating procedures, and systems that improve output quality and speed
Lead research and insights by mining customer reviews, competitor ads libraries, audience pain points, and product differentiators to identify winning angles and positioning strategies
Own creative testing strategy by building and managing structured testing roadmaps with clear hypotheses, variables to test, iteration plans, and learning agendas

Create world-class creative briefs that transform insights into crisp, actionable direction for designers and video editors including hooks, messaging frameworks, visual concepts, format specifications, and success criteria
Direct performance storytelling by mapping the customer journey from awareness through consideration to purchase and translating it into compelling paid social concepts
Build UGC and production guidance including shot lists, video scripts, creator direction, and content planning that supports performance-oriented execution
Manage creator relationships by sourcing, coordinating, and guiding content creators to produce on-brief, performance-oriented content that aligns with testing objectives
Write high-performing ad copy including primary text, headlines, hooks, and on-screen text that aligns with strategic goals and testing frameworks
Collaborate on conversion rate optimization by partnering with teams on landing page improvements and conversion-focused messaging updates
Track and communicate performance by monitoring key performance indicators by concept and angle, synthesizing learnings, and reporting insights to internal teams and clients
Develop inspiration and competitor boards to inform creative direction and identify market trends and opportunities
Lead copy across paid ads for in-ad copy and headline variations that drive engagement and conversions
1-2 years of experience in ecommerce or performance marketing with strong preference for direct-to-consumer brand experience
Proven track record of paid social creative strategy with expertise in Meta Ads (required) and familiarity with TikTok or YouTube (preferred)
Strong grasp of creative testing methodology including hypothesis development, structured testing frameworks, iteration velocity, and performance analysis
Experience working with design and video teams from brief development through execution to iteration, with ability to provide clear, actionable creative direction
Data-fluent approach with comfort using performance metrics including click-through rates, conversion rates, cost per acquisition, and return on ad spend to make decisions and guide priorities
Deep understanding of customer psychology, marketing funnels, and storytelling principles that drive direct response performance
Excellent copywriting skills across multiple formats including hooks, scripts, ad copy, and landing pages with proven ability to write persuasive, conversion-focused content
Highly organized with strong attention to detail, quality control processes, and ability to manage multiple clients and deadlines simultaneously
Proficiency with Meta Ads Manager, Shopify, Google Analytics, Figma, Motion, and collaboration tools including Slack, Asana, or Notion
Client-facing experience including presenting strategy, leading client calls, and confidently pushing for action based on data and creative insights
Experience with conversion rate optimization and landing page testing frameworks to improve post-click performance
Experience building creative playbooks, training materials, or onboarding systems for creative teams
Familiarity with additional paid social platforms including TikTok Ads, YouTube Ads, or Pinterest Ads
Background in agency environments managing multiple client accounts with varying needs and timelines
Generous Time Off: Competitive PTO policy and paid holidays to maintain work-life balance
Fully Remote: Work from anywhere on US hours and a distributed team culture
Compensation: Competitive salary commensurate with experience.
description:
About the job

About Lilo Social

Lilo Social is a full-funnel eCommerce growth agency specializing in acquisition and retention for direct-to-consumer brands. With a team of over 75 professionals, we deliver best-in-class paid media, email marketing, and creative services to help brands scale profitably. We work across the entire customer lifecycle: from content creation and paid advertising to conversion rate optimization and retention marketing.




Our partnership-first approach has earned us a 90% client retention rate and recognition as an Inc. 5000 honoree for three consecutive years (2023, 2024, 2025). We are proud to be a Klaviyo Master Elite Agency, a distinction held by only 0.2% of Klaviyo's 6,500+ partners. To date, we have driven billions of dollars in attributed revenue for our partners.




The Role

Lilo Social is seeking a Creative Strategist to lead performance-driven creative strategy across a portfolio of direct-to-consumer brands. You will own the end-to-end creative testing engine—from research and messaging frameworks to briefs, storyboards, conversion rate optimization ideas, and ongoing iteration—helping clients consistently find creative winners and scale spend profitably. This is a highly cross-functional role where you will partner closely with design, video, media buying, and account teams while working directly with clients to set creative direction, align on priorities, and build repeatable roadmaps for growth.




What Success Looks Like

A clear, repeatable creative testing system running across your client portfolio with consistent velocity to new concepts, angles, and iterations
Creative insights that translate into measurable performance improvements including higher click-through rates, conversion rates, lower customer acquisition costs, and scalable winning campaigns
Strong client trust in your strategic point of view, demonstrated through proactive communication, data-backed recommendations, and leadership in creative direction
Elevated internal creative standards through contributions to team training, standard operating procedures, and systems that improve output quality and speed




Responsibilities

Lead research and insights by mining customer reviews, competitor ads libraries, audience pain points, and product differentiators to identify winning angles and positioning strategies
Own creative testing strategy by building and managing structured testing roadmaps with clear hypotheses, variables to test, iteration plans, and learning agendas
Create world-class creative briefs that transform insights into crisp, actionable direction for designers and video editors including hooks, messaging frameworks, visual concepts, format specifications, and success criteria
Direct performance storytelling by mapping the customer journey from awareness through consideration to purchase and translating it into compelling paid social concepts
Build UGC and production guidance including shot lists, video scripts, creator direction, and content planning that supports performance-oriented execution
Manage creator relationships by sourcing, coordinating, and guiding content creators to produce on-brief, performance-oriented content that aligns with testing objectives
Write high-performing ad copy including primary text, headlines, hooks, and on-screen text that aligns with strategic goals and testing frameworks
Collaborate on conversion rate optimization by partnering with teams on landing page improvements and conversion-focused messaging updates
Track and communicate performance by monitoring key performance indicators by concept and angle, synthesizing learnings, and reporting insights to internal teams and clients
Develop inspiration and competitor boards to inform creative direction and identify market trends and opportunities
Lead copy across paid ads for in-ad copy and headline variations that drive engagement and conversions




Requirements

1-2 years of experience in ecommerce or performance marketing with strong preference for direct-to-consumer brand experience
Proven track record of paid social creative strategy with expertise in Meta Ads (required) and familiarity with TikTok or YouTube (preferred)
Strong grasp of creative testing methodology including hypothesis development, structured testing frameworks, iteration velocity, and performance analysis
Experience working with design and video teams from brief development through execution to iteration, with ability to provide clear, actionable creative direction
Data-fluent approach with comfort using performance metrics including click-through rates, conversion rates, cost per acquisition, and return on ad spend to make decisions and guide priorities
Deep understanding of customer psychology, marketing funnels, and storytelling principles that drive direct response performance
Excellent copywriting skills across multiple formats including hooks, scripts, ad copy, and landing pages with proven ability to write persuasive, conversion-focused content
Highly organized with strong attention to detail, quality control processes, and ability to manage multiple clients and deadlines simultaneously
Proficiency with Meta Ads Manager, Shopify, Google Analytics, Figma, Motion, and collaboration tools including Slack, Asana, or Notion




Nice to Have

Client-facing experience including presenting strategy, leading client calls, and confidently pushing for action based on data and creative insights
Experience with conversion rate optimization and landing page testing frameworks to improve post-click performance
Experience building creative playbooks, training materials, or onboarding systems for creative teams
Familiarity with additional paid social platforms including TikTok Ads, YouTube Ads, or Pinterest Ads
Background in agency environments managing multiple client accounts with varying needs and timelines




Compensation and Benefits

Generous Time Off: Competitive PTO policy and paid holidays to maintain work-life balance
Fully Remote: Work from anywhere on US hours and a distributed team culture
Compensation: Competitive salary commensurate with experience.

description:
About the job



About Us 



SimplePractice is headquartered in Los Angeles, California, but we have team members who work and live across the United States, Dominican Republic, Mexico City, and Ukraine.



At SimplePractice, we are improving access to quality care by equipping health and wellness clinicians with all the tools they need to thrive in private practice. 



More than 250,000 providers trust SimplePractice to build their business through our industry-leading software with powerful tools that simplify every part of practice management. From admin work to clinical care, our suite of innovative solutions work together to reduce administrative burden—empowering solo and small group practitioners to thrive alongside their clients. 



Award-winning and people-first, SimplePractice is shaping the future of health tech. Recognized by MedTech Breakthrough, the Digital Health Awards, Best Places to Code in Mexico and Built In's Best Places to Work.



 



The Role



SimplePractice is seeking a friendly, eager-to-learn Customer Success Specialist (Product Specialist) to join our Customer Success team in Mexico City. 



SimplePractice is built for our customers, and supporting their success is our top priority. We strive to provide unparalleled support and encourage our customers to always let us know how we can improve our products. 



In this role, you’ll use your passion for collaboration and technology to provide our customers with a world-class experience. You would act as the front line of communication with our customers, serving inbound phone calls as well as assisting customers via chat and email. This role will give you the opportunity to provide tangible value and support to our growing community.



This is a full-time hourly (non-exempt) position, with overtime available during peak seasons. We’re looking for some individuals that can also offer non-traditional business hours availability, including weekends and evening hours, to help us manage our response time goals throughout the entire week. 



 



Responsibilities



Address inbound customer inquiries regarding our platform’s features Answer all inbound support emails within 60 minutes or less, continually striving for faster response times Triage incoming customer support emails to specialized CS team members as needed Participate in live chat during our standard business hours Schedule live customer screen-sharing sessions and phone calls as needed Investigate and troubleshoot complex customer questions using intuitive problem-solving skills Identify patterns in incoming support emails and chats, and keep an eye out for potential issues Escalate product issues to our Technical Support Specialists Contribute to our ambitious conversion rate goals by conducting live onboarding calls with customers in a free trial
Maintain alignment with the larger Customer Success team and other internal stakeholders Identify and report knowledge gaps and work to get them added to our knowledge systems Work with other SimplePractice teams to handle customer inquiries when inter-department collaboration is needed

 



Desired Skills & Experience



English proficiency 
1-2 years in a customer support or service industry role, serving a diverse and professional customer base 
You have a bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university
You have demonstrated the ability to self-manage towards a set of defined goals & prioritize among many different tasks
A sense of urgency to complete work efficiently and accurately
Comfortable with ambiguity to determine the best solution for our customers 
Comfortable with simultaneously managing and troubleshooting a large number of computer-based programs, applications, and toolsets to perform job duties (e.g., Slack, Asana, Zendesk, Google Workspace)

 



Bonus Points



You're highly empathetic, patient, and really love helping people
You have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with an emphasis on a casual, friendly, and conversational tone
You're self-managing and self-motivating, can organize in a multitasking environment, and can adjust priorities as needed
A plus if you have past experience working with Zendesk or in SAAS companies

 



Monthly Compensation Range



MXN $20,158.33 - $25,350.00 per month 



Base salary is one component of total compensation. 



The above represents the expected base compensation range for this job requisition. Ultimately, in determining your pay, we’ll consider many factors including, but not limited to, skills, experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors.

 

Base salary is one component of total compensation. Employees may also be eligible for an annual bonus or commission. Some roles may also be eligible for overtime pay.The amount below represents the expected annual base compensation range for this job requisition. Ultimately, in determining your pay, we’ll consider many factors including, but not limited to, skills, experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors.



Base Compensation Range: $241,900 MXN - $302,400 MXN

title: Business Development
skills: Fortalecer la imagen de HSBC como proveedor líder de soluciones patrimoniales, proporcionando a los clientes acceso a experiencia técnica avanzada en productos.
Apoyar a los RMs para aumentar la confianza de más clientes en las capacidades de HSBC y mejorar la efectividad y la calidad de las necesidades cubiertas para los clientes objetivo.

Asistir a los RMs y ayudar a cerrar ventas, profundizando y haciendo crecer las relaciones con los clientes mediante la identificación y el descubrimiento de necesidades, la identificación de soluciones patrimoniales relevantes y el análisis técnico, y apoyando a los RMs en la presentación y explicación de la(s) solución(es) al cliente.

Realizar revisiones de cartera con los RMs, evaluar la idoneidad de los productos y el impacto de cambios de mercado y regulatorios en los productos mantenidos por los clientes, según corresponda; identificar oportunidades y gestionar riesgos.

Acompañar a los RMs en 3 a 5 reuniones diarias con clientes y aportar asesoramiento e información basada en conocimiento técnico, actuando como segundo punto de contacto para los RMs en productos y soluciones específicas.
Mínimo de un año de experiencia laboral como especialista de producto o asesor/a financiero/a.
Sólidas habilidades de atención al cliente, con capacidad para ofrecer una experiencia destacada y soluciones basadas en necesidades.

Conocimiento profundo de soluciones y productos financieros complejos específicos, incluyendo conocimiento de mercados financieros, dinámicas socioeconómicas y requisitos regulatorios relevantes para los productos.
Amplia visión del mercado externo sobre condiciones de mercado, nuevas soluciones de producto y comprensión de la actividad actual de los mercados financieros.
Capacidad y experiencia demostradas para identificar correctamente las necesidades del cliente.
Habilidades sólidas para gestionar un proceso de ventas consultivo.
Sólida comprensión de los estándares globales de prevención de blanqueo de capitales (AML).
Trabajo en equipo y habilidades de colaboración.
Conocimiento de regulaciones de cumplimiento locales y del Grupo.
Especialización en una o más categorías de producto, p. ej.: Productos Estructurados/FX/Inversiones/Planificación Financiera/Jubilación/Protección/Hipotecas, etc.
description:
About the job

Please note this job advertisement is only available in Spanish. To view the job, select ‘Español - ES’ from the language selector in the page menu.

El BDM suele desplegarse para aportar valor a clientes con necesidades más complejas, p. ej., clientes P&A, W, PCS, PE, y se asigna a un equipo de Premier Relationship Managers (RMs). Aunque los RMs son responsables del proceso integral de asesoramiento y ventas con los clientes, el BDM les ayuda a comprender mejor a los clientes, a reunirse con ellos junto con los RMs cuando sea necesario, a formular recomendaciones adecuadas y a ayudar a los clientes a entender el valor y los riesgos de las soluciones disponibles.

En ocasiones, el BDM también puede impartir formación de producto para apoyar y ejecutar lanzamientos de nuevos productos, y liderar eventos de educación para clientes para ayudarles a adquirir un mejor conocimiento de los mercados financieros y de las soluciones financieras. El BDM también debe seguir el proceso de ventas basado en necesidades, ofreciendo a los clientes una experiencia patrimonial significativa y consistente y, en última instancia, una experiencia sobresaliente.

Responsabilidades principales


Fortalecer la imagen de HSBC como proveedor líder de soluciones patrimoniales, proporcionando a los clientes acceso a experiencia técnica avanzada en productos.
Apoyar a los RMs para aumentar la confianza de más clientes en las capacidades de HSBC y mejorar la efectividad y la calidad de las necesidades cubiertas para los clientes objetivo.
Asistir a los RMs y ayudar a cerrar ventas, profundizando y haciendo crecer las relaciones con los clientes mediante la identificación y el descubrimiento de necesidades, la identificación de soluciones patrimoniales relevantes y el análisis técnico, y apoyando a los RMs en la presentación y explicación de la(s) solución(es) al cliente.
Realizar revisiones de cartera con los RMs, evaluar la idoneidad de los productos y el impacto de cambios de mercado y regulatorios en los productos mantenidos por los clientes, según corresponda; identificar oportunidades y gestionar riesgos.
Acompañar a los RMs en 3 a 5 reuniones diarias con clientes y aportar asesoramiento e información basada en conocimiento técnico, actuando como segundo punto de contacto para los RMs en productos y soluciones específicas.


Conocimientos y experiencia


Mínimo de un año de experiencia laboral como especialista de producto o asesor/a financiero/a.
Sólidas habilidades de atención al cliente, con capacidad para ofrecer una experiencia destacada y soluciones basadas en necesidades.
Conocimiento profundo de soluciones y productos financieros complejos específicos, incluyendo conocimiento de mercados financieros, dinámicas socioeconómicas y requisitos regulatorios relevantes para los productos.
Amplia visión del mercado externo sobre condiciones de mercado, nuevas soluciones de producto y comprensión de la actividad actual de los mercados financieros.
Capacidad y experiencia demostradas para identificar correctamente las necesidades del cliente.
Habilidades sólidas para gestionar un proceso de ventas consultivo.
Sólida comprensión de los estándares globales de prevención de blanqueo de capitales (AML).
Trabajo en equipo y habilidades de colaboración.
Conocimiento de regulaciones de cumplimiento locales y del Grupo.
Especialización en una o más categorías de producto, p. ej.: Productos Estructurados/FX/Inversiones/Planificación Financiera/Jubilación/Protección/Hipotecas, etc.


Ofrecemos.

Contratación directa por parte del Banco.

Sueldo base, bono trimestral por desempeño, aguinaldo, prima vacacional, seguro de vida, seguro de gastos médicos mayores, créditos, descuentos en los productos del Banco, participación activa en programas de sustentabilidad/ responsabilidad social, descuentos en más de 9,000 establecimientos y un plan de carrera acorde a tus aspiraciones.

“En HSBC esperamos que nuestra gente se trate con dignidad y respeto, creando una cultura incluyente que promueva igualdad de oportunidades. Nuestros valores definen quiénes somos como organización y lo que nos distingue, valoramos la diferencia, avanzamos juntos, nos hacemos responsables de nuestras acciones, usamos el buen juicio, hacemos lo correcto y hacemos que las cosas sucedan”.

“En HSBC nos encontramos orientados a garantizar la igualdad de género y capacitación constante hacia nuestros empleados, así como a la protección de sus derechos laborarles y sociales”.

“En HSBC ofrecemos a nuestros colegas un mayor número de días para que puedan disfrutar al máximo su boda, cuidar al nuevo integrante de la familia, o vivir el duelo por la pérdida de algún familiar”.
