description:
About the job

Overview

CAREERS TO SMILE ABOUT 

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

Responsibilities

The Opportunity 

As a One Touch & Process Automation Analyst, the candidate will gain in-depth exposure to end-to-end purchasing operations for beverage products while supporting the U.S. beverage market. The position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to key operational processes, including invoice validation and Proof of Delivery (POD) reviews, while driving impactful process improvement initiatives.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone passionate about automation, data analysis, and continuous improvement. The selected candidate will play a key role in identifying inefficiencies, developing automation solutions, and enhancing daily operational processes that directly support business performance.

Your Impact 


Develop and implement automation solutions to improve purchasing processes and operational efficiency.
Manage inventory records and maintain purchasing and inventory reports to support business decisions.
Review invoices and Proof of Delivery (POD) documentation to ensure accuracy and compliance.
Investigate and resolve delivery discrepancies by coordinating with carriers and Customer Care teams.
Identify opportunities for process improvement and support continuous improvement initiatives across purchasing operations.


Qualifications

Who Are We Looking For? 


Experience in process improvement and operational efficiency initiatives.
Fluent English (written and spoken).
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint.
Knowledge of Microsoft Power Platform (Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power Pages).
Ability to learn and navigate multiple systems and platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proactive, curious, and self-driven mindset.
Excellent teamwork and communication skills.
Knowledge of programming languages or scripting tools is a plus, but not required.


If this is an opportunity that interests you, we encourage you to apply even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements.

What can you expect from us: 


Opportunities to learn and develop every day through a wide range of programs. 
Internal digital platforms that promote self-learning. 
Development programs according to Leadership skills. 
Specialized training according to the role. 
Learning experiences with internal and external providers. 
We love to celebrate success, which is why we have recognition programs for seniority, behavior, leadership, moments of life, among others. 
Financial wellness programs that will help you reach your goals in all stages of life. 
A flexibility program that will allow you to balance your personal and work life, adapting your working day to your lifestyle. 
And because your family is also important to us, they can also enjoy benefits such as our Wellness Line, thousands of Agreements and Discounts, Scholarship programs for your children, Aid Plans for different moments of life, among others. 


We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We respect and value diversity as a work force and innovation for the organization.

title: Assistant Marketing Manager (Remote Mexico)
skills: Support brand marketing team and franchisees in campaign development and execution, print, digital menu boards, local store marketing (LSM), in-store / on-site store support, new product testing, vendor licensing, budgets, purchase orders and invoicing.
Provide regional marketing campaign support:
Coordination, execution, and communication with system through development of creative services promotional catalog.
Develop standard and/or customized point of purchase (POP) to ensure wide variety of quality products and affordable pricing.

Work with marketing leadership team to develop and execute campaign communication including but not limited to regional marketing creative solutions, intranet postings, crew communication, and frequently asked questions.
Provide franchisee guidance on optional campaign support materials including custom request process.

Establish high quality, low cost providers and develop dependable vendor relationships. Manage and maintain relationships to result in cost-savings efficiencies with the distribution centers for delivery of marketing materials.

title: Paid Social Specialist
skills: Support client updates and weekly reporting with accurate, on‑time inputs; explain results and next steps in plain language tied to goals.
Execute approved changes (e.g., promo, budget, pacing) within guardrails; escalate when trade‑offs are needed.
Own day-to-day campaign execution across Paid Social (Meta, TikTok, etc)
Apply solid Paid Social craft (creative + audience + signal quality) and grow your awareness of how Social connects to Search, Commerce, TVAD, and Creative.

Apply the Tinuiti Way playbooks and best practices: follow the standard SOPs, checklists, and templates for Paid Social setup, QA, delivery checks, and weekly readouts; meet our quality bars (pixel/CAPI health and event mapping, UTMs, catalog/DPA integrity, creative rotation/fatigue, audience overlap, and policy/brand‑safety compliance).
Escalate deviations promptly, capture outcomes in the standard readout format, and share feedback through existing channels.

Operate platform features (Meta Ads Manager including Advantage+/basic value optimization/CAPI; basic TikTok Ads Manager; Pinterest Performance+ as relevant) using checklists; run controlled pilots and monitor outcomes before scaling.
Use team‑shared Bliss Point signals (e.g., simple Forecast snapshots, Creative Insights) to guide day‑to‑day optimizations; log issues and feedback for Product/Analytics partners.
Monitor performance daily/weekly; build accurate recurring reports; run basic A/Bs with predefined success criteria and a simple read cadence; keep budgets and pacing healthy.
Maintain data quality using checklists (pixel/CAPI basics, event checks, UTMs/GA4 alignment); partner with Analytics to validate reads and resolve anomalies.

Manage a clear work queue; deliver on time; reduce rework and non‑converting spend (e.g., limit creative fatigue, de‑dupe overlapping audiences, avoid learning‑phase resets); close the loop with documented learnings.
Greatness attracts Greatness: Seek feedback, hone your craft, and embrace new tools and AI.
Clients grow, we grow: Focus on outcomes, evidence, and simple explanations.
Culture of ownership: Move fast, cut waste, and own results end‑to‑end.
2+ years in Paid Social execution with hands‑on builds, launches, pacing, and optimizations; strong familiarity with Meta Ads buying.
Preferred: experience buying on TikTok, Pinterest Performance+, Snapchat, Reddit, and / or LinkedIn.
Comfortable with GA4 and platform analytics; can turn data into clear next steps.
Strong communication and collaboration; confident handling client updates with support.
Advanced Excel/Sheets and presentation tools; Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience.
Growth support: Contribute to qualified reach, new‑customer acquisition, and revenue from Paid Social.
Creative: Faster creative innovation cycles and higher win rate; reduced fatigue.
Audience and signal: Expand high‑intent audiences; improve pixel/CAPI/event health; fewer “limited learning” cases.
Innovation cadence: Deliver small, safe innovations (creative, audience, bidding, format) and record decisions and learnings.
Product/AI adoption: Pilot and adopt platform automation and Bliss Point‑guided optimizations with proven incremental impact.
Efficiency (guardrail): Maintain or improve CAC/ROAS while growing profitable volume.
description:
About the job

Who We Are

Tinuiti is the largest independent full-funnel marketing agency in the U.S. across the media that matters most, with $4 billion in digital media under management and more than 1,200 employees. Built for marketers who demand growth and accountability, Tinuiti unites media and measurement under one roof to eliminate waste—the biggest growth killer of all—and scale what works. Its proprietary technology, Bliss Point by Tinuiti, reveals the truth around growth and waste, and how to capitalize on it. With expert teams across Commerce, Search, Social, TV & Audio, and more, Tinuiti delivers measurable results with brutal simplicity: Love Growth. Hate Waste.

We’d Love To Hear From You If

Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the criteria, women and other marginalized folks tend to only apply when they check every box. So if you think you qualify, but don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, please still get in touch.

As a Paid Social Specialist, you will help build, launch, and improve Paid Social campaigns (primarily Meta; plus TikTok, Pinterest Performance+, Snapchat, Reddit as relevant) to grow new customers and revenue, while keeping efficiency on track. You’ll follow clear playbooks, turn performance and creative learnings into next actions, and try new ideas in controlled environments before wider rollout. You’ll partner closely with Creative, Analytics, Product/Bliss Point, and senior Channel Leads in your Pod, communicate clearly, and document what works so the team can reuse it. We align scope and expectations to our job architecture for consistency and transparent progression.

Key responsibilities

Client‑Centric


Support client updates and weekly reporting with accurate, on‑time inputs; explain results and next steps in plain language tied to goals.
Execute approved changes (e.g., promo, budget, pacing) within guardrails; escalate when trade‑offs are needed.


The Tinuiti Way


Own day-to-day campaign execution across Paid Social (Meta, TikTok, etc)
Apply solid Paid Social craft (creative + audience + signal quality) and grow your awareness of how Social connects to Search, Commerce, TVAD, and Creative.
Apply the Tinuiti Way playbooks and best practices: follow the standard SOPs, checklists, and templates for Paid Social setup, QA, delivery checks, and weekly readouts; meet our quality bars (pixel/CAPI health and event mapping, UTMs, catalog/DPA integrity, creative rotation/fatigue, audience overlap, and policy/brand‑safety compliance).
Escalate deviations promptly, capture outcomes in the standard readout format, and share feedback through existing channels.


Product‑Led


Operate platform features (Meta Ads Manager including Advantage+/basic value optimization/CAPI; basic TikTok Ads Manager; Pinterest Performance+ as relevant) using checklists; run controlled pilots and monitor outcomes before scaling.
Use team‑shared Bliss Point signals (e.g., simple Forecast snapshots, Creative Insights) to guide day‑to‑day optimizations; log issues and feedback for Product/Analytics partners.


Data & Measurement‑Driven


Monitor performance daily/weekly; build accurate recurring reports; run basic A/Bs with predefined success criteria and a simple read cadence; keep budgets and pacing healthy.
Maintain data quality using checklists (pixel/CAPI basics, event checks, UTMs/GA4 alignment); partner with Analytics to validate reads and resolve anomalies.


Owner Mindset


Manage a clear work queue; deliver on time; reduce rework and non‑converting spend (e.g., limit creative fatigue, de‑dupe overlapping audiences, avoid learning‑phase resets); close the loop with documented learnings.


Ways of working and values


Greatness attracts Greatness: Seek feedback, hone your craft, and embrace new tools and AI.
Clients grow, we grow: Focus on outcomes, evidence, and simple explanations.
Culture of ownership: Move fast, cut waste, and own results end‑to‑end.


Professional & Technical Qualifications


2+ years in Paid Social execution with hands‑on builds, launches, pacing, and optimizations; strong familiarity with Meta Ads buying.
Preferred: experience buying on TikTok, Pinterest Performance+, Snapchat, Reddit, and / or LinkedIn.
Comfortable with GA4 and platform analytics; can turn data into clear next steps.
Strong communication and collaboration; confident handling client updates with support.
Advanced Excel/Sheets and presentation tools; Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience.


KPIs


Growth support: Contribute to qualified reach, new‑customer acquisition, and revenue from Paid Social.
Creative: Faster creative innovation cycles and higher win rate; reduced fatigue.
Audience and signal: Expand high‑intent audiences; improve pixel/CAPI/event health; fewer “limited learning” cases.
Innovation cadence: Deliver small, safe innovations (creative, audience, bidding, format) and record decisions and learnings.
Product/AI adoption: Pilot and adopt platform automation and Bliss Point‑guided optimizations with proven incremental impact.
Efficiency (guardrail): Maintain or improve CAC/ROAS while growing profitable volume.

title: E-commerce Simulation Expert (Remote)
skills: Role: E-commerce Simulation Expert (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $40 - $50/hour
description:
About the job

Role: E-commerce Simulation Expert (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $40 - $50/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an E-commerce Simulation Expert to work on a contract basis. The role focuses on building and refining synthetic e-commerce environments that train and evaluate advanced AI models. Your expertise in real-world commerce will directly shape how AI systems learn, reason, and perform across critical domains. This work supports the development of reliable AI agents and contributes to the evolution of human-in-the-loop AI training.




Key Responsibilities:

• Design and construct synthetic digital stores with realistic product assortments, logical category structures, and authentic pricing models to reflect real-world e-commerce complexity.

• Generate, structure, and maintain comprehensive product catalogs ensuring high data quality, logical consistency, and alignment with market standards.

• Develop diverse shopping scenarios and user prompts that simulate varied consumer intents, preferences, and challenging edge cases to stress-test AI behavior.

• Create evaluation rubrics and conduct rigorous quality assurance to ensure generated data and simulations meet domain-specific accuracy and realism requirements.

• Collaborate with AI teams to refine prompt engineering and scenario design for continuous improvement of AI training datasets.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Demonstrated experience in e-commerce operations, including product data management, catalog structuring, or retail systems with a focus on data integrity and real-world relevance.

• Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills to design complex, logically consistent product hierarchies and pricing logic.

• Familiarity with AI/LLM prompting and evaluation principles, or ability to quickly learn how to structure inputs that generate high-quality training data for AI systems.

• Background in scenario design and user behavior modeling, preferably in retail, consumer goods, or related domains.

• Attention to detail and a quality assurance mindset to maintain high standards in synthetic data generation and simulation environments.

• Self-directed work style with ability to manage multiple asynchronous tasks and deliver consistent, high-quality output under defined deadlines.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers the chance to contribute directly to the development of frontier AI systems by leveraging real-world e-commerce expertise. You will work within a global network supporting AI research, with exposure to cutting-edge reinforcement learning environments and agent-based AI training. The position is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact through applied domain knowledge in a rapidly evolving field.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.




Apply Now!

The company works with over 20,000 carefully vetted language professionals covering more than 110 languages, ensuring accuracy, reliability, and scalability for clients of all sizes. title: Strategic Planner / Strategy Lead
skills: Full-Time
Remote
2.000 USD Per Month
description:
About the job

Role Objective

Full-Time
Remote
2.000 USD Per Month







Role Objective




We are looking for a person with strategic vision, commercial judgment, and creative capabilities to develop strategies, proposals, and presentations that help us generate new business opportunities, strengthen client relationships, and turn ideas into actionable projects.




The role will be focused on creating strategies for marketing projects, innovation, experiences, 360 campaigns, digital platforms, commercial partnerships, loyalty programs, incentives, activations, and business proposals for brands, banks, fintechs, technology companies, and other regional clients.




Main Responsibilities

Strategic Development

Create integrated strategies for marketing, communication, technology, experiences, loyalty, education, incentives, and business development projects.

Translate client needs into clear, actionable proposals with strong commercial potential.

Identify market opportunities, communication territories, relevant insights, and differentiated strategic angles.

Develop strategic concepts for campaigns, platforms, commercial programs, and value propositions.




Research and Analysis

Conduct market, competitor, consumer, trend, and business opportunity analysis.

Research categories, industries, audiences, and cultural contexts to support strategic recommendations.

Identify commercial opportunities in specific markets and turn them into concrete proposals.

Synthesize complex information into clear, relevant, and useful insights for decision-making.




Proposal and Pitch Deck Creation

Develop commercial presentations and pitch decks with strategic narrative, clear structure, and a sales-oriented approach.

Create proposals that convincingly communicate our values and help close new business opportunities.

Build storytelling for projects, campaigns, platforms, partnerships, and commercial solutions.

Organize ideas into visual and executive formats, with a focus on clarity, impact, and persuasion.




Marketing and Campaigns

Design 360 campaign proposals, including strategy, concept, audiences, channels, tactics, media plans, activations, and KPIs.

Develop media plans and channel recommendations according to objectives, audiences, and budget.

Propose awareness, acquisition, engagement, usage, loyalty, incentives, and conversion initiatives.

Connect strategy, creativity, communication, user experience, and business objectives.




Required Experience

Previous experience in strategic planning, marketing, consulting, creative agencies, media agencies, business development, or similar roles.

Experience developing commercial proposals, 360 campaigns, media plans, brand strategies, or communication strategies.

Proven ability to create strategic and commercial presentations from scratch.

Experience in market analysis, benchmarking, research, trends, and insight development.

Experience working with regional brands, banks, fintechs, payment methods, technology, mass consumption, tourism, gastronomy, entertainment, or digital platforms is desirable.




Key Skills

Strategic thinking and analytical capabilities.

Excellent writing and commercial storytelling skills.

Ability to turn complex ideas into simple, clear, and sales-oriented proposals.

Business vision and understanding of commercial objectives.

Creativity applied to real solutions.

Judgment to identify opportunities and build proposals with sales potential.

Research, synthesis, and information structuring capabilities.

Knowledge of marketing, communication, 360 campaigns, and media planning.

Autonomy, proactivity, and the ability to work with minimal supervision.

Strong presentation skills, especially in PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google Slides.




Ideal Profile

A curious, strategic, commercial, and creative person, capable of quickly understanding a client’s context, identifying an opportunity, and turning it into a strong proposal.

The person should feel comfortable working on a variety of projects, from marketing campaigns and activations to technology platforms, incentive programs, commercial partnerships, brand experiences, and regional strategies.

We are looking for someone who not only thinks of ideas, but also knows how to structure them, sell them, and turn them into real business opportunities for us.

description:
About the job

Hey there!

We’re Fever, the world’s leading tech platform for culture and live entertainment.

Our mission? To democratize access to culture and entertainment. With our proprietary cutting-edge technology and data-driven approach, we’re revolutionizing the way people engage with live entertainment.

Every month, our platform inspires over 300 million people in +55 countries (and counting) to discover unforgettable experiences while also empowering event creators with our data and technology, helping them scale, innovate, and enhance their events to reach new audiences.

Our results? We’ve teamed up with major industry leaders like Netflix, F.C. Barcelona, and Primavera Sound, presented international award-winning experiences, and are backed by several leading global investors! Impressive, right?

To achieve our mission, we are looking for bar-raisers with a hands-on mindset who are eager to help shape the future of entertainment!

Ready to be part of the experience?

Now, let’s discuss this role and what you will do to help achieve Fever’s mission.

We're Hiring: Support Agent – Candlelight Mexico 

 📍 On-site in Mexico City | Part.Time 20h/week (Monday-Thursday)

We’re looking for a proactive and detail-oriented Support Agent/ Administrative & Operations Assistant to join our Candlelight team—working behind the scenes to bring magical experiences to life across the Mexican region, while being based in Mexico City.

This role focuses on ensuring that all administrative, documentation, and operational processes behind our events run smoothly. You will play a key role in managing event-related documentation, tracking contracts and signatures, coordinating logistics, and supporting basic financial administration tasks.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, hands-on, comfortable taking ownership of tasks, and able to work autonomously in a fast-paced, international environment.

What You’ll Do

📄 Documentation & Administrative Management


Manage and maintain organized records of event documentation, contracts, permits, and signed official documents
Coordinate signature collection and follow up with stakeholders on pending documentation
Prepare documentation for event execution and shipment when required
Ensure accurate filing, tracking, and proper record-keeping across administrative processes


💰 Finance & Operational Admin Support


Register and organize supplier invoices
Support tracking of payments related to vendors, permits, and administrative services
Maintain organized records for financial documentation and reconciliation


🤝 Internal Coordination


Maintain visibility across tasks, deadlines and project progress
Support communication between stakeholders involved in operational workflows


What You Bring


You live in Mexico City and are legally eligible to work here
You speak Spanish fluently & advanced English (B2 level).
Experience in structured operations, support or admin roles
Strong attention to detail, process orientation and follow-through
Comfort using Google Sheets or Excel (intermediate level or higher)
A team-first mindset, clear communicator and proactive problem-solver
Ability to juggle tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment


Nice to Have


Background in events, production or the music industry
Experience in administrative-heavy environments
Familiarity with tools like Airtable, Notion, Monday, or similar


Why Join Us?


40% discount on all Fever events and experiences
Work in a location in the heart of Mexico City
Help shape the backstage of magical concert experiences
Learn about event production, internal workflows and scalable operations
Room to grow and take on new challenges as the team evolves
Great work environment with a young, international team of talented people to work with!


We welcome people from all walks of life—if this role excites you and you’re motivated to learn and contribute, we want to hear from you.

This role is located in Mexico City.

Thank you for considering joining Fever. We cannot wait to learn more about you!

If you want to learn more about us: Fever's Blog | Tech.Eu |TechCrunch

Fever is committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workspace where everyone's background and ideas count. Our main goal is to find the best possible talent regardless of place of birth, racial or ethnic origin, gender, gender identity, religion, opinion, sexual orientation, disability, pregnancy, marital status, age or caring responsibilities. We encourage everyone to apply!

If you require any kind of accommodation during the selection process please contact our Talent team so we can help you by providing a welcoming and seamless journey.

description:
About the job

What Makes Us Unique 

At Cloudbeds, we're not just building software, we’re transforming hospitality. Our intelligently designed platform powers properties across 150 countries, processing billions in bookings annually. From independent properties to hotel groups, we help hoteliers transform operations and uplevel their commercial strategy through a unified platform that integrates with hundreds of partners. And we do it with a completely remote team. Imagine working alongside global innovators to build AI-powered solutions that solve hoteliers' biggest challenges. Since our founding in 2012, we've become the World's Best Hotel PMS Solutions Provider and landed on Deloitte's Technology Fast 500 again in 2024 – but we're just getting started. 




How You'll Make an Impact: 

As a Customer Support Coach, you will be the voice of and advocate for our customers, providing exceptional support. By resolving questions and troubleshooting issues across multiple channels, you’ll ensure a seamless experience for our hotel partners. Your contributions will strengthen client relationships, promote software adoption, and shape the future of our platform by sharing valuable feedback. You'll also play a key role in creating training materials, updating support resources, and staying ahead of system updates, making a lasting impact on our customers and team.




Our Customer Support Team:

Join a dynamic, fully remote team of over 60 global Customer Support professionals united by our goal of delivering exceptional customer experiences across live chat, email, and phone. We pride ourselves on our supportive, diverse, and collaborative culture, where continuous learning, growth, and innovation drive everything we do. Be part of a team that values connection, celebrates success, and always strives to exceed expectations!




What you Bring to the Team:

Assist and support our existing customers (hotel properties) daily in the use of our software
Resolve customers' inquiries via phone, email, chat, or any other media that Cloudbeds uses to help customers
Troubleshoot and make outbound calls for escalated issues
Foster strong customer relationships with empathy to drive loyalty, retention, and increased software adoption.
Gather customers' feedback and requirements for future releases of the software
Use training materials and attend company-wide online training sessions to keep up-to-date with the latest system updates

1+ years of hospitality experience in hotels, hostels, BNBs, or similar with Front Desk, Night Auditor, Revenue Manager, General Manager, or similar roles

Native or Fluent in English and Spanish, Portuguese is a plus.
Excellent communication skills and a positive attitude
Excellent problem-solving skills and emotional intelligence
Great levels of learning agility
A compatible graduation course or more than 2 years of proven experience in the area
Skill with computers and systems
Excellent internet/wifi connection




What to Expect - Your Journey with Us 

Behind Cloudbeds' revolutionary technology is a team of redefining what's possible in hospitality. We're 650+ employees across 40+ countries, bringing together elite engineers, AI architects, world-class designers, and hospitality veterans to solve challenges others haven't dared to tackle. 




Our diverse team speaks 30+ languages, but we all share one language: a passion for innovation and travel. From pioneering breakthroughs in machine learning to revolutionizing how hotels operate, we're not just watching the future of hospitality unfold – we're coding it, designing it, writing it, and shipping it. If you're ready to work alongside some of the brightest minds in tech who are obsessed with using AI to transform a trillion-dollar industry, this is your chance to be part of something extraordinary.

description:

Es un rol que combina visión estratégica, mentalidad experimental y ejecución comercial de alto impacto. La persona en este rol tendrá que construir desde la estrategia hasta el equipo, con autonomia real para hacerlo.

Estrategia de crecimiento

title: Specialist, Content Optimization
skills: Experience with tools like Helium 10, Data Dive, or Jungle Scout.
Familiarity with Amazon's A+ Content and Brand Store modules.
Experience across multiple categories or markets.
description:
About the job

Brainlabs is the media agency built to answer one question: what's actually driving profit? Founded in 2012 by Daniel Gilbert, we were built by engineers before we were a media agency. Today, 1,000+ Brainlabbers across five continents use our proprietary agents, built on 32 media tools and over 2,500 logged experiments, to help brands connect every channel they plan and buy to one thing: the bottom line.

The role

Brands live and die by their product pages. A great product with poor content gets buried. A well-optimized listing with clear titles, strong keywords, and accurate attributes climbs. This role

owns that gap.

As a Content Optimization and Catalogue Management Specialist, you'll be responsible for how our clients products show up, get found, and convert on Amazon and Mercado Libre. That means writing and refining listings, managing catalogue health, and building the kind of organic visibility that doesn't disappear the moment ad spend drops.

You'll work directly with client teams and internal strategists to keep catalogues clean, content sharp, and rankings moving in the right direction.

What will you do?

Your day-to-day breaks into two connected areas.

Content Optimization

Content optimization is where you drive organic performance. You'll conduct keyword research, write and update product titles, bullet points, descriptions, and backend attributes. You'll audit existing content against platform best practices and identify gaps before they become ranking problems. You'll test copy variations and track what actually moves the needle.

Catalogue Management

Catalogue management is where you keep the operation running. You'll handle the publication and maintenance of product listings, manage bulk uploads and flat files, resolve listing errors

and suppressed ASINs or items, and keep content synchronized across multiple accounts and categories. When something breaks, you figure out why and fix it.

Beyond those two pillars, you'll monitor organic ranking performance, report on content health metrics, and bring recommendations that are grounded in data, not guesswork.

What are we looking for?

Experience

Around three years working in content optimization and/or catalogue management on Amazon and/or Mercado Libre. You understand how these platforms rank organic results and you've

influenced those rankings for real brands.

Platform Fluency

You know Seller Central and Vendor Central. You've worked with Mercado Libre item publication flows. Flat files, bulk uploads, listing errors, and suppressed items are familiar

territory, not new concepts.

Keyword and SEO Fundamentals

You know how to research, prioritize, and place keywords in a way that platforms reward. You understand the difference between what drives impressions and what drives conversions.

Analytical Mindset

You read performance data and draw conclusions from it. If organic rankings drop, you have a process for diagnosing why. If a content change works, you can explain what it did and why it helped.

Attention to Detail

Catalogue work is unforgiving. A wrong attribute, a missing image, a misformatted file: these things cause real problems. You catch errors before they ship.

English Proficiency

You're comfortable reading, writing, and communicating professionally in English. You'll work with international teams and documentation.

Nice to Have


Experience with tools like Helium 10, Data Dive, or Jungle Scout.
Familiarity with Amazon's A+ Content and Brand Store modules.
Experience across multiple categories or markets.



What happens next?

We know searching for a job is tough and that you want to find the best career and employer for you. We also want to ensure that this position is the best fit for both you and us. Therefore, you will participate in a comprehensive interview process that includes skills interviews with our team. The goal of this process is to allow you to get to know us as we learn more about you.

Brainlabs actively seeks and encourages applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds and identities. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace: we are committed to equal opportunity for all applicants and employees regardless of age, disability, sex, gender reassignment, sexual orientation, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion, or belief, and marriage and civil partnerships. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation during the application process, please let us know!

Please note that we will never ask you to transfer cash or make any other payment to us in order to apply for a role or to work for Brainlabs. Any such asks are fraudulent and should be reported to the appropriate authorities in your area.

title: Content & Localisation Marketer | Brazil
skills: MUST: Full professional-level Brazilian Portuguese
MUST: Professional English & Spanish
Role Type: Full-time contract role
📱 Booking system
💻 Website & App
🎟️ In-person classes, events, courses, 1-on-1, and more
🤑 Automated payments & subscriptions
🐣 Client & business management
🙌 + much more

Take our high-performing Spanish marketing and growth assets (product marketing, sales collateral, landing pages, website, email) and duplicate, translate and re-architect them into native Brazilian Portuguese
Evolve from translation to locally created content as your read on the market grows
Own brand voice and tone in Brazilian Portuguese (persuasive, creative, friendly)
Implement our SEO playbook for Brazil: local keyword research, on-page optimization, and SEO best practices in your writing
SEO is a key driver of our growth in Mexico. We expect it to be the same in Brazil, and you are central to that
Write and optimize website pages, landing pages and ad copy that convert, with a strict focus on readability and Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO)
Build lead magnets and launch assets that attract and convert
Communicate the product and its benefits to Brazilian studios in collaboration with product and marketing
Support product launches with positioning, copy and assets, framed around the Studio Success Model
Manage Brazil social media and produce the content calendar
Write marketing email and lifecycle copy to move users from awareness to conversion and to retain them
Write scripts for videos and record product videos for the Brazilian market
Build, manage and launch localized growth assets in HubSpot: landing pages, forms, property segmentation, and automated multi-channel lead-nurturing workflows
Track content and SEO performance, report progress, and use insights to improve impact

Bold Brazil market localization: a strong track record of launching or scaling digital products in Brazil, with deep expertise in adapting messaging to local business psychology, cultural nuances, and specific B2B and B2C semantics.

HubSpot campaign & CMS management: hands-on experience building, managing and launching localized growth assets within HubSpot (or similar tools), including localized landing pages, forms, property segmentation, and automated multi-channel lead-nurturing workflows.

International SEO & content optimization: experience implementing localized SEO strategies for the Brazilian market. Proven ability to conduct local keyword research, optimize on-page copy, and ensure high visibility in local search engines.

Growth-driven content production: experience producing high-conversion content across the full funnel, including ad creatives, educational material and onboarding assets, with a strict focus on readability, visual alignment and CRO.

Visual & textual content discipline: direct experience owning the final output of content production pipelines. A sharp eye for layout formatting, margin hygiene, typographic hierarchy and visual consistency when translating content across platforms.
Hands-on startup execution: experience as a high-output, autonomous contributor in fast-paced startup environments, executing day-to-day with a proactive ownership mindset over the final result.
You love writing and obsess over copy that converts
Strong grasp of digital marketing channels: SEO, social media, email marketing and content marketing
Strong SEO understanding for copywriting, best practices and keyword research
Experience writing high-converting landing pages, ads and website copy (CRO mindset)
Product marketing chops for SaaS
HubSpot or other CRM experience (email, flows, automations, CMS)
Great with AI tools and getting things done online, strong computer skills
A self-starter and manager of one. You do not need a big team to execute
Flexible and creative. You will try anything to get our ICP to adopt Fitune
Native Brazilian Portuguese (must)
Professional English (must)
Professional Spanish (must)
Affinity with the fitness industry or experience in it (big plus)
Strong analytical skills and comfort using data to inform content and SEO
Prior experience localizing a product specifically for Brazil
You cannot work hard right now
You need a traditional, highly structured environment. As a startup we are still figuring things out and you are excited to figure it out with us
Remote role, based in Brazil

About the job

First Marketing hire in Brazil




Disclaimer:

MUST: Full professional-level Brazilian Portuguese
MUST: Professional English & Spanish 
Role Type: Full-time contract role




What's Fitune?

The Shopify for Fitness & Wellness Studios

Fitness & wellness studios struggle with complicated software or manual processes to run their businesses.

We built Fitune, the one-stop-shop to make running their studio less stressful and more profitable.

We are not just software. We are the system for a studio to win, helping studios Attract, Convert, Retain and Operate.




How?

With Fitune, studios get a conversion-optimised website, app and admin software including:

📱 Booking system
💻 Website & App
🎟️ In-person classes, events, courses, 1-on-1, and more
🤑 Automated payments & subscriptions
🐣 Client & business management
🙌 + much more




Where are we at?

Fitune launched in 2021 and now serves hundreds of fitness & wellness studios across Mexico, processing hundreds of thousands of bookings a month. Our ~30-person team is on a mission to make running and growing a studio easy.

We’re on a mission to become the biggest platform in Latam. We won Mexico, we’re now entering Brazil.







The Role

👀 What we're looking for




We are looking for a hungry, content-obsessed marketer to own all marketing content for Brazil and localize our proven Mexican growth engine into the Brazilian market.

We have a marketing machine that works in Mexico, your job is to bring that engine to Brazil and make it sing in native Brazilian Portuguese.

To start, you will translate and localize our Mexican assets. As your understanding of the Brazilian market deepens, more and more should be created locally and from scratch, tuned to local business psychology, culture and how Brazilian studios actually buy.

This is not a dashboard role. Your motto: "Ideas are cheap, execution is everything."




What you'll do

1. Localization & content duplication

Take our high-performing Spanish marketing and growth assets (product marketing, sales collateral, landing pages, website, email) and duplicate, translate and re-architect them into native Brazilian Portuguese
Evolve from translation to locally created content as your read on the market grows
Own brand voice and tone in Brazilian Portuguese (persuasive, creative, friendly)

2. SEO content & keyword research

Implement our SEO playbook for Brazil: local keyword research, on-page optimization, and SEO best practices in your writing
SEO is a key driver of our growth in Mexico. We expect it to be the same in Brazil, and you are central to that

3. High-converting copy (website, landing pages, ads)

Write and optimize website pages, landing pages and ad copy that convert, with a strict focus on readability and Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO)
Build lead magnets and launch assets that attract and convert

4. Product marketing

Communicate the product and its benefits to Brazilian studios in collaboration with product and marketing
Support product launches with positioning, copy and assets, framed around the Studio Success Model

5. Social media, email & lifecycle

Manage Brazil social media and produce the content calendar
Write marketing email and lifecycle copy to move users from awareness to conversion and to retain them

6. Video

Write scripts for videos and record product videos for the Brazilian market

7. HubSpot campaign & CMS management

Build, manage and launch localized growth assets in HubSpot: landing pages, forms, property segmentation, and automated multi-channel lead-nurturing workflows

8. Performance & optimization

Track content and SEO performance, report progress, and use insights to improve impact




Desired experience & qualifications

Bold Brazil market localization: a strong track record of launching or scaling digital products in Brazil, with deep expertise in adapting messaging to local business psychology, cultural nuances, and specific B2B and B2C semantics.
HubSpot campaign & CMS management: hands-on experience building, managing and launching localized growth assets within HubSpot (or similar tools), including localized landing pages, forms, property segmentation, and automated multi-channel lead-nurturing workflows.
International SEO & content optimization: experience implementing localized SEO strategies for the Brazilian market. Proven ability to conduct local keyword research, optimize on-page copy, and ensure high visibility in local search engines.
Growth-driven content production: experience producing high-conversion content across the full funnel, including ad creatives, educational material and onboarding assets, with a strict focus on readability, visual alignment and CRO.
Visual & textual content discipline: direct experience owning the final output of content production pipelines. A sharp eye for layout formatting, margin hygiene, typographic hierarchy and visual consistency when translating content across platforms.
Hands-on startup execution: experience as a high-output, autonomous contributor in fast-paced startup environments, executing day-to-day with a proactive ownership mindset over the final result.




Who are you?

Must

You love writing and obsess over copy that converts
Strong grasp of digital marketing channels: SEO, social media, email marketing and content marketing
Strong SEO understanding for copywriting, best practices and keyword research
Experience writing high-converting landing pages, ads and website copy (CRO mindset)
Product marketing chops for SaaS
HubSpot or other CRM experience (email, flows, automations, CMS)
Great with AI tools and getting things done online, strong computer skills
A self-starter and manager of one. You do not need a big team to execute
Flexible and creative. You will try anything to get our ICP to adopt Fitune

Language requirements:

Native Brazilian Portuguese (must)
Professional English (must)
Professional Spanish (must)

Nice to have

Affinity with the fitness industry or experience in it (big plus)
Strong analytical skills and comfort using data to inform content and SEO
Prior experience localizing a product specifically for Brazil

Who is this not a fit for?

You cannot work hard right now
You need a traditional, highly structured environment. As a startup we are still figuring things out and you are excited to figure it out with us







📍 Location

Remote role, based in Brazil




How to apply ✅

Think you'd be a great fit for this position at Fitune?

 Fill out our Job Application Form at https://forms.gle/oKQcKw8yARTdvkct7

title: Technical Account Manager, Digital Transformation

description:
About the job

Only applications of candidates with Mexican citizenship will be evaluated for this role in compliance with the provisions of Article 7 of the Federal Labor Law.

Please submit your resume in English - we can only consider applications submitted in this language.

Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
2 years of experience in a technical project management or a customer-facing role. 
Experience supporting customers in cloud operations (e.g., launch or capacity planning, product release management), technical support, escalation management, or consulting.


Preferred qualifications:


 Experience translating business requirements into technological solutions.
Experience collaborating with teams, groups, business units, channel partners, systems integrators, and third-party developers to deliver high-impact solutions.
Experience in application or workload migration to public cloud providers.
Understanding of IT operations, database systems, networking, IT security, application development, service architecture, cloud-native application development, hosted services, storage systems, or content delivery networks.
Excellent written and verbal communication, presentation, problem-solving, and client management skills.


About The Job

The Google Cloud Platform team helps customers transform and build what's next for their business — all with technology built in the cloud. Our products are developed for security, reliability and scalability, running the full stack from infrastructure to applications to devices and hardware. Our teams are dedicated to helping our customers — developers, small and large businesses, educational institutions and government agencies — see the benefits of our technology come to life. As part of an entrepreneurial team in this rapidly growing business, you will play a key role in understanding the needs of our customers and help shape the future of businesses of all sizes use technology to connect with customers, employees and partners.

Google Cloud accelerates every organization’s ability to digitally transform its business and industry. We deliver enterprise-grade solutions that leverage Google’s cutting-edge technology, and tools that help developers build more sustainably. Customers in more than 200 countries and territories turn to Google Cloud as their trusted partner to enable growth and solve their most critical business problems.

Responsibilities


Accelerate customer adoption of Google Cloud by leading the implementation journey. Provide technical guidance and manage timelines, milestones, migration goal and business transformation strategies.
Advocate for customer needs in order to overcome adoption blockers and drive new feature development. Lead across multiple work streams and teams to maintain customer momentum.
Develop relationships with stakeholders to understand customer’s business, develop roadmaps, and lead quarterly business reviews and executive sessions to better understand business and technical needs.
Plan for customer events and launches, partnering with Support, Engineers, and Site Reliability Engineers to ensure customer success, and work with customers and Support to guide issues/escalations to resolution.
Develop best practices and assets based on learnings from customer engagements to support initiatives to scale through partners and accelerate Google Cloud adoption.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

description:
About the job

About Role
As a Fullstory Analyst/Onboarding Specialist you are the primary guide for our clients as they begin their journey with Fullstory. You will lead them through a structured process designed to educate, enable, and empower their teams to maximize the platform's value. This is a technical training role.
Key Responsibilities

Guiding Fullstory onboarding customers throughout the onboarding project, from kick-off to final training session.
Coaching on installation, answering basic questions, sharing best practices, and providing high-quality, live-instructor training of the Fullstory platform.
Working with the clients to schedule and keep onboarding on track against agreed-upon timelines.
CX insights/ Data analysis work with guidance from management and leadership.
Continuous review of Fullstory features, seeking training (self or guided) as needed.
Staying up to date on industry trends, uses of AI in analytics, and other relevant topics to the job/company/industry.
Participating and managing the completion of tasks on time and efficiently.
Building and reviewing presentation materials for client meetings, keeping accurate and detailed records/notes of training sessions, and communicating professionally to clients and team members.

Key Requirements

Completion of 4-year college degree in Human Computer Interaction (HCI), UX Research, Digital Marketing, Digital Analytics, or other related field.
6+ months of web analytics experience using tools such as Google Analytics, Fullstory, and similar tools.
1+ year of digital UX-related (web design, UX design, user research, front-end development, or other similar) experience.
Highly analytical, numbers-oriented, and able to interpret statistical data at both the user and business levels.
Working knowledge of digital marketing and digital commerce, specifically traffic acquisition, e-commerce, and lead generation practices and related technologies.
Strong attention to detail, extremely organized, and capable of managing projects with limited supervision.
Knowledge of website design and UX best practices, and understanding of web development.
Effective, proactive, and efficient communication skills.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, dynamic environment

Preferred Qualifications: Extensive experience with the Fullstory platform, familiarity with basic qual/quant research, strong understanding of how websites are developed and updated, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript and related front-end technologies , and working knowledge of mobile app development and related frameworks.
Working Conditions

Work Environment: 100% Remote
Schedule: Part-time employment.

Why Join Us?

You will report directly to the COO, ensuring your strategies are heard, valued, and implemented without bureaucratic red tape. APPLY NOW!

description:
About the job

Role: AI Data Collector (Remote)

Location: Work from anywhere

Job Type: Part-Time / Flexible

Daily Commitment: Flexible

No language barriers — this project records tasks only; no audio or speech required.

No experience required.




About the Role

Rilith AI is partnering with contributors — students, working professionals (office/field), and homemakers — to work on a research project in which participants record short egocentric (first-person POV) videos of everyday household tasks and daily activities performed at their work locations. You will record your hands carrying out real-life activities — at home or at work — with no extra time required beyond your normal routine. Everybody can participate.




Pay Rate

USD 10 per hour — United States
USD 6.5 per hour — Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Colombia, Argentina




Each participant can record up to 100 hours per environment (e.g. home, warehouse).




Eligibility Requirements

You must:

Reside in the United States, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Colombia, or Argentina.
Own a compatible device:
Apple iPhone — iPhone 11 through 17 (all variants: Mini / Plus / Pro / Pro Max) (Not accepted: iPhone 16e, iPhone 17e)
Google Pixel — Pixel 6 through 10 (standard & Pro)
Samsung Galaxy — Galaxy S21 through S26 (standard / + / Ultra)




What You Will Do

Record first-person (egocentric) videos while performing real-life activities, including:

Household tasks (cooking, cleaning, laundry, and more)
Warehouse operations
Logistics activities
Retail and commercial operations

Check the app for the full list of available tasks.




Bonus & Payment Terms

Pay rate: USD 10/hr (US) / USD 6/hr (LATAM)
Payment method: PayPal
Flexible payment options - hourly, daily, weekly - as per the participants




Why Join?

Contribute to the development of next-generation AI and robotics systems
Get paid for every hour of video recorded in accordance with project guidelines
Start earning from day one
Record during your normal daily routine — no extra time needed
24/7 expert support available
Students and working professionals may request a certificate of contribution upon completion

Your data will remain secure. No personal information will be shared or disclosed without your consent.




About Rilith:

Rilith is empowering intelligent machines at scale. With teams spanning the USA, LATAM, EU, and Asia, we bring a truly global perspective to every project we undertake. Our on-the-ground experience extends beyond software and data — our team has hands-on experience working directly alongside robotic hardware, giving us a deeper understanding of real-world deployment needs. At Rilith, we take data responsibility seriously; all footage we collect is PII-free, with no face capture, ensuring every deliverable is fully privacy-safe. We don't just collect data — we collect it the right way.




We are highly agile and adaptive to challenging data projects.




Specializations:

Egocentric Head-Mounted Video – First-person POV captures using head-mounted rigs across residential and commercial settings. We handle participant recruitment, field deployments, and high-quality footage delivery.

Egocentric Human Hand Video – Hand-object interaction recordings in manufacturing, household, and daily-use settings using a multi-view rig (head, left-wrist, and right-wrist cameras).

Robotics Manipulation – Video capture of robotic gripper (2–3 finger) and dexterous hand (4–5 finger) tasks using a multi-view rig (head/overhead, left/right gripper, and front/top views).




Join our global network of Rilith Warriors:

Rilith has expertise in creating data specifically for Physical AI, which it does in collaboration with a globally spread freelancer network — we call them Rilith Warriors. What differentiates us from others is that we respect, care for, and understand the challenges faced by freelancers.

We ensure:

Timely payment
Continuous projects

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Executes and participates in general marketing functions that are more routine in nature, require smaller spend budgets and requires minimal contact with clients. Requires oversight with developing approach for more complex marketing functions which include higher thresholds of spend levels.

Participates in collaboration with business development, customers, product management, sales and other business partners in support of client-customized, content-rich marketing collateral, such as proposals, corporate communication, and other marketing related information for marketing functions more routine in nature. Will make recommendations regarding marketing process. Supports the marketing approach within strategy and budget.
Supports the development of strategic marketing plans for existing lines and participates in the interpretation of plans into “big ideas” to extend the company’s brand vision.
Supports the strategic direction, growth, and vision of assigned strategic growth objectives for the segment and manages the execution of programs. Has minimal client contact.
Supports the development of multiple pieces of promotional and advertising materials, such as press releases, social and digital media published on-line via web sites, twitter, etc.

Based on assigned strategic growth initiatives, builds and maintains existing relationships with internal contacts, external communications consultants and/or agencies. Participates in meetings with internal and/or external clients to understand their ongoing strategies and how relationships can be strengthened within the client’s organization.
Supports the development of product promotion and launch strategies.

Beginning to act as a marketing advocate, communications consultant, and/or liaison for business support units and/or operational units within the organization. Supports communications with leaders in those units on specific communications issues and strategic growth initiatives and coordinates appropriate marketing resources to achieve their functional goals.

Recommends measurements for return on investment (ROI) for marketing projects within the specific goals of building and delivering campaigns, such as internal and external communications, product launches, new business units, acquisitions, and RFP/RFI proposals within planned scope, schedule, cost and quality, and trade shows/events. Tracks costs against measurements.
Bachelor's Degree
Relevant Experience or Degree in: Digital Marketing , Advertising ,
Typically Minimum 3 Years Relevant Exp
Prefer marketing service or marketing agency experience. Prefer sales force and email automation experience. Prefer payments industry experience.

Skills / Knowledge - Having wide-ranging experience, uses professional concepts and company objectives to resolve moderately complex issues in creative and effective ways. Having ownership of a sub-function, account or matrix management responsibilities, applies knowledge to meet goals, maintain relationships, propose opportunities to expand the business, and lead matrix teams. Some barriers to entry exist at this level (e.g., dept./peer review).

Job Complexity - Works on complex issues where analysis of situations or data requires an in-depth evaluation of variable factors. Exercises judgment in selecting methods, techniques and evaluation criteria for obtaining results. Networks with key contacts outside own area of expertise. Builds on/Maintains external relationships of assigned accounts.
Supervision - Determines methods and procedures on new assignments and may coordinate activities of other personnel (Team Lead).
Marketing production execution - Broad marketing services and/or print/production
Communication skills - Including communication with internal or external audiences

Recommends measurements for return on investment (ROI) for marketing projects within the specific goals of building and delivering campaigns, such as internal and external communications, product launches, new business units, acquisitions, and RFP/RFI proposals within planned scope, schedule, cost and quality, and trade shows/events. Tracks costs against measurements.


What Are We Looking For in This Role?

Minimum Qualifications


Bachelor's Degree
Relevant Experience or Degree in: Digital Marketing , Advertising ,
Typically Minimum 3 Years Relevant Exp


Preferred Qualifications


Prefer marketing service or marketing agency experience. Prefer sales force and email automation experience. Prefer payments industry experience.


What Are Our Desired Skills and Capabilities?


Skills / Knowledge - Having wide-ranging experience, uses professional concepts and company objectives to resolve moderately complex issues in creative and effective ways. Having ownership of a sub-function, account or matrix management responsibilities, applies knowledge to meet goals, maintain relationships, propose opportunities to expand the business, and lead matrix teams. Some barriers to entry exist at this level (e.g., dept./peer review).
Job Complexity - Works on complex issues where analysis of situations or data requires an in-depth evaluation of variable factors. Exercises judgment in selecting methods, techniques and evaluation criteria for obtaining results. Networks with key contacts outside own area of expertise. Builds on/Maintains external relationships of assigned accounts.
Supervision - Determines methods and procedures on new assignments and may coordinate activities of other personnel (Team Lead).
Marketing production execution - Broad marketing services and/or print/production
Communication skills - Including communication with internal or external audiences

description:
About the job

Siemens Digital Industries Software is a leading provider of solutions for the design, simulation, and manufacture of products across many different industries. Formula 1 cars, skyscrapers, ships, space exploration vehicles, and many of the objects we see in our daily lives are being conceived and manufactured using our Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) software.

We are looking for proactive engineers with a strong desire to learn, a problem-solving mindset, and a clear customer-first attitude. As part of a global Siemens organization, you will work closely with international teams to support and continuously improve Siemens Digital Industries Software solutions, playing a key role in ensuring stability, performance, and customer satisfaction across our application landscape.

Key Responsibilities: 

Depending on the area of the team you join, your responsibilities may include supporting the operation, maintenance, and incident resolution of Siemens software solutions in the cloud or contributing to the development of customer-specific customizations and enhancements. Our primary focus areas include Teamcenter and Opcenter, and you will be exposed to real-world production environments, global customers, and collaborative, follow-the-sun operations—making this role an excellent opportunity to grow both technically and professionally within a high-performing global team.

Qualifications: 


High level of written and spoken English.
Bachelor’s degree in computer science or a related field.
Availability to work on-site during the initial training period.
Candidates must be based in Mexico City or Monterrey
Minimum of 3 year of professional experience is preferred.
Previous experience with Siemens Software such as Teamcenter and Opcenter or similar is highly desirable.


Technical Skills


Strong Software development background in Python, C and C++, HTML5, CSS2, modern JavaScript (NodeJS/NPM), ReactJS
Experience with databases and query languages (SQL)
Familiarity with CyberArk and VirtualBox (Nice to have)


We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Qualified Applicants must be legally authorized for employment in Mexico. Qualified Applicants will not require employer sponsored work authorization now or in the future for employment in Mexico.

Why us? 

Working at Siemens Software means flexibility - Choosing between working at home and the office at other times is the norm here. We offer great benefits and rewards, as you'd expect from a world leader in industrial software.

A collection of over 377,000 minds building the future one day at a time in over 200 countries. We're dedicated to equality, and we welcome applications that reflect the diversity of the communities we work in. All employment decisions at Siemens are based on qualifications, merit, and business need. Bring your curiosity and creativity and help us shape tomorrow!

Siemens Software. Transform the Everyday with Us

description:
About the job

We’re not looking for a marketing guru.

We’re looking for someone who can keep things moving.




A growing property management company needs a Marketing Assistant who can turn ideas into content, keep listings updated, and make sure properties are presented professionally across every platform.




Because in property management, empty listings cost money.

And inconsistent marketing creates confusion.




That’s where you come in.

If you’re organized, creative, and know how to manage content without letting details slip through the cracks, this could be the role for you.




What You’ll Do

Create marketing content and graphics using Canva
Repurpose existing materials into fresh, engaging content
Draft flyers, newsletters, and property descriptions
Update and maintain property listings on Apartments.com and other rental platforms
Schedule and manage social media posts on Facebook and Instagram
Keep marketing materials accurate, on-brand, and posted on time
Maintain a content calendar for campaigns and updates
Support basic video editing for social and marketing content
Monitor engagement and respond to simple social media inquiries when needed




What We’re Looking For

Experience with Canva and basic marketing content creation
Familiarity with social media scheduling and posting
Basic video editing skills
Experience updating websites or property listings
Strong attention to detail and organization
Ability to manage multiple tasks independently
Clear written and verbal communication skills
Property management marketing experience is a plus




Why This Role Matters

Good marketing helps great properties get noticed.

Your work will help keep listings active, content consistent, and marketing operations running smoothly, so the team can focus on serving residents and growing the business.

If you enjoy creating content, staying organized, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Retention Specialist (Latin America Contractor)
skills: Customer Engagement: Proactively reach out to customers who have requested cancellations or expressed dissatisfaction. Listen to their concerns, identify pain points, and offer personalized solutions to retain their business.
Issue Resolution: Address and resolve customer issues in a timely and effective manner. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure that all customer concerns are fully resolved and documented.
Retention Strategies: Develop and implement tailored retention strategies for different customer segments, utilizing data and customer feedback to continuously improve these approaches.
Renewal Assistance: Assist customers with the renewal process, ensuring they fully understand the value of our services and the benefits of continuing their relationship with us.
Reporting & Analytics: Track and report on retention metrics, customer feedback, and the effectiveness of retention strategies. Provide insights and recommendations to the Retention Manager.
Product Knowledge: Stay up-to-date on all product features, updates, and industry trends to effectively communicate the value proposition to customers.
Collaboration: Work closely with the Sales, Product, and Customer Success teams to share insights and ensure a cohesive approach to customer retention.
Upsell & Cross-Sell: Identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities that align with customer needs, contributing to revenue growth.
2+ years of experience in customer service, sales, or a related field.
Strong foundation in customer-facing roles with excellent communication and active listening skills.
Proven track record of meeting or exceeding quotas/KPIs.
Experience with Salesforce or similar CRM systems.
Ability to handle high-volume, fast-paced phone interactions with resilience.
Problem-solving and conflict resolution skills.
Adaptability to a fast-changing environment with attention to detail and consistent follow-through.
Customer-centric mindset with a genuine desire to help businesses succeed.
Experience in the Home Improvement/Construction vertical or adjacent fields.
Truth. We value honesty and data. We seek to understand what is reality, so we can effectively respond to it.
Slope. Rate of change over time. We hire and reward based on a team member’s potential, capacity, and growth-mindset, rather than a fancy resume.
Mutual Benefit. The best outcomes happen when everyone wins - customers, team members, and the company. We seek to understand each other’s aspirations and create alignment to get there.
Competitive Greatness. We desire an opportunity and environment from which to pull the greatest versions of ourselves out into the world, rather than just a “job”.
Mission-driven, values-based culture.
Competitive pay.
Unmatched opportunities to learn and develop; front-row seat at a fast-growing tech startup
description:
About the job

About Hearth

Hearth is the AI growth system for local home improvement small businesses. We build revenue-critical software for home improvement contractors — the roofers, HVAC techs, and builders who keep America running.

Our platform helps real business owners win more jobs, protect profit margins, and manage homeowner relationships professionally so they can build generational companies for their families and communities.

We are now building the next layer: a personal AI employee for every small business in the trades focused on autonomously generating sales for America’s home improvement pros. An assistant who answers every call 24/7, qualifies leads, books appointments, follows up on quotes and estimates, and helps small business owners go from missing half of their business to capturing all of it.

With 15,000+ paying customers, default profitability, and backing from some of the world’s best investors (Founder’s Fund, 8VC, Human Capital, etc.), Hearth is focused on bringing AI to the trades.

The Opportunity

As a Retention Specialist at Hearth, you will play a critical role in our Customer Success team, focusing on managing inbound cancellations, running retention initiatives, and driving revenue growth. Your mission is to deeply understand our customers' needs, address their concerns, and use all available resources to retain their business. This role is ideal for someone who is motivated by results, resilient in challenging situations, and passionate about helping businesses succeed.

The ideal candidate for this role is someone who is eager to leverage their unique skills to significantly impact the customer experience while developing their career and expanding their influence within the company. This opportunity is not just a job—it's a chance to use your strengths to transform customer retention and influence the overall direction of how we assist our customers. You will have the ability to shape the top of the funnel through detailed data collection and reporting, becoming a pivotal member of the company. Your efforts will be recognized as key to driving rapid company growth, and you’ll be compensated based on your results, similar to a sales rep. This is a role where your contributions truly matter.

What You'll Do

We’re searching for someone who’s got a natural knack for sales—you know how to ask the right questions, dig deep, and truly understand what’s bothering our customers. You’re the type of person who doesn’t just stop at the surface but uses every tool at your disposal to find solutions that work. Cold calls? Tough conversations? They don’t scare you. In fact, you thrive in those moments, staying cool and collected no matter how challenging things get.

Curiosity is in your DNA. You’re genuinely interested in our customers and passionate about helping them succeed. When the going gets tough, you don’t shy away; instead, you roll up your sleeves and get the job done, no matter what it takes. Doing the right thing is important to you—even if it’s not the easiest or most popular choice.

You’re hungry for results and not just here to clock in and out. You’re driven to make a real impact, and you see your performance as the key to controlling your own income. And let’s be honest—you’re ambitious. You’re always looking for ways to grow, improve, and move up in your career.


Customer Engagement: Proactively reach out to customers who have requested cancellations or expressed dissatisfaction. Listen to their concerns, identify pain points, and offer personalized solutions to retain their business.
Issue Resolution: Address and resolve customer issues in a timely and effective manner. Collaborate with internal teams to ensure that all customer concerns are fully resolved and documented.
Retention Strategies: Develop and implement tailored retention strategies for different customer segments, utilizing data and customer feedback to continuously improve these approaches.
Renewal Assistance: Assist customers with the renewal process, ensuring they fully understand the value of our services and the benefits of continuing their relationship with us.
Reporting & Analytics: Track and report on retention metrics, customer feedback, and the effectiveness of retention strategies. Provide insights and recommendations to the Retention Manager.
Product Knowledge: Stay up-to-date on all product features, updates, and industry trends to effectively communicate the value proposition to customers.
Collaboration: Work closely with the Sales, Product, and Customer Success teams to share insights and ensure a cohesive approach to customer retention.
Upsell & Cross-Sell: Identify upsell and cross-sell opportunities that align with customer needs, contributing to revenue growth.


What We’re Looking For


2+ years of experience in customer service, sales, or a related field.
Strong foundation in customer-facing roles with excellent communication and active listening skills.
Proven track record of meeting or exceeding quotas/KPIs.
Experience with Salesforce or similar CRM systems.
Ability to handle high-volume, fast-paced phone interactions with resilience.
Problem-solving and conflict resolution skills.
Adaptability to a fast-changing environment with attention to detail and consistent follow-through.
Customer-centric mindset with a genuine desire to help businesses succeed.


Preferred


Experience in the Home Improvement/Construction vertical or adjacent fields.


Our Core Values


Truth. We value honesty and data. We seek to understand what is reality, so we can effectively respond to it.
Slope. Rate of change over time. We hire and reward based on a team member’s potential, capacity, and growth-mindset, rather than a fancy resume. 
Mutual Benefit. The best outcomes happen when everyone wins - customers, team members, and the company. We seek to understand each other’s aspirations and create alignment to get there.
 Competitive Greatness. We desire an opportunity and environment from which to pull the greatest versions of ourselves out into the world, rather than just a “job”. 


Location

This a remote role based in Latin America

Benefits


Mission-driven, values-based culture.
Competitive pay.
Unmatched opportunities to learn and develop; front-row seat at a fast-growing tech startup


More About Us

Hearth embraces diversity. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and do not discriminate on the basis of sex, race, color, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, citizenship, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.

Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.

Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.

Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
