description:
About the job

Descripción de la empresa Decidata es una empresa tecnológica que ayuda a otras empresas a conectar sus estrategias de marketing online y offline. Desarrollamos una tecnología propia capaz de detectar, monitorizar y analizar cada anuncio de TV, Tv de Paga, Radio, permitiendo sincronizarlos en tiempo real con campañas de marketing digital. Nuestro objetivo es avanzar hacia una integración digital completa en la compra y monitorización de campañas, incluyendo Programmatic TV y Digital Billboards. Trabajamos con clientes en múltiples mercados, incluyendo EE. UU., Canadá, México, Brasil, Argentina, Colombia, Perú, Ecuador y Chile. Si te interesa el cruce entre datos, multimedia y publicidad digital, Decidata ofrece un entorno innovador y en constante evolución.

Descripción del puesto En el puesto de Digital Sales en Decidata, te encargarás de identificar oportunidades de negocio, gestionar el ciclo completo de venta y mantener relaciones sólidas con clientes actuales y potenciales. En tu día a día, prospectarás cuentas, harás demostraciones de la solución, prepararás propuestas comerciales y darás seguimiento a leads cualificados junto con los equipos de marketing y producto. También tendrás que analizar resultados de campañas, detectar oportunidades de upselling y aportar feedback para mejorar la oferta de servicios. Este es un puesto de tiempo completo y se desempeña en modalidad remota, coordinándote con equipos internacionales en distintos husos horarios.

title: RSNA Expert - Marketing Specialist
description:
About the job

Position Summary:

We are seeking an experienced Overseas Exhibition Project Manager to oversee the comprehensive management of the entire process for international exhibitions, including the Radiological Society of North America (RSNA) annual meeting. This role will lead exhibition planning, execution, vendor coordination, and client relationship management to ensure optimal brand presentation and participation outcomes at international events. Additionally, this position will serve as a key liaison for the company's digital accounts, collaborating closely with the marketing team to effectively convert offline exhibition traffic into online digital assets.




Position Responsibilities:

1. Exhibition Project Management (RSNA & Overseas Exhibitions)

· Manage the full lifecycle of planning and execution for the RSNA annual meeting and other overseas exhibitions, including booth reservation, exhibit material shipping, construction plan coordination, and on-site services.

· Maintain efficient communication with venues, external suppliers (construction, logistics, service providers) to ensure the smooth execution of all exhibition phases.

· Develop and manage project timelines and quality standards, ensuring critical milestones are met on schedule.

· Oversee on-site operations during exhibitions, including construction supervision, activity execution, and emergency handling.

· Possess thorough knowledge of RSNA rules and regulations to ensure exhibition behavior complies with organizer requirements and mitigates compliance risks.

2. Supplier and Budget Management

· Participate in the tendering, evaluation, and selection process for service suppliers (construction, logistics, PR, etc.), managing the Request for Proposals (RFP) process.

· Prepare and monitor exhibition project budgets, including booth fees, construction costs, shipping, travel expenses, etc., ensuring expenditures stay within budget and identifying cost-saving opportunities.

· Responsible for reviewing, archiving, and ensuring compliance for all exhibition-related contracts.

3. Client Relations and Sales Support

· Maintain efficient communication with exhibitors, providing professional services and on-site support.

· Nurture existing client relationships, identify potential client needs, and assist the sales team with registration and administrative processes.

· Collaborate with the digital marketing team to ensure sales leads generated during exhibitions are effectively entered into the CRM system (e.g., Salesforce) for data integration.

4. Cross-Cultural Communication & International Coordination

· Navigate cross-regional and cross-cultural work environments, building strong collaborative relationships with overseas organizers, service providers, and partners.

· Adapt to overseas travel requirements, leading teams to participate in RSNA and other international exhibitions, providing on-site support and problem-solving.

· Coordinate with internal departments (Marketing, Sales, Product, etc.) to ensure exhibition objectives align with the company's overall strategy.

5. Documentation and Reporting

· Organize, archive, and manage exhibition-related documents, including participation contracts, service agreements, and exhibition materials.

· Regularly prepare comprehensive post-exhibition summary reports, including effectiveness evaluation, ROI analysis, and recommendations for improvement

· Monitor industry trends and exhibition developments, providing data-driven insights and optimization suggestions for the company's exhibition strategy.




Required Skills & Education:

1. Education Background

· Bachelor's degree or above, preferably in International Business, Exhibition Management, Marketing, or a related field.

2. Work Experience

· Minimum 3 years of project management experience specifically with overseas exhibitions, having successfully led the preparation and execution of multiple international events.

· Experience with RSNA or other major medical/professional international exhibitions is highly preferred.

· Experience in domestic and international exhibition organization is preferred.

3. Professional Skills

· Proficient in the full cycle of exhibition project management, including planning, execution, and evaluation.

· Familiar with international trade practices and characteristics of overseas markets, possessing strong negotiation skills.

· Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite and project management tools (e.g., MS Project, Asana).

· Familiarity with CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce) is a plus.

· Experience in financial modeling and budget management is an advantage.

4. Language Ability

· Fluent in English with excellent listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills, capable of using English as a working language for business communication with international partners.

· Overseas work or study background is highly preferred.

5. Core Competencies

· Excellent communication, coordination, and execution skills, capable of building and maintaining strong collaborative relationships across departments and international teams.

· Strong project and time management skills, able to handle multiple tasks and emergencies simultaneously.

· High sense of responsibility and strong ability to work under pressure, adaptable to overseas travel and the high-intensity pace of exhibition work.

· Strong learning agility and market insight, capable of quickly grasping industry dynamics.

· Customer-oriented mindset with attention to detail and a pursuit of excellence in the industry’s exhibition experience.




Carestream is an Equal Opportunity Employer

Carestream is an equal opportunity organization. We recruit, employ, train, compensate, and promote withoutregard to race, religion, creed, color, national origin, age, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status,disability, veteran status, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state or local law.

Applying for a job with Carestream

All applicants must complete the on-line application process.

title: Growth Marketer
skills: Nurture and conversion. Build and optimize nurture, re-engagement, and sales-funnel catalyst campaigns in GoHighLevel that move new leads through the funnel and into sales conversations.
Lead-to-sales handoff. Design the handoff so reps get context, not just a name. The goal is qualified leads that are ready to engage, not raw volume.
Paid search. Improve performance from Google Ads, from targeting and copy to landing pages and conversion tracking. Kill what’s not working, double down on what is.
Social creative. Manage the generation and optimization of creative for social ads, often AI-assisted. Test fast, read the data, cut losers quickly.
Reporting. Work with Operations to build improved reporting across all leads and the full marketing funnel, from first-touch attribution through to closed revenue.
The number. Own a monthly qualified-lead and pipeline target. You’ll have visibility into your performance and the autonomy to act on it.
Creative. You come up with the hook, the angle, and the test, not just the spreadsheet.
AI-native. You use AI well beyond chat. You’ve built loops, agents, or automations to do your own job faster, and you can show us what you’ve made.
A systems thinker. Your instinct is to build a repeatable system, not to grind through the same manual task twice.
Metrics-driven. You’re comfortable owning a number and being measured against it. You speak in CPL, CAC, conversion rate, and ROAS.
Excited by speed. You want to work at a fast-growing company where you ship, learn, and iterate, not wait on a quarterly planning cycle.
Target-based Experimenter: testing > assuming. You build and measure, adjust, repeat, and then scale. And know when to double down based on data + your experience.
Your answer to every lead-gen problem is “spend more on ads.” Paid is one channel, not the strategy.
You avoid committing to a number, or treat activity as the same thing as results.
description:
About the job

Growth Marketer

Seneca Cost Segregation · Remote · Salary: $2000-$3000/month + Target based bounus




About Seneca

We help real estate investors keep more of their money. Cost segregation is a tax strategy that accelerates depreciation and puts cash back in our clients’ hands, often six figures in Year 1. We’ve performed over 10,200 studies and analyzed more than $1B in cost basis, and we’ve never lost an IRS audit.

We’re a lean, fast-growing team scaling toward $5–10M. That means real ownership, real budget, and real impact from day one. No layers, no politics, no waiting for permission.




The Role

We’re hiring a Growth Marketer to own the full marketing funnel, from first touch to qualified pipeline. You’ll build the nurture and conversion systems that turn leads into sales conversations, sharpen our paid channels, and create the reporting that tells us exactly what’s working.




This is a hands-on builder role, not a team-management role (yet). You’ll do the work, with the leverage to grow as we scale. If you think in systems instead of manual effort, use AI to multiply your own output, and want to see your work move the revenue number, this is for you.




What You’ll Own

Nurture and conversion. Build and optimize nurture, re-engagement, and sales-funnel catalyst campaigns in GoHighLevel that move new leads through the funnel and into sales conversations.
Lead-to-sales handoff. Design the handoff so reps get context, not just a name. The goal is qualified leads that are ready to engage, not raw volume.
Paid search. Improve performance from Google Ads, from targeting and copy to landing pages and conversion tracking. Kill what’s not working, double down on what is.
Social creative. Manage the generation and optimization of creative for social ads, often AI-assisted. Test fast, read the data, cut losers quickly.
Reporting. Work with Operations to build improved reporting across all leads and the full marketing funnel, from first-touch attribution through to closed revenue.
The number. Own a monthly qualified-lead and pipeline target. You’ll have visibility into your performance and the autonomy to act on it.




Who You Are

Creative. You come up with the hook, the angle, and the test, not just the spreadsheet.
AI-native. You use AI well beyond chat. You’ve built loops, agents, or automations to do your own job faster, and you can show us what you’ve made.
A systems thinker. Your instinct is to build a repeatable system, not to grind through the same manual task twice.
Metrics-driven. You’re comfortable owning a number and being measured against it. You speak in CPL, CAC, conversion rate, and ROAS.
Excited by speed. You want to work at a fast-growing company where you ship, learn, and iterate, not wait on a quarterly planning cycle.
Target-based Experimenter: testing > assuming. You build and measure, adjust, repeat, and then scale. And know when to double down based on data + your experience. 

 

This Probably Isn’t a Fit If…

Your answer to every lead-gen problem is “spend more on ads.” Paid is one channel, not the strategy.
You avoid committing to a number, or treat activity as the same thing as results.




Our Stack

GoHighLevel (CRM and automation), Google Ads, Meta Ads, GA4 and Google Search Console, GTM, WordPress site, Claude AI and some Codex. You don’t need to be an expert in all of these on day one, but you should be the kind of person who gets fluent in a new tool fast and self-starts to hit your targets.




How to Apply

Send us a short note on a growth system you built and the result it drove. Include one example of a loop, agent, or automation you made to speed up your own work. We read every application that shows us something real.

title: Head of Retention Marketing
skills: Constant reinforcement of value: Work with Product Marketing on the messaging and own the program design that consistently reminds customers why they chose Superside.
Renewal behavior: Build the touchpoints and content that drive account usage and create the psychological and commercial conditions for renewal.
Churn mitigation: Work with data to identify customers at risk and activate account teams and automated programs to re-engage them before the renewal conversation.

Customer Advocacy Board: Partner with ABM to operationalize a Superside CAB. And PMM and TPD to drive customer reviews and help identify opportunities for customers to participate in marketing activities.
Subscription utilization programs: Build the systems that move customers from passive subscribers to active users — driving toward full utilization of their plan each month.
New feature education: Every major product release that Product Marketing launches— needs a customer-facing campaign that explains the what, why, and how.
Usage-triggered journeys: Partner with the lifecycle team to design automated journeys triggered by utilization signals.

AM & GAD toolkits: Build the playbooks and asset libraries that give Account Managers and Group Account Directors what they need to proactively manage customer health - email templates, outreach guides, objection handling - without requiring a custom campaign for every account.
Expansion enablement: Create the materials that help AMs and GADs articulate and sell new Superside offerings, cross-sell services, and grow accounts.

Subscription or retention marketing ownership: You have owned renewal, churn reduction, or subscription growth as a primary mandate - not as a byproduct of a broader marketing role. This was your job.

Utilization or product usage as a core KPI: In at least one role, you were accountable for driving customers to actively use a product or service. You built programs around that metric. You know what moves it and what does not.

Programmatic lifecycle infrastructure: You have built or substantially redesigned a customer lifecycle program — segmentation logic, trigger design, journey mapping, A/B frameworks — not just executed campaigns someone else designed.

Account team or CSM enablement: You have built toolkits, playbooks, and resources for customer-facing commercial teams - and you know the difference between an asset they will actually use and one that sits in a folder.

Data-driven cohort thinking: You do not wait for a report to tell you something is wrong. You know how to segment an account base by health, identify risk patterns, and design interventions for specific cohorts.
A builder, not a maintainer: This function does not have the infrastructure yet. You are energized by that, not intimidated. You have built from near-zero before.
Commercially minded: You think in terms of revenue impact. You know that renewal rate and utilization are lagging indicators of the work you do upstream, and you build toward those numbers.
A relationship investor: Cross functional leaders who work with customers daily are your distribution channel and your intelligence network.
Operationally rigorous: You produce documentation, playbooks, and toolkits that outlast you. You build systems that account teams can run, not campaigns that require you to run them.
A senior IC ready to become a people leader: You will not manage a team on day one. In 12–24 months you will build one. You already think and operate at that level.
description:
About the job

Superside is hiring a Head of Retention Marketing reporting to the Sr. Director of ABM and Field Marketing. The mandate is straightforward: keep customers, grow accounts, and make sure every customer understands the value they're getting from Superside before they're ever asked to renew. That means owning the retention program design that reinforces value at every stage of the customer journey — from onboarding through adoption through renewal. It means building utilization programs and equipping account teams with the playbooks, toolkits, and intelligence they need to proactively manage account health without waiting for a crisis. And it means working with data to spot churn risk early and activate the right interventions before the conversation gets hard.




What you’ll own




Retention Programs

Constant reinforcement of value: Work with Product Marketing on the messaging and own the program design that consistently reminds customers why they chose Superside.
Renewal behavior: Build the touchpoints and content that drive account usage and create the psychological and commercial conditions for renewal.
Churn mitigation: Work with data to identify customers at risk and activate account teams and automated programs to re-engage them before the renewal conversation.
Customer Advocacy Board: Partner with ABM to operationalize a Superside CAB. And PMM and TPD to drive customer reviews and help identify opportunities for customers to participate in marketing activities.




Adoption & Utilization

Subscription utilization programs: Build the systems that move customers from passive subscribers to active users — driving toward full utilization of their plan each month.
New feature education: Every major product release that Product Marketing launches— needs a customer-facing campaign that explains the what, why, and how.
Usage-triggered journeys: Partner with the lifecycle team to design automated journeys triggered by utilization signals.




Account Team Enablement

AM & GAD toolkits: Build the playbooks and asset libraries that give Account Managers and Group Account Directors what they need to proactively manage customer health - email templates, outreach guides, objection handling - without requiring a custom campaign for every account.
Expansion enablement: Create the materials that help AMs and GADs articulate and sell new Superside offerings, cross-sell services, and grow accounts.







Must-Have Experience




Subscription or retention marketing ownership: You have owned renewal, churn reduction, or subscription growth as a primary mandate - not as a byproduct of a broader marketing role. This was your job.
Utilization or product usage as a core KPI: In at least one role, you were accountable for driving customers to actively use a product or service. You built programs around that metric. You know what moves it and what does not.
Programmatic lifecycle infrastructure: You have built or substantially redesigned a customer lifecycle program — segmentation logic, trigger design, journey mapping, A/B frameworks — not just executed campaigns someone else designed.
Account team or CSM enablement: You have built toolkits, playbooks, and resources for customer-facing commercial teams - and you know the difference between an asset they will actually use and one that sits in a folder.
Data-driven cohort thinking: You do not wait for a report to tell you something is wrong. You know how to segment an account base by health, identify risk patterns, and design interventions for specific cohorts.







Who you are




A builder, not a maintainer: This function does not have the infrastructure yet. You are energized by that, not intimidated. You have built from near-zero before.
Commercially minded: You think in terms of revenue impact. You know that renewal rate and utilization are lagging indicators of the work you do upstream, and you build toward those numbers.
A relationship investor: Cross functional leaders who work with customers daily are your distribution channel and your intelligence network.
Operationally rigorous: You produce documentation, playbooks, and toolkits that outlast you. You build systems that account teams can run, not campaigns that require you to run them.
A senior IC ready to become a people leader: You will not manage a team on day one. In 12–24 months you will build one. You already think and operate at that level.

title: Customer Service Wholesale
location: Cuajimalpa de Morelos, Mexico City, Mexico (Hybrid)
description:
About the job

¡Te invitamos a unirte a un equipo dinámico!




Somos una marca líder en el mundo deportivo que inspira a atletas y entusiastas a alcanzar su máximo potencial. Si compartes nuestra pasión por la excelencia, la innovación y el deseo de superar límites, esta es tu oportunidad para unirte a un equipo dinámico y lleno de energía.

 

Estamos buscando “CUSTOMER SERVICE JUNIOR PARA CANAL DE VENTA WHOLESALE”




 El objetivo principal del rol es garantizar una atención clara, oportuna y eficiente, fungiendo como el principal punto de contacto para resolver dudas, incidencias y requerimientos de las cuentas actuales de Wholesale, Sporting Goods y Tactical, así como de futuras cuentas que puedan integrarse al negocio.




Experiencia: Un año de experiencia en atención a clientes de venta mayoreo. Seguimiento a pedidos y devoluciones. Carga de órdenes de compra en portales, verificación de stock, coordinación de entregas, aclaración de facturas, solución a incidencias de retrasos. 




Conocimientos:

· Manejo de Excel intermedio-avanzado.

· Manejo de ERP (SAP, Oracle o similar).

· Conocimiento de procesos logísticos, inventarios y facturación.

· Manejo de portales de clientes y carga de órdenes de compra.

· Inglés avanzado.

 

Actividades:

Atención y servicio al cliente

· Dar seguimiento a pedidos, devoluciones, aclaraciones de facturación y solicitudes especiales.

· Mantener comunicación constante con clientes, tiendas y plataformas digitales, brindando información clara y actualizada.

· Asegurar una experiencia de servicio alineada con los estándares de la marca.

· Fungir como punto de contacto principal para resolver dudas e incidencias de las cuentas asignadas.

2.Gestión y seguimiento operativo

· Monitorear el flujo completo del pedido desde su colocación hasta la entrega.

· Resolver de manera proactiva incidencias relacionadas con retrasos logísticos, diferencias de inventario, cambios de producto y problemas de entrega.

· Dar seguimiento a órdenes pendientes y asegurar su cierre oportuno.

· Registrar en sistema toda la información relevante relacionada con pedidos, comunicaciones y soluciones implementadas.

· Cargar órdenes de compra en portales y sistemas internos.

· Realizar revisiones de inventario disponible y apoyar en la asignación de producto.

3.Coordinación interna

· Trabajar en conjunto con las áreas de Logística, Finanzas, Marketing, Ventas y Supply Chain para asegurar respuestas ágiles y efectivas.

· Escalar oportunamente riesgos o situaciones críticas que puedan impactar el nivel de servicio.

· Dar seguimiento a procesos de crédito, facturación y documentación requerida por los clientes.

4.Soporte comercial

· Apoyar al equipo comercial en actividades administrativas y operativas.

· Dar seguimiento a lanzamientos, promociones y proyectos especiales con clientes.

· Elaborar y actualizar reportes de ventas, inventarios y estatus de pedidos.

· Identificar oportunidades de mejora en procesos y proponer acciones para incrementar la eficiencia operativa.

· Contribuir al cumplimiento de los niveles de servicio (SLA) y los objetivos comerciales del canal.

 

Ofrecemos: 

Sueldo competitivo

Prestaciones Superiores y de ley

Fondo de ahorro 

Vales de despensa 

Vales de alimentos 

Seguro de Vida

Gastos médicos mayores 

Contratación directa por la empresa

 

Zona de trabajo: Santa Fe, modalidad de trabajo híbrido. 

Horario: de lunes a viernes de 07:00 a 16:00 o de 08:00 a 17:00 horas




¡Queremos conocerte!

title: Analyst, Strategy & Planning
skills: At TransUnion de México - Buró de Crédito, we foster a collaborative, dynamic, and innovative environment where we continuously explore new technologies and tools to remain agile. This enables our team to strengthen their skills, develop new capabilities, and reach their full potential.

Supports the LATAM regional business planning process, ongoing planning activities and LATAM strategy execution. Collaborate with the leadership team to facilitate and support planning sessions and business strategy execution. Plans and develop long-term goals and short-term objectives. Supports execution of regional business plans, key initiatives, M&A and partnership processes. Track competition and identifies threats or opportunities. Report progress and analyze issues that affect productivity, profitability, and growth for the region.
Come be a part of our team! You’ll work with great people, pioneering products and cutting-edge technology.
Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering, Business or other related discipline.
Ideal candidate should have 1-2yrs of experience in a top-tier Consulting organization, Investment Banking or Private Equity.
Must have business planning experience along with strong facilitation skills.
Must be proficient with Excel, PowerPoint, and the Office Suite of applications. Think-Cell knowledge is a plus.
Must be 100% fluent in English and Spanish.
Ability to work independently or as part of a dynamic team in a rapidly changing environment.
Good project management capabilities and high attention to detail; comfortable working under deadlines and producing key deliverables.
Sound business judgment and excellent problem solving and influencing skills.
Proficient financial, analytical, organizational, and people skills.
Proven ability to quickly grasp industry and business-wide concepts to assess implications to drive actions and to capitalize on opportunities.
Team worker; excellent communication and presentation skills with experience in providing crisp, insightful information to executive teams. Ability to travel at least 10% of time.
Collaborate with LATAM region to create and maintain business plans that have clear and measurable targets. Develop senior-level presentations for short-term and long-range plans.
Support strategic planning process considering regional objectives.
Support the execution of the regional business plan and track and report current year progress.
Support all regional activity associated with new markets and new LOB development.
Develop financial projections to estimate market size, revenue projections, P&L projections, and investment requirements for new projects
Support M&A and partnership processes across the region.
Conduct industry research to understand market trends and competitive landscape and translate findings into presentations and memos for the leadership team
Execute ad hoc strategic analysis for leadership team. Gather competitive intelligence on an ongoing basis, monitor strategy, and predict competitive response for key competitors.
Build content to support a variety of Strategy and M&A process components and other high-visibility, high-stakes materials involving C-suite internal stakeholders.
Support leadership team in key initiatives across the organization: prepare presentations for leadership team, research, financial analysis, among others.
Develop ongoing reports: Macro, Board of Directors, results.
Support project management of different initiatives across the region.
Perform other related duties as assigned.

description:
About the job

★ PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR CV IN ENGLISH ★

Online Engagement Specialist

Location: Remote — LATAM

Employment Type: Full-Time (Independent Contractor)

Industry: Travel & Hospitality

Compensation: $1,720 – $1,820 USD/month

Schedule: 40 hours/week | Eastern Time (EDT)

Working Hours


Monday – Wednesday: 4:00 PM – 11:00 PM (or 12:00 AM EDT) 
Saturday – Sunday: 12:00 PM – 8:00 PM (or 9:00 PM EDT) 


About The Role

We are seeking a highly engaging, customer-focused, and proactive Online Engagement Specialist to become the first point of contact for visitors exploring one of the leading destination wedding travel platforms in North America.

This role combines real-time customer interaction with live chat technology to engage prospective clients, answer questions, qualify leads, and guide visitors toward scheduling a consultation with a Destination Wedding Specialist.

More than simply responding to chats, you'll play a critical role in generating qualified leads, creating exceptional customer experiences, and helping couples take the first step toward planning one of the most memorable events of their lives.

The ideal candidate is an outstanding communicator with exceptional written English, thrives in fast-paced environments, enjoys multitasking, and knows how to build meaningful customer interactions that convert interest into scheduled consultations.

This is a new position within the organization, offering the opportunity to help shape best practices, contribute ideas, and make a meaningful impact as the team continues to grow.

What You'll Do

Live Chat & Customer Engagement


Serve as the first point of contact for website visitors through live chat. 
Engage prospective clients in real time using a friendly and consultative approach. 
Respond to customer questions about destination weddings, travel services, and the planning process. 
Personalize conversations to deliver an exceptional customer experience. 
Build rapport with visitors and encourage continued engagement. 


Lead Qualification & Appointment Scheduling


Engage with prospective clients to understand their travel and wedding planning needs. 
Collect and update customer information accurately within the CRM. 
Qualify leads and connect them with the appropriate Destination Wedding Specialist. 
Schedule consultations and ensure clients receive all necessary follow-up communications. 
Support a seamless transition from initial inquiry to specialist consultation. 


Customer Experience & Performance


Manage multiple live chat conversations simultaneously while maintaining exceptional response times and service quality. 
Support engagement and lead conversion through personalized interactions. 
Contribute to improving chat messaging, workflows, and customer experience. 
Collaborate with internal teams to continuously optimize performance and conversion rates. 


Collaboration & Continuous Improvement


Work closely with Marketing and Sales teams to improve customer engagement strategies. 
Share customer insights and feedback to enhance chat performance. 
Help identify opportunities to improve operational efficiency and lead generation. 


Required Qualifications

What We're Looking For


C1 English proficiency or higher (written and spoken) is REQUIRED. 
Outstanding written communication skills with excellent grammar and spelling. 
Minimum 2 years of experience in:
Live Chat Support 
Customer Support 
Customer Success 
Online Sales Support 
Lead Qualification 
Experience interacting with customers through live chat, messaging platforms, or CRM systems. 
Typing speed of 60+ WPM. 
Ability to confidently manage multiple customer conversations simultaneously in a fast-paced environment. 
Tech-savvy with the ability to quickly learn new platforms and digital tools. 
Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail. 
Ability to work independently in a fully remote environment. 


Preferred Qualifications


Experience within:
Travel 
Hospitality 
Tourism 
Wedding Industry 
Experience using live chat software and CRM platforms. 
Experience scheduling appointments for sales teams. 
Previous experience supporting U.S.-based customers. 


What Success Looks Like

Success in this role will be measured by your ability to:


Increase qualified lead generation. 
Improve consultation booking rates. 
Deliver an exceptional customer experience through live chat. 
Successfully manage multiple concurrent conversations while maintaining quality and responsiveness. 
Increase visitor engagement and conversion. 
Help drive the growth of confirmed destination wedding events. 


Why Join Us?


Competitive compensation in USD. 
10 paid days off annually (prorated during the first year). 
Fully remote contractor opportunity. 
Join a collaborative and growing team. 
Opportunity to make a direct impact in a newly created position. 
Long-term career growth within the travel and hospitality industry. 
Dynamic, technology-driven environment focused on delivering exceptional customer experiences. 


Important Notes

Applications may not be considered if candidates:


Do not meet the C1 English requirement. 
Do not demonstrate excellent written English skills. 
Have typing speeds below 60 WPM. 
Lack experience managing multiple live customer conversations simultaneously. 
Are unavailable to work the required Eastern Time (EDT) schedule. 


This is a remote/telecommute position.

description:
About the job

Ready to lead, disrupt and reinvent the sleep industry?




We are Emma – The Sleep Company. Founded in 2015, we’ve become the world’s largest D2C sleep brand, available in over 35 countries with more than 25 stores across Europe. Recommended by leading consumer associations in EMEA, APAC, and the Americas, we develop the best sleep comfort products that empower our customers to awaken their best every day. 

 

At Emma, we are a community of smart, driven people, united by a strong culture of collaboration, teamwork, and knowledge sharing. We think big, take ownership, and are empowered to drive meaningful impact. Through hands-on experience, mentorship, and continuous learning, we drive our own growth and learning. With Emmies from over 60 nationalities and offices across multiple regions, our diverse perspectives enrich our workplace culture. Ready to shape the future of sleep with us? Let’s make it happen!




What you will do: 



Develop and implement marketing strategies: Lead the creation and optimization of marketing strategies in marketplaces to enhance brand awareness and drive performance in the marketplace


Performance management: Continuously monitor and manage the performance of various marketing methods and KPIs, adapting strategies as necessary to meet business goals


Ad optimization: Oversee the performance and optimization of m


arketplace ads to ensure they effectively reach targeted audiences and yield high return on investment


Content optimization: Lead efforts in optimizing listings with updated titles, engaging descriptions, A+ content, and visually appealing images to attract and retain customers


Collaborative approach: Work cross-functionally with other teams to develop cohesive and impactful marketing campaigns, both internally in Emma and with the marketplaces POCs


Self-starter mentality: Thrive in a high-paced environment with a willingness to learn and adapt, taking complete ownership of your work stream without expecting micromanagement. 


Analytics: Utilize data and analytics to inform decision-making and strategic optimization


Flat organizational structure: Comfortable working in a non-hierarchical organization where roles are high-impact and entail a substantial degree of ownership


Budgment Management and Control: Manage, and optimize the internal budget and spend for the marketplaces channels, aligning with the counterparts for each marketplace. 








Who we are looking for:


Nice to have experience: Working with Amazon or Mercado Libre Ads or a background in selling in marketplaces


Detail-oriented: A meticulous attention to detail in developing and implementing marketing strategies and optimizing content


Innovative thinker: A proactive approach to problem-solving, with the ability to think outside the box and develop innovative solutions to complex problems


Growth-oriented: A desire to learn exponentially, constantly seeking opportunities for personal and professional development








What we offer:


A combination of personal and company growth to accelerate your career and help you reach your goals


The chance to work on exciting and challenging projects either independently or as part of a dedicated, international team


Wellbeing activities


Hybrid home office policy


Flexible schedules


Pet-friendly office


Monthly salary + pantry & restaurant vouchers


Benefits higher than the law











Become an Emmie




Emma is transforming the world of sleep - and we want the highest-performing people to help us pull it off. We want you. But only if you're willing to go all in. Only if you're willing to question, disrupt, innovate, and create from the ground up. 




We proudly celebrate diversity. We are an equal-opportunity employer committed to promoting inclusion in our workplace. We consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, ethnic origin, religion or belief, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, or age.




Our aim is to get back to you in a couple of days, however, we are currently receiving a large number of applications and this might lead to a delay in the process. We will get back to you as soon as possible! 




Upon your consent, we may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as get the interviews transcript and summarised by AI. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgement. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please reach out to privacy@emma-sleep.com

Job Title: Finance and Sales Positions




About the Company:

Our client is a financial services company that provides lending solutions, with a focus on real estate financing.




Job Description:

Our client is looking to hire candidates for finance and sales roles. The finance role requires a general understanding of finance, capital stock, and financial models, while the sales role involves cold calling leads generated from marketing campaigns.




Responsibilities of the role:

• Conduct cold calls to leads from marketing campaigns.

• Understand and apply financial concepts related to lending services.




Required Experience and Qualifications:

• Experience in finance or sales roles.

• Understanding of real estate financing is a plus.




Preferred Skills:

• Call center experience, particularly in cold calling.




Personality:

• Strong communication skills and a proactive approach to problem-solving.




Software & Tools:

• Familiarity with CRM software and financial modeling tools.




Schedule:

• Candidates will work US hours.




Salary and Benefits:

• Payment in USD or Local Currency according to candidate's preference.

• Full-time remote opportunity.

title: Graphic Designer
description:
About the job

About Zenit Corp

Zenit Corp is a rapidly growing ecommerce group that owns and operates multiple direct-to-consumer brands across different international markets.

Our focus is on building category-leading brands with strong fundamentals, scalable systems, and long-term growth potential. We are not building a single ecommerce business, but a portfolio of brands designed to scale aggressively over the coming years.

We are looking for creative individuals who want more than just a job. We want people who are eager to create, execute, improve, and grow alongside the company.

The Graphic Designer will be responsible for producing high-quality visual assets that strengthen our brands, support marketing initiatives, enhance communication, and help drive growth across multiple ecommerce channels.

This role is ideal for someone creative, detail-oriented, organized, and capable of translating brand direction, marketing goals, and AI-generated concepts into strong and polished visual content.

Role

The Graphic Designer is responsible for developing visual content across social media, websites, advertising campaigns, ecommerce assets, presentations, banners, and promotional materials.

This role requires strong design skills, excellent visual judgment, an understanding of branding, experience working with AI-generated imagery, and the ability to adapt creative assets across multiple formats and platforms.

The designer will collaborate closely with marketing, content, creative, and leadership teams to ensure all visual materials are consistent, engaging, brand-aligned, and execution-ready.

Graphic Designer Responsibilities❖ Graphic Design

➢ Design visual assets for social media, websites, landing pages, advertising campaigns, and ecommerce platforms.

 ➢ Create banners, presentations, promotional materials, and brand communication assets.

 ➢ Develop visually appealing and brand-consistent designs.

 ➢ Translate creative direction and campaign requirements into production-ready visuals.

❖ AI Image Generation

➢ Use AI-based image generation tools to create, enhance, and adapt visuals for campaigns, content, and ecommerce assets.

 ➢ Understand basic AI workflows such as prompting, refinement, upscaling, background creation, and variations.

 ➢ Combine AI-generated visuals with traditional design tools to produce high-quality final outputs.

 ➢ Identify opportunities to leverage AI to speed up production and improve creative output.

❖ Brand & Visual Consistency

➢ Maintain visual consistency across all brands and marketing channels.

 ➢ Apply branding principles such as typography, color systems, layout, composition, and visual hierarchy.

 ➢ Organize design files, templates, and visual resources in a structured way.

 ➢ Contribute to improving scalable design systems for multi-brand use.

❖ Creative Collaboration

➢ Work closely with the creative strategist and marketing team.

 ➢ Translate campaign objectives into effective visual executions.

 ➢ Contribute ideas for visual concepts, campaigns, and brand development.

❖ Content Optimization

➢ Adapt designs for different platforms, formats, and use cases.

 ➢ Create multiple variations of assets for testing and performance optimization.

 ➢ Refine designs based on feedback, performance data, and creative direction.

Role Objectives❖ Creative Quality & Impact

➢ Produce high-quality visual content aligned with brand identity.

 ➢ Support marketing initiatives with strong and effective creative assets.

 ➢ Improve engagement through visually compelling design.

 ➢ Strengthen consistency across all brand touchpoints.

 ➢ Improve production speed and efficiency through AI tools and optimized workflows.

Requirements❖ Experience & Technical Skills

➢ Minimum of 2 years of experience in graphic design.

 ➢ Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite or similar design tools.

 ➢ Basic experience with AI image generation tools and workflows (mandatory).

 ➢ Strong portfolio demonstrating previous design work.

 ➢ Solid understanding of layout, typography, composition, branding, and digital design principles.

 ➢ Ability to create assets for ads, social media, websites, presentations, and campaigns.

❖ Communication & Work Style

➢ B2 English level or higher.

 ➢ Strong communication skills in English and Spanish.

 ➢ High attention to detail and strong organizational habits.

 ➢ Ability to work independently as well as in a team.

 ➢ Open to feedback and able to iterate quickly.

 ➢ Strong ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.

What We Offer❖ Compensation & Growth

➢ Salary up to USD 10,000 per year.

 ➢ High-performance, fast-paced work environment.

 ➢ Direct impact on a rapidly scaling ecommerce group.

 ➢ Long-term collaboration opportunities.

 ➢ Exposure to multiple ecommerce brands and creative challenges.

 ➢ Experience working with AI-powered design workflows and modern creative tools.

Required Equipment❖ Work Setup

➢ High-performance computer.

 ➢ Stable and fast internet connection.

 ➢ Distraction-free workspace.

Candidate Profile❖ Ideal Candidate

➢ Creative, detail-oriented, structured, and reliable.

 ➢ Strong visual judgment and understanding of brand consistency.

 ➢ Comfortable using AI tools to improve speed and quality of design output.

 ➢ Able to manage multiple projects, deadlines, and feedback cycles.

 ➢ Ownership mindset with consistent delivery of high-quality work.

 ➢ Excited to work in a fast-paced environment where design directly impacts business growth.

title: Community Manager Trabajo Remoto
description:
About the job

Estamos en búsqueda de un(a) Community Manager de Recursos Humanos y Administración para apoyar en la gestión de la comunicación digital y el fortalecimiento de la imagen organizacional. La persona seleccionada participará en la creación y programación de contenido para redes sociales, difusión de vacantes, apoyo en campañas de atracción de talento, seguimiento de mensajes y consultas, actualización de contenido institucional y elaboración de reportes básicos sobre el desempeño de las publicaciones. Esta oportunidad es ideal para quienes desean adquirir experiencia en comunicación organizacional, reclutamiento digital, marca empleadora y administración de comunidades en línea. La modalidad de trabajo puede ser remota o híbrida; para quienes opten por el esquema híbrido, las actividades presenciales se realizan en la Zona Centro de la Ciudad de México, cerca de Paseo de la Reforma. Buscamos personas creativas, organizadas y con interés en el desarrollo del talento humano.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn.
Collaborative mindset and ability to work in diverse teams.
Passion for marketing, healthcare, skincare, and consumer behavior.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.

description:
About the job

About Puig




At Puig, we are a family-owned company with a long-term commitment to creativity, innovation, and people development. Through our Graduate Program, we are looking for exceptional young talent ready to begin their professional journey in a dynamic, international, and purpose-driven environment.




This is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career, gain exposure to a leading global organization, and contribute to the growth of prestigious dermatological brands that improve people's quality of life through science-backed skincare solutions.




Your Mission




As a Marketing Graduate (Entry Level) within our Derma Division, you will support the Marketing team in the development and execution of brand strategies, product launches, and commercial initiatives while gaining hands-on experience across multiple areas of the business.




You will work closely with cross-functional teams including Medical Affairs, Sales, Trade Marketing, Digital, and Finance, contributing to projects that have a direct impact on business performance and patient outcomes.




What You'll Do




• Support the planning, execution, and optimization of digital marketing campaigns across social media platforms, with a strong focus on TikTok and emerging digital channels.

• Assist in the creation, coordination, and publishing of engaging content aligned with brand strategies, consumer trends, and business objectives.

• Collaborate closely with the Digital & Marketing team to manage community engagement, monitor social conversations, and identify opportunities to increase brand relevance and reach.

• Support influencer and content creator initiatives, including campaign coordination, performance tracking, and reporting.

• Monitor digital KPIs and campaign performance, providing insights and recommendations to improve engagement, visibility, and business impact.

• Contribute to the development and management of a dedicated social media project aimed at healthcare professionals, helping create educational and scientifically relevant content for derma brands.

• Coordinate with Medical Visit and cross-functional teams to ensure content accuracy, compliance, and alignment with brand objectives.

• Assist in the preparation of presentations, reports, and business reviews related to digital performance, marketing initiatives, and key projects.

• Participate in local and global marketing projects, gaining exposure to brand management, consumer engagement, and omnichannel strategies.

• Provide support on ad hoc marketing initiatives, product launches, events, and special projects across the Derma division.




Who You Are

Recent graduate (Bachelor's degree completed within the last 1–2 years) in Marketing, Business Administration, Communication, Pharmaceutical Business, or related fields.
Previous internship, trainee, student leadership, or extracurricular experience is highly valued.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn.
Collaborative mindset and ability to work in diverse teams.
Passion for marketing, healthcare, skincare, and consumer behavior.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.




Requirements

Bachelor's degree completed by the start date.
Intermediate English proficiency (written and spoken).
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel and PowerPoint preferred).
Availability to work full-time.
Authorization to work in Mexico.




What We Offer

Participation in a global Graduate Program designed to accelerate your development.
Exposure to senior leaders and international projects.
Structured learning and development opportunities.
Mentorship and career guidance.
A collaborative, inclusive, and innovative culture.
Opportunity to build a long-term career within Puig.




Our Commitment

At Puig, we believe that diversity, equity, and inclusion drive innovation and creativity. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive, contribute, and grow.

Join us and help shape the future of dermatological skincare through science, innovation, and purpose. ✨

title: Partnerships Associate
description:
About the job

Hiring: Partnerships Associate for Million Dollar Sellers, a top-tier ecommerce community.




You’ll own the full partner lifecycle, close new partners, then make sure every commitment runs cleanly.


ABOUT MDS

An exclusive community of 700+ seven- and eight-figure Amazon and ecommerce founders. $12B+ in combined member sales. Nearly 100 events per year. Backed by industry-leading partners.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

— Identify and close new partners across affiliates, event sponsorships, and community offerings, owning outreach from first contact to signed agreement

— Onboard every new partner completely into the database, app listing, newsletter, offers, and deliverable tracker before anyone has to ask

— Track and execute all partner deliverables across events, campaigns, and affiliate programs so nothing promised slips

— Send the weekly Partners Newsletter every Tuesday and maintain the partner database, contracts, offers, and KPI tracking

— Produce AI-powered Community Pulse summaries surfacing partner mentions and insights from across community channels


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

— Experience in partnerships, sales, or BD, with a track record of closing deals, not just managing them

— Equally comfortable sending a cold outreach email and cleaning up a database

— Operational discipline: you onboard fully, track deliverables proactively, and treat the database as a live system, not an archive

— Familiarity with CRMs and ops tools, Airtable, ClickUp, SignNow, or similar

— Comfort using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) as part of your regular workflow

— Strong written English, you’ll be writing outreach, a weekly newsletter, and partner comms

— Based in LATAM (Mexico, Argentina preferred). Travel required minimum 3x per year

— Available 10AM–3PM EST core hours




COMPENSATION

— USD $2,000–$3,000/month (band reflects balance of sales track record and operational depth)

— Tiered bonus plan tied to annual revenue goals

— Long-term, multi-year engagement

— Direct line to the VP of Revenue




HOW TO APPLY

Apply through our form: https://forms.clickup.com/90161589637/f/2kz0tbc5-516/HN6BGAJ1CDZMH3QDD7




You’ll need: CV, a Loom intro, and 5 minutes.

We respond to every applicant within 48 hours.

title: Content Specialist, Video Production
skills: Bring a strong perspective on what makes video content hook viewers, including scripting techniques, editing and pacing strategies, and visual structure.
Support the development of video content for Study.com and marketing channels, with a strong emphasis on optimizing for YouTube & TikTok engagement and discoverability.
Onboard, manage, and support a group of freelance video creators and video editors to ensure high-quality and efficient video creation
Track production rates, identify project bottlenecks, and resolve blockers to meet weekly, monthly, and quarterly goals
Write, edit, and update standard operating practices to ensure operational efficiency across video projects

Analyze video performance and incorporate insights to iterate on scripting/editing to increase social engagement rate, including formats inspired by successful content outside of traditional educational content (e.g., listicles, explainers, visual essays, "study with me").
Provide ongoing feedback, direction, and performance analysis to support content strategy.
Leverage performance data to identify effective video formats, and iteratively refine our strategy through insight-driven ideation
Use AI and tech tools to streamline production (Claude desktop, Langflow, etc) and improve video quality with video first AI tools (HeyGen, Elevenlabs, etc)

Strong project management and communication skills with the ability to track multiple content production streams, manage freelancer relationships, and collaborate effectively across cross-functional teams.
2–3 years of video production experience with a strong understanding of project management, social media marketing, and the YouTube and TikTok ecosystem.
A portfolio of social media videos produced or YouTube/TikTok channels they have managed or contributed to
Experienced in creating video titles according to SEO best practices, scripting, storyboarding, producing videos, and collaborating with editors.
Data-savvy and comfortable making recommendations based on performance insights to improve video engagement and viewer retention.
Skilled in sourcing and guiding freelance video content creators and editors to produce engaging, platform-optimized content.
Inc. Best Place to Work

Golden Bridge Awards – Gold Winner, Corporate Social Responsibility
San Francisco Business Times & Silicon Valley Business Journal – Best Places to Work
description:
About the job

Our mission is to open the door to the life-changing impact of education. We build market-leading, online college credit and test-preparation solutions that help working adults earn their degrees, advance their careers, and achieve economic mobility. Recognized by Fast Company and GSV 150 as one of the world’s most innovative education companies, we are also at the forefront of AI-first learning, combining artificial intelligence with the latest learning science to improve access and outcomes at scale.




Our product is our impact. Through our Keys to College and Keys to the Classroom initiatives, we have saved students over $700 million in tuition and enabled hundreds of thousands of learners to pursue and succeed in careers across nursing, teaching, counseling, business, and other high-demand fields. We are deeply focused on measurable outcomes and building products that create meaningful, lasting change in people’s lives.




The contractor will be expected to work PST business hours. Additionally, they will need to use their own computer and have reliable access to high-speed internet.




Job Description




Bring a strong perspective on what makes video content hook viewers, including scripting techniques, editing and pacing strategies, and visual structure.
Support the development of video content for Study.com and marketing channels, with a strong emphasis on optimizing for YouTube & TikTok engagement and discoverability.
Onboard, manage, and support a group of freelance video creators and video editors to ensure high-quality and efficient video creation
Track production rates, identify project bottlenecks, and resolve blockers to meet weekly, monthly, and quarterly goals
Write, edit, and update standard operating practices to ensure operational efficiency across video projects
Analyze video performance and incorporate insights to iterate on scripting/editing to increase social engagement rate, including formats inspired by successful content outside of traditional educational content (e.g., listicles, explainers, visual essays, "study with me").
Provide ongoing feedback, direction, and performance analysis to support content strategy.
Leverage performance data to identify effective video formats, and iteratively refine our strategy through insight-driven ideation
Use AI and tech tools to streamline production (Claude desktop, Langflow, etc) and improve video quality with video first AI tools (HeyGen, Elevenlabs, etc)




Requirements




Strong project management and communication skills with the ability to track multiple content production streams, manage freelancer relationships, and collaborate effectively across cross-functional teams.
2–3 years of video production experience with a strong understanding of project management, social media marketing, and the YouTube and TikTok ecosystem.
A portfolio of social media videos produced or YouTube/TikTok channels they have managed or contributed to
Experienced in creating video titles according to SEO best practices, scripting, storyboarding, producing videos, and collaborating with editors.
Data-savvy and comfortable making recommendations based on performance insights to improve video engagement and viewer retention.
Skilled in sourcing and guiding freelance video content creators and editors to produce engaging, platform-optimized content.




At Study.com, we know that strong communities are built on the power of our diversity, and we respect and strive to understand for the diverse identities, race, ethnicities, backgrounds, and perspectives of our team members. Our work and company culture have been consistently recognized, including:

Inc. Best Place to Work
Fortune Great Places to Work – Best Workplaces in the Bay Area
Golden Bridge Awards – Gold Winner, Corporate Social Responsibility
San Francisco Business Times & Silicon Valley Business Journal – Best Places to Work




Study.com provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, veteran or disability status. In addition to federal law requirements.




Study.com complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. Additionally, qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records will be considered for the position in accordance with applicable state and local laws.

title: Analista JR de Análisis de Datos Digital
skills: Analizar conversaciones, tendencias y métricas en redes sociales para generar insights accionables.
Realizar monitoreo digital y búsquedas especializadas (Boolean Search).
Desarrollar informes ejecutivos y dashboards con visualización clara y estratégica de resultados.
Identificar oportunidades, prevenir crisis y contribuir a la toma de decisiones comunicacionales.
Presentar hallazgos e informes ante clientes internos y externos.
Apoyar el desarrollo de líneas narrativas con base en el comportamiento y sentimiento de las audiencias.
Coordinarse con equipos de contenido, pauta y consultoría para alinear la estrategia digital.
Licenciatura concluida en Ciencias Políticas, Comunicación, Periodismo, Relaciones Internacionales, Sociología o afines.
Dominio de búsquedas booleanas y herramientas de Social Listening.
Conocimiento en estadística básica, interpretación de métricas y herramientas de marketing digital.
Manejo de herramientas como Meltwater, Google Analytics, Talkwalker, Sprinklr u otras equivalentes.
Inglés intermedio.
Alta capacidad analítica, pensamiento crítico y gusto por los retos estratégicos.
Manejo indispensable de excel avanzado.
Sueldo mensual: $18,000 a $23,000 MXN netos, según experiencia.
Modalidad híbrida (home office + oficinas cerca del Metro Sevilla, CDMX).
Horario laboral a tiempo completo.
Oportunidades reales de crecimiento profesional.
Ambiente de trabajo dinámico, colaborativo y con propósito.
description:
About the job

Vacante: Analista JR de Análisis de Datos Digital

Modalidad híbrida | Ciudad de México

Inicio estimado: Junio 2026




En CUADRANGULAR, agencia líder en comunicación digital política, buscamos un(a) Analista JR de Análisis de Datos Digital que combine visión analítica, criterio estratégico y pasión por lo público para apoyar en la construcción de campañas digitales de alto impacto.




Este rol es clave para posicionar a candidatos, partidos políticos, marcas comerciales e iniciativas privadas en plataformas digitales, a través del análisis de datos, interpretación de métricas y el desarrollo de líneas de comunicación basadas en resultados.




¿Qué harás en este rol?




Analizar conversaciones, tendencias y métricas en redes sociales para generar insights accionables.
Realizar monitoreo digital y búsquedas especializadas (Boolean Search).
Desarrollar informes ejecutivos y dashboards con visualización clara y estratégica de resultados.
Identificar oportunidades, prevenir crisis y contribuir a la toma de decisiones comunicacionales.
Presentar hallazgos e informes ante clientes internos y externos.
Apoyar el desarrollo de líneas narrativas con base en el comportamiento y sentimiento de las audiencias.
Coordinarse con equipos de contenido, pauta y consultoría para alinear la estrategia digital.




Requisitos indispensables




Licenciatura concluida en Ciencias Políticas, Comunicación, Periodismo, Relaciones Internacionales, Sociología o afines.
Dominio de búsquedas booleanas y herramientas de Social Listening.
Conocimiento en estadística básica, interpretación de métricas y herramientas de marketing digital.
Manejo de herramientas como Meltwater, Google Analytics, Talkwalker, Sprinklr u otras equivalentes.
Inglés intermedio.
Alta capacidad analítica, pensamiento crítico y gusto por los retos estratégicos.
Manejo indispensable de excel avanzado.




Se aplicará una prueba técnica de booleanas para validar conocimientos y habilidades.




Lo que ofrecemos




Sueldo mensual: $18,000 a $23,000 MXN netos, según experiencia.
Modalidad híbrida (home office + oficinas cerca del Metro Sevilla, CDMX).

Oportunidades reales de crecimiento profesional.

#VacanteDigital #AnalistaDeRedes #EscuchaSocial #SocialListening #AnálisisDeDatos #ComunicaciónPolítica #OportunidadLaboral #AgenciaDigital #TalentoCuadra #CUADRANGULAR




En CUADRANGULAR promovemos un entorno laboral inclusivo y respetuoso.
